zh-tw.title: 幹話語錄
zh-tw.description: 一句話戳破所有正能量謊言——犀利、諷刺、但說的都是你心裡話的幹話精選

en.title: Witty Comebacks
en.description: One-liners that cut through the noise — sharp, sardonic, and suspiciously accurate about everyday life

items.sourceNote: 負能量語錄參考，重新創作諷刺正向思考主題

zh-tw.content:
有人說：保持正向，一切都會好起來的。

我說：好，那我去正向地想想我的帳單要怎麼辦。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個動不動就叫你「想開一點」的朋友，讓他知道情緒價值不等於解決方案
variations: 保持樂觀是對的。但樂觀不會幫你繳水電費，這一點請也樂觀地接受。

en.content:
Someone told me: just stay positive, everything will work out.

I replied: great. I'll positively figure out what to do about my bills.
en.editorNote: Send to the friend who responds to every problem with 'just think positive' — gentle pushback delivered with a smile
variations: Staying optimistic is valid. But optimism won't pay your utilities. Please accept that fact optimistically.

items.sourceNote: 幹話語錄參考，重新創作夢想與現實主題

zh-tw.content:
夢想很重要，一定要去追。

但房租比夢想更準時，建議先把房租算清楚，再出發。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合傳給正在說「我想辭職去追夢」的朋友，幫他多想兩秒
variations: 追夢的路上，記得帶足夠的存款，不然中途就會先追到7-11打工。

en.content:
Dreams are important. Absolutely pursue them.

But rent is more punctual than any dream. I suggest sorting that out first before you set off.
en.editorNote: Send to the friend who just announced they're quitting their job to follow their passion — a gentle reality check
variations: On the road to chasing your dreams, make sure you pack enough savings — otherwise you'll end up chasing a part-time job first.

items.sourceNote: 靠北語錄參考，重新創作金錢快樂主題

zh-tw.content:
錢買不到快樂——

說這句話的人，顯然沒有試過下班後直接打開外送App，
然後在三分鐘內完全快樂起來。
zh-tw.editorNote: 下班沒力氣煮飯時傳給自己看，同時按下訂單確認，非常應景
variations: 我不確定錢能不能買到快樂，但我很確定沒有錢會讓我不快樂。兩件事。

en.content:
Money can't buy happiness —

Obviously said by someone who has never opened a food delivery app after work
and felt completely better within three minutes.
en.editorNote: Read this while placing your takeout order after a long day — the timing will feel exactly right
variations: I'm not sure money buys happiness. I am sure that not having money causes unhappiness. These are two separate claims.

items.sourceNote: 幽默語錄參考，重新創作做自己主題

zh-tw.content:
做自己就好，不需要改變。

但如果你自己是個很難相處的人，
那這句話可能需要加一個但書。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個說完「我就是這樣」就什麼都不管的朋友，讓他思考一下
variations: 「做自己」是很好的建議，前提是你的「自己」不會讓周圍的人過得很辛苦。

en.content:
Just be yourself — there's no need to change.

Unless 'yourself' is someone who's genuinely difficult to be around.
In that case, a small exception may apply.
en.editorNote: Share with the person who uses 'I'm just being myself' as a reason to avoid accountability — they'll either get it or they won't
variations: 'Be yourself' is great advice, provided your 'self' doesn't make life harder for everyone around you.

items.sourceNote: 負能量幹話語錄參考，重新創作努力成功主題

zh-tw.content:
吃苦是成功的必要條件。

那請問現在比我更辛苦的人，是不是快要比我更成功了？

我很期待他的好消息。
zh-tw.editorNote: 聽完主管說「年輕就是要吃苦」之後，在心裡默念這段話，可以有效維持表情穩定
variations: 如果努力就一定會成功，那現在失敗的人，代表他們之前還不夠努力。這個邏輯很乾淨。

en.content:
Suffering builds character and leads to success.

So the people who are struggling more than me right now — they must be on the verge of enormous success.

I genuinely look forward to their announcements.
en.editorNote: Recite this internally after someone tells you 'hard work always pays off' — keeps your expression neutral while your brain does the math
variations: If effort always leads to success, then people who are currently failing simply weren't working hard enough before. Very clean logic.

items.sourceNote: 幹話語錄參考，重新創作想太多樂觀主題

zh-tw.content:
不要想太多，樂觀一點。

但萬一我想太多之後，發現問題真的存在，
那是不是應該謝謝我當初有認真想？
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個每次說「你想太多了」但事後又說「你怎麼都沒預料到」的人，一次解決兩個問題
variations: 過度思考有時候叫做焦慮，有時候叫做預防。取決於你後來有沒有被證明是對的。

en.content:
Don't overthink it. Just be optimistic.

But what if I overthink it and discover the problem is real?
Shouldn't you then thank me for overthinking?
en.editorNote: Send to whoever says 'you're overthinking' but later says 'why didn't you see this coming?' — resolves both complaints at once
variations: Overthinking is sometimes called anxiety. Sometimes it's called preparation. The difference is whether you turned out to be right.

items.sourceNote: 搞笑語錄參考，重新創作時間療癒主題

zh-tw.content:
時間是最好的良藥，任何傷都能治。

可惜時間不能退款，也不能換成假期，
目前沒有其他功能。
zh-tw.editorNote: 失戀或心情不好時自嘲用，也適合傳給那個叫你「時間久了就好了」的朋友
variations: 時間確實能治癒，只是治癒的速度和你付出的等待時間不一定成正比。

en.content:
Time is the best medicine — it heals everything.

Unfortunately, time cannot be refunded or exchanged for vacation days.
No additional features are available.
en.editorNote: Use as self-deprecating humor after a rough patch, or send to whoever told you 'you'll feel better with time'
variations: Time does heal things. The healing timeline, however, is not guaranteed to match the waiting time you invested.

items.sourceNote: 負能量語錄參考，重新創作努力必成功主題

zh-tw.content:
只要夠努力，一定會成功的。

那麼多認真工作的人，可以先進來我這邊坐一下，
我們討論一下那個「一定」是怎麼定義的。
zh-tw.editorNote: 職場激勵演講結束後，默念這段話讓自己回到正常狀態，效果顯著
variations: 「努力就會成功」這句話的問題在於，它描述了一個相關性，但跳過了所有的例外情況。

en.content:
Work hard enough and you will definitely succeed.

Anyone who's been working seriously for years — please come sit with me.
I'd like to discuss what 'definitely' means.
en.editorNote: Mentally recite after a corporate motivational speech to return to normal cognitive function
variations: The problem with 'hard work equals success' is that it describes a correlation while skipping over all the exceptions.

items.sourceNote: 幹話語錄參考，重新創作笑一下主題

zh-tw.content:
笑一下嘛，笑一下又不會怎樣。

好，如果你能告訴我笑完帳單就消失了，
我立刻笑給你看，而且笑得很真誠。
zh-tw.editorNote: 有人叫你「開心一點」卻沒有提供任何解決方案時，這句話傳給他，非常精準
variations: 我很願意笑，但笑是情緒，不是解決方法。這兩件事情我分得很清楚。

en.content:
Come on, just smile. It won't kill you.

If you can confirm that smiling makes the bills disappear,
I will smile immediately, and I'll mean it.
en.editorNote: Send to whoever tells you to 'cheer up' without offering any actual help — precise and efficient
variations: I'm very willing to smile. But smiling is an emotion, not a solution. I keep these two things separate.

items.sourceNote: 靠北語錄參考，重新創作在意別人眼光主題

zh-tw.content:
不要在意別人的眼光，做自己最重要。

——來自一個從來沒有在工作考評裡被寫過
「與他人協作有待加強」的人。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個說完「不在意別人眼光」但其實很怕得罪人的朋友，看他會不會笑出來
variations: 「不要在意別人眼光」在某些場合是智慧，在某些場合是藉口，差別在於後果由誰承擔。

en.content:
Don't care what people think. Just be yourself.

— Advice from someone who has never received
'needs to improve collaboration' on a performance review.
en.editorNote: Send to the friend who says they don't care what people think but spends an hour crafting every message — see if they laugh
variations: 'Stop caring what people think' is wisdom in some contexts and an excuse in others. The difference is who ends up dealing with the consequences.

items.sourceNote: 搞笑語錄參考，重新創作人生苦短及時行樂主題

zh-tw.content:
人生苦短，要及時行樂。

說的也是，正是因為及時行樂，
所以現在帳戶也苦。
算是一種因果報應。
zh-tw.editorNote: 月底看著帳戶餘額時傳給自己，既是安慰也是解釋，一魚兩吃
variations: 及時行樂的代價，就是不及時的時候也要行樂，然後要還。這個循環我很熟悉。

en.content:
Life is short — live in the moment.

Fair point. And because I've been living in the moment so consistently,
my bank account is now also very short.
A kind of karmic balance.
en.editorNote: Read while staring at your end-of-month balance — equally a comfort and an explanation
variations: The cost of seizing the day is that when the day is over, you still have to pay for it. A cycle I know well.

items.sourceNote: 靠北語錄參考，重新創作世界美好多看看主題

zh-tw.content:
這個世界很美好，要多出去走走看看。

謝謝，等我把待辦清單清完，
我馬上去看。
大概下週，最快。
zh-tw.editorNote: 節假日被問「你去哪裡玩了嗎」的時候，這個回答既說明了現狀，又保留了未來的可能性
variations: 世界很大，我也很想去看。但目前我的行事曆跟我說不行，我尊重它的意見。

en.content:
The world is beautiful — you should get out and see more of it.

Thanks, I'll get right on that once I finish my to-do list.
Probably next week. Earliest.
en.editorNote: Use when someone asks if you 'got out' during the holiday — honest, non-committal, and technically leaves the future open
variations: The world is large and I'd love to see it. My calendar disagrees. I respect its perspective.

items.sourceNote: 搞笑語錄參考，重新創作天生樂觀心靈導師主題

zh-tw.content:
有些人天生樂觀，什麼都看得開。

然後他們就去當心靈導師了，
把「看得開」包裝成課程，賣給看不開的人，
賺了一份很開心的薪水。
zh-tw.editorNote: 看到某個勵志帳號又在賣課程時，可以傳給懂的朋友，是一種無聲的默契
variations: 最會說「放下執念」的人，通常對自己的收入執念得很深。這不是諷刺，這是觀察。

en.content:
Some people are naturally optimistic — they let everything go.

So they become life coaches,
package 'letting go' into a course, sell it to people who can't,
and earn a very cheerful income.
en.editorNote: Send to a friend when you both spot a motivational influencer promoting their new paid course — a moment of shared understanding
variations: The people who talk most about 'releasing attachment' tend to be deeply attached to their revenue. Not a criticism. Just an observation.

items.sourceNote: 負能量語錄參考，重新創作永不放棄主題

zh-tw.content:
不管發生什麼事，都不要輕易放棄。

有時候放棄也是一種策略，
只是這個版本的故事，版權還沒有被廣泛流通。
zh-tw.editorNote: 在決定要不要放棄某件事的時候，可以傳給自己，給自己一個不同的角度
variations: 「放棄」和「停損」是同一個行為，語言不同，情緒的接受度差很多。

en.content:
No matter what happens, never give up.

Sometimes quitting is a valid strategy.
That version just hasn't gone viral yet.
en.editorNote: Send to yourself when you're weighing whether to walk away from something — permission to see it from a different angle
variations: 'Giving up' and 'cutting losses' describe the same action. The word choice makes a big difference in how easily we accept it.

items.sourceNote: 幽默語錄參考，重新創作活出意義主題

zh-tw.content:
人活著要有意義，不能虛度光陰。

謝謝提醒，我剛才正在認真研究朋友的限時動態，
已經很有意義了，謝謝關心。
zh-tw.editorNote: 有人說你在浪費時間的時候，這個回答既防禦又幽默，而且技術上不算說謊
variations: 意義是主觀的，對不對？所以今天認真滑了三小時手機，也是一種自我實現。

en.content:
Live a meaningful life. Don't waste your time.

Thanks for the reminder. I was just thoroughly analyzing my friend's stories.
Already very meaningful. Appreciate the concern.
en.editorNote: Use when someone implies you're being unproductive — defensive, humorous, and technically not a lie
variations: Meaning is subjective, right? So spending three focused hours scrolling today is also a form of self-actualization.

items.sourceNote: 幹話語錄參考，重新創作凡事有原因主題

zh-tw.content:
所有事情的發生，都是有原因的。

好，我接受這個哲學。
那我的帳單為什麼每個月都有原因地繼續出現？

對不起，那個原因我不太喜歡，
請問可以選別的原因嗎？
zh-tw.editorNote: 有人說「一切都是命中注定」的時候傳這個，讓他思考一下命運的選擇性
variations: 「一切都是有原因的」這句話，在你想通那個原因之前是安慰，想通之後通常是更大的困惑。

en.content:
Everything happens for a reason.

Alright, I accept this philosophy.
So why does my monthly bill also keep happening, with a reason, every single month?

I'm not a fan of that particular reason.
Is there a way to request a different one?
en.editorNote: Send when someone says 'everything is meant to be' — invites them to apply the logic more specifically
variations: 'Everything happens for a reason' is comforting until you figure out the reason. After that, it's usually a bigger problem.

items.sourceNote: 幹話語錄參考，重新創作多運動快樂主題

zh-tw.content:
多運動就會快樂起來。

我試過了。
運動完之後我最快樂的那一刻，是停下來的瞬間。

所以我每天精準地快樂一次，
非常有效率。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給健身房的朋友，或是那個一直叫你「要多動」的人，讓他重新評估這個建議的細節
variations: 「運動會分泌多巴胺」這是真的。但分泌最集中的那一刻，是結束之後躺下來的那個當下。請合理使用。

en.content:
Exercise more and you'll feel happier.

I tried it.
The moment I'm happiest after exercise is the exact second I stop.

So I experience one precise moment of happiness per day.
Very efficient.
en.editorNote: Send to a gym friend or whoever keeps telling you to 'move more' — invites them to reconsider the specifics of their advice
variations: 'Exercise releases dopamine.' True. The most concentrated release is the moment you lie down after finishing. Please use responsibly.

items.sourceNote: 幹話語錄參考，重新創作多認識人人脈主題

zh-tw.content:
多認識人，人脈就是財脈。

謝謝提醒，我的人脈已經相當豐富了。
其中有幾位，目前還欠我錢。

如果這也算財脈的話，
那我的財脈確實活躍，只是資金流向有點奇特。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給在說「要多建立人脈」的朋友，讓他確認一下人脈和財脈的實際換算關係
variations: 人脈是資產，但資產分很多種。有些人脈是流動資產，有些是呆帳。管理方法不一樣。

en.content:
Build your network — your connections are your capital.

Thanks for the reminder. My network is already quite extensive.
Several of those connections currently owe me money.

If that counts as capital flow,
then my network is very active — the direction of the funds is just a bit unusual.
en.editorNote: Send to whoever is always talking about 'building connections' — invites them to verify the actual exchange rate between network and capital
variations: Connections are assets. But assets come in types. Some connections are liquid assets. Some are bad debt. Requires different management.

items.sourceNote: 幹話語錄參考，重新創作早睡早起主題

zh-tw.content:
早睡早起，身體就會變好。

我試過了。
早起之後我非常難受，
但我是在更早的時間開始難受，

效率確實提升了。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個每天叫你早起的朋友，讓他思考一下這個建議的實際效果
variations: 早起的人確實有蟲吃。問題是，我不想要那隻蟲，我寧可繼續睡。這是個人飲食偏好的問題。

en.content:
Sleep early, wake up early — your body will thank you.

I tried it.
After waking up early, I felt terrible.
But I was feeling terrible at an earlier hour,

so the efficiency did improve.
en.editorNote: Send to the person who keeps telling you to wake up early — lets them examine the specific outcome of their advice
variations: The early bird gets the worm. The problem is I don't want the worm. I'd rather keep sleeping. That's a personal dietary preference.

items.sourceNote: 無端建議語錄參考，重新創作沒人問你但你很熱心主題

zh-tw.content:
有一種人，你沒有問他，他已經開始建議你了。

而且建議完之後還會說：
「我這是為你好。」

好，謝謝你為我好。
但我當初沒有問，這個細節我記得很清楚。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個永遠在提供你沒要求的意見的朋友或親戚，適合節日家族聚會後發洩
variations: 沒有人問你，不代表你不能說。但說完之後加一句「我這是為你好」，那個責任就轉移得很不自然。

en.content:
Some people don't wait to be asked.
They'll have a full set of recommendations ready before you've finished your sentence.

And after delivering all of it, they'll add:
'I'm only saying this because I care.'

Appreciated. But I didn't ask.
That part I remember clearly.
en.editorNote: Send after a family gathering or to the friend who always has input on your life choices — cathartic and precise
variations: Giving unsolicited advice isn't wrong on its own. Ending it with 'I'm doing this for you' is where the accountability quietly disappears.

items.sourceNote: 單身幹話語錄參考，重新創作「你怎麼還沒交往」主題

zh-tw.content:
「你這麼好，怎麼還沒交往？」

謝謝你覺得我好。
但如果好就一定有人追，
那好的產品怎麼也會有庫存？

市場有它的邏輯，
不是我的問題。
zh-tw.editorNote: 被問「怎麼還單身」時的萬用回應，把問題直接轉成市場分析，讓對方沒有辦法繼續追問
variations: 單身不是因為條件不好，是因為我對「已售出」這個狀態的要求比較高，目前選品中。

en.content:
'You're so great — why are you still single?'

Thank you for thinking so.
But if being great guaranteed someone would come along,
why do good products still sit on shelves?

The market has its own logic.
This is not a personal failure.
en.editorNote: The go-to response when someone asks why you're still single — reframes the question as market analysis and leaves no obvious follow-up
variations: Being single isn't a quality issue. It's that my requirements for the 'sold' status are fairly specific. Currently still browsing.

items.sourceNote: 幹話語錄參考，重新創作走出舒適圈主題

zh-tw.content:
要走出舒適圈，才能成長。

我理解。
但我花了好幾年才把舒適圈建好，
你叫我走出去，

至少讓我把門鎖上再出發，
不然我回來的時候可能有人住進來了。
zh-tw.editorNote: 有人叫你「要突破自己」的時候，這段話可以讓你在心裡默默說完，表情保持平靜
variations: 舒適圈不是牢籠，是你努力維護的空間。偶爾出去看看沒問題，但沒有義務永遠待在外面。

en.content:
You need to step outside your comfort zone to grow.

Fair point.
But it took me years to build that comfort zone.
If I'm stepping out,

at least let me lock the door first.
Otherwise someone might move in while I'm gone.
en.editorNote: Recite internally when someone tells you to 'push your boundaries' — keeps your expression neutral while your inner monologue finishes its thought
variations: A comfort zone isn't a cage — it's a space you worked to create. Visiting outside it occasionally is fine. Living outside it permanently was never part of the deal.

items.sourceNote: 職場幽默語錄參考，重新創作開會主題

zh-tw.content:
又開了一個小時的會，
什麼決定都沒有。

但我學到了一件事：
只要把「後續再討論」說得夠有力，
任何議題都可以無限延期，
而且還顯得很負責。
zh-tw.editorNote: 開完一場沒有結論的會議之後，傳給同樣在場的同事，共鳴效果百分之百
variations: 會議的本質，是把一個人可以決定的事情，變成所有人都沒有辦法決定的事情，然後再開一次會。

en.content:
We just had an hour-long meeting.
No decisions were made.

But I did learn one thing:
if you say 'we'll circle back on this' with enough conviction,
any issue can be postponed indefinitely
while still sounding responsible.
en.editorNote: Send to a coworker right after a meeting that ended with zero conclusions — the shared recognition is immediate
variations: The purpose of a meeting is to take something one person could decide alone and turn it into something no one can decide together. Then schedule a follow-up.

items.sourceNote: 職場搞笑語錄參考，重新創作「要主動積極」主題

zh-tw.content:
工作要主動積極，不要等人家吩咐才做。

我試著主動提案了，
結果被說「超出你的職責範圍」。

後來我等人家吩咐再做，
又被說「不夠積極主動」。

謝謝，我已經找到這個遊戲的規則了：
規則是沒有規則，評分標準是看心情。
zh-tw.editorNote: 在職場上被矛盾要求搞到頭暈的時候，把這段話傳給懂的朋友，不需要解釋，他一定看得懂
variations: 職場上「主動積極」和「越權」之間的界線，不是由你的行動決定的，是由當下誰心情比較好決定的。

en.content:
Be proactive at work. Don't wait to be told.

I tried being proactive and submitting ideas.
I was told I was 'overstepping my role.'

So I waited to be told.
Then I was told I wasn't being proactive enough.

Thank you. I've figured out the rules:
there are no rules. The scoring depends on the day.
en.editorNote: Send to a coworker when you've both been hit with contradictory feedback — no explanation needed, they'll get it instantly
variations: In most workplaces, the line between 'taking initiative' and 'overstepping' isn't defined by your actions. It's defined by whoever's in the best mood that day.

items.sourceNote: 職場負能量語錄參考，重新創作社群媒體比較主題

zh-tw.content:
不要跟別人比，跟自己比就好。

好主意。

但我昨天滑了四十分鐘的限動，
全部都是別人出國、升職、買新車的精選集，

請問跟自己比的功能，
要從哪裡開啟？
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個同時叫你「不要比較」又每天在限動曬成就的朋友，一魚兩吃
variations: 「不要跟別人比」是很健康的想法。但建議同步把限動演算法一起調整，不然這個想法每天都會被覆蓋掉。

en.content:
Don't compare yourself to others. Only compare yourself to who you were yesterday.

Great advice.

But I just spent forty minutes scrolling through stories —
a curated highlight reel of everyone's vacations, promotions, and new cars.

Could you tell me which setting turns on
the 'compare only to yourself' mode?
en.editorNote: Send to the friend who tells you not to compare yourself to others while also posting their own daily achievement stories — hits two targets at once
variations: 'Stop comparing yourself to others' is genuinely healthy advice. I'd also recommend updating the algorithm at the same time, or it'll be overwritten daily.

items.sourceNote: 職場厭世語錄參考，重新創作「年紀大了就懂了」主題

zh-tw.content:
你還年輕，等你老了就懂了。

所以你的意思是，
我現在所有的困惑和痛苦，
都是因為我還不夠老？

好，那我先去老一下，
請問幾歲才算「夠懂了」？

我想把那個日期標在行事曆上。
zh-tw.editorNote: 被長輩說「你不懂」的時候，這段話在心裡說一遍，可以把不耐煩轉換成幽默
variations: 「等你老了就懂了」這句話的意思是：我現在不打算解釋，但我很確定我是對的。這兩件事情並不一樣。

en.content:
You're young. You'll understand when you're older.

So what you're saying is,
all my current confusion and struggle
exists simply because I'm not old enough yet?

Alright. I'll go get older.
At what exact age does understanding kick in?

I'd like to put it in my calendar.
en.editorNote: Recite mentally when an older person dismisses your perspective with 'you'll get it eventually' — converts frustration into internal comedy
variations: 'You'll understand when you're older' roughly translates to: I'm not going to explain this, but I'm confident I'm right. These are not the same thing.

items.sourceNote: 上班族厭世金句參考，重新創作「週末要好好休息」主題

zh-tw.content:
週末要好好充電，下週才有精神上班。

對，我週末充了很多電。
充到週日晚上電全滿了。

然後週一早上九點，
電量顯示：百分之三。

我懷疑這個設備有耗電問題，
但原廠說這是正常現象。
zh-tw.editorNote: 週一早上進辦公室時傳給同事，他不需要解釋，他和你的電量一樣低
variations: 「休息是為了走更長遠的路」這句話我信。只是週末兩天能補回來的，似乎不足以應付週間五天消耗掉的。數學不太對。

en.content:
Rest up on the weekend so you have energy for work next week.

I did rest. I recharged extensively.
By Sunday evening I was at full capacity.

Then Monday at 9 a.m.,
battery level: three percent.

I suspect there's a drain issue with this device,
but the manufacturer says it's functioning normally.
en.editorNote: Send to a coworker on Monday morning — no explanation needed, their battery is at three percent too
variations: 'Rest is fuel for the road ahead.' I believe this. I just notice that two weekend days don't seem to fully offset five weekdays of depletion. The math is slightly off.

items.sourceNote: 幽默回嘴語錄參考，重新創作「你看起來很累」主題

zh-tw.content:
「你今天看起來很累耶。」

謝謝你的觀察報告。
我本人也注意到了這個現象，
而且比你早大約十二個小時就注意到了。

目前正在進行中，感謝關心，
請繼續保持安全距離。
zh-tw.editorNote: 有人在你狀態最差的時候說出這句話，傳這個給他，既禮貌又說清楚不需要多嘴
variations: 「你看起來很累」這句話，說出來的人一定是沒有比你更累，不然他不會有力氣觀察你。

en.content:
'You look really tired today.'

Thank you for that detailed field report.
I was already aware of the situation —
about twelve hours before you noticed.

Investigation is ongoing. Appreciate the concern.
Please maintain a safe observational distance.
en.editorNote: For when someone points out you look tired at your absolute worst — polite, clear, and discourages follow-up commentary
variations: Anyone who has the energy to tell you that you look tired is, by definition, less tired than you. They should use that energy more wisely.

items.sourceNote: 機智幹話語錄參考，重新創作「要有耐心」主題

zh-tw.content:
凡事要有耐心，好事需要等待。

我很有耐心。
我已經有耐心地等了很久，
很有耐心地看著機會一個一個出現，
然後也很有耐心地看著它們一個一個離開。

請問耐心的功效，大概什麼時候會開始作用？
我只是想知道我還需要充幾次貨。
zh-tw.editorNote: 有人叫你「再等等，時機未到」時，這段話可以讓你在心裡說完，表情依然平靜
variations: 耐心是美德，但美德不附帶保證書，不保固，也不退款。這一點請在使用前確認。

en.content:
Be patient. Good things take time.

I am patient.
I've been patiently waiting for a long time,
patiently watching opportunities appear one by one,
and then patiently watching them leave, also one by one.

At what point does patience start producing results?
I just want to know how many more rounds I need to stockpile.
en.editorNote: Recite internally when someone tells you to 'just wait, the timing isn't right yet' — keeps your composure intact while your brain finishes the thought
variations: Patience is a virtue. Virtues, however, come with no warranty, no service guarantee, and no refund policy. Please confirm this before use.

items.sourceNote: 幽默幹話語錄參考，重新創作「我最近很忙」已讀不回主題

zh-tw.content:
「不好意思，最近太忙了，沒時間回。」

忙到沒時間，但限動每天都有更新。

我理解，有些任務的優先順序就是比較高，
例如：「記錄今天喝了什麼咖啡」，
這個確實比「回覆朋友的訊息」緊急一些。

沒關係，我等你不忙了再說。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個已讀不回但限動天天在曬的朋友，不需要說什麼，他自己會懂
variations: 「太忙了」是可以理解的理由，但忙到沒有三秒鐘回一個訊息，卻有十分鐘上傳限動，這個忙法我想多了解一下。

en.content:
'Sorry, I've been super busy — didn't have time to reply.'

Too busy to respond. Yet the stories update daily.

I understand — some tasks simply rank higher in priority.
For instance: 'document today's coffee order'
clearly outranks 'reply to a friend's message.'

No worries. I'll wait until you're less busy.
en.editorNote: Send to the person who ghosts your messages but posts daily stories — no explanation needed, the point lands on its own
variations: 'Too busy' is a valid reason. Being too busy for a three-second reply but not for a ten-minute story upload is a scheduling philosophy I'd love to study further.

items.sourceNote: 機智回嘴語錄參考，重新創作「別人都做得到，你怎麼不行」主題

zh-tw.content:
「別人都做得到，你為什麼做不到？」

謝謝提供比較對象。
那我可以問一下，
「別人」在這裡具體是指哪幾位？

因為我也找了一些「別人」，
他們也有一些事情做不到，
我想把名單對一下，
看看我們是不是可以互相理解。
zh-tw.editorNote: 被拿來和「別人」比較時，這段話說出來（或在心裡說），可以把比較的邏輯直接送回給對方
variations: 「別人都做得到」這句話，省略了一個重要細節：別人也有很多事情做不到，只是你剛好沒有在觀察那個部分。

en.content:
'Everyone else can do it. Why can't you?'

Thank you for providing a reference group.
Could I ask — who exactly are we talking about here?

Because I've also collected some data on 'everyone else,'
and they have a fairly long list of things they can't do either.
I'd like to compare notes
and see if we can arrive at some mutual understanding.
en.editorNote: Use when someone compares you unfavorably to 'everyone else' — returns the logic of comparison directly to the sender
variations: 'Everyone else can do it' quietly omits the part where everyone else also has a long list of things they can't do. You just weren't watching that part.

items.sourceNote: 幹話語錄參考，重新創作「你有沒有想過這個問題」主題

zh-tw.content:
「你有沒有想過，你的問題出在哪裡？」

有，我想過。
我想了很久，想得相當仔細，
大概比你替我想的時間長了十倍左右。

謝謝你的關心，
只是這個問題我已經認識它很久了，
我們不需要再做介紹。
zh-tw.editorNote: 有人用「你有沒有想過」開頭要給你建議時，這段話幫你在心裡先緩衝一下，再決定要不要聽
variations: 別人說「你有沒有想過」的時候，通常他們想說的是：「你應該照我說的方式去想。」這兩句話意思不太一樣。

en.content:
'Have you ever stopped to think about what your problem actually is?'

Yes. I have.
I've thought about it at length, in considerable detail —
probably about ten times longer than you've thought about it on my behalf.

Thank you for your concern.
This problem and I are already well acquainted.
No further introductions are necessary.
en.editorNote: For when someone opens with 'have you ever thought about...' before delivering unsolicited analysis — gives your internal monologue a moment to buffer
variations: When someone says 'have you ever thought about it,' they usually mean 'have you thought about it exactly the way I'm about to describe.' These are not the same question.

items.sourceNote: 幹話語錄參考，重新創作「要懂得感恩」說教主題

zh-tw.content:
你要懂得感恩，很多人連你現在這樣都沒有。

我知道，我很感恩。
但感恩和抱怨不是互斥的，
我可以同時感謝擁有的，
也可以對不滿意的地方說出來。

這兩件事情，我應該可以同時處理，
不需要其中一個把另一個消音。
zh-tw.editorNote: 有人用「要感恩」堵住你的情緒出口時，這段話讓你說清楚：感恩不等於不能有感受
variations: 「你要感恩」是真的。但感恩不是用來壓制情緒的道具，它是一種心態，不是一個句點。

en.content:
You should be grateful. Plenty of people would love to be in your position.

I know. And I am.
But gratitude and frustration aren't opposites.
I can appreciate what I have
while also acknowledging what isn't working.

These two things can coexist.
One doesn't have to silence the other.
en.editorNote: Use when someone responds to your frustration with 'just be grateful' — clarifies that gratitude and feelings aren't mutually exclusive
variations: 'Be grateful' is genuine advice. But gratitude isn't a tool for suppressing emotions — it's a mindset, not a full stop at the end of a conversation.

items.sourceNote: 幹話語錄參考，重新創作「成功要靠貴人」主題

zh-tw.content:
成功的人背後都有貴人相助。

好，那我想請問一下：
貴人什麼時候上班？
有沒有預約方式？
還是直接walk-in就好？

因為我等了一段時間了，
想確認一下我是不是排到了正確的隊伍。
zh-tw.editorNote: 有人說你只差一個貴人時，這段話問出所有人心裡其實想問的問題
variations: 貴人存在是真的，但貴人不是叫來就來的。大多數的成功故事裡，主角等貴人等到快先成功了，貴人才剛好出現。

en.content:
Every successful person had someone who helped them along the way.

Great. Quick question:
What are this mentor's office hours?
Is there an appointment system?
Or is it walk-in only?

I've been waiting for a while now
and I want to make sure I'm in the right queue.
en.editorNote: For when someone tells you that you just need 'the right person to believe in you' — asks the question everyone is actually thinking
variations: Mentors and lucky breaks are real. They just don't arrive on demand. In most success stories, the protagonist was almost already there by the time the right person showed up.

items.sourceNote: 幽默語錄參考，重新創作「計畫趕不上變化」主題

zh-tw.content:
計畫趕不上變化，所以計畫沒什麼用。

好，那我不計畫。
然後你說我沒有方向感。

後來我有計畫，
然後你說計畫趕不上變化。

我現在只是想確認一下：
到底有沒有一種狀態，是你覺得可以的？
zh-tw.editorNote: 被用「計畫趕不上變化」否定了你的計畫之後，把這段話說出來，問題就回到了說話的人身上
variations: 「計畫趕不上變化」是事實，但沒有計畫，連趕都不知道要往哪裡趕。這兩件事的關係，比這句話看起來複雜。

en.content:
Plans never survive contact with reality, so what's the point of planning?

Fair. So I stopped planning.
Then you said I had no direction.

So I made a plan.
Then you said plans never work out anyway.

I just want to confirm:
is there any state of being you'd actually be okay with?
en.editorNote: Use after someone dismisses your plan with 'things always change anyway' — neatly redirects the contradiction back to the person who said it
variations: 'Plans never survive reality' is true. But without a plan, you don't even know which direction you're failing to reach. The relationship between these two facts is more complicated than the saying implies.

items.sourceNote: 機智幹話語錄參考，重新創作「錯過FOMO」主題

zh-tw.content:
你有沒有一種感覺，
別人的人生一直在發生很多事，
只有你的人生在原地等待更新？

後來我發現，
別人的人生也在等待更新，
只是他們在等的時候，
順手拍了一張照然後發出來而已。

我少的不是生活，是出片率。
zh-tw.editorNote: 滑了半小時限動之後覺得自己人生空白時傳給自己，立刻恢復清醒
variations: FOMO的本質，是把別人精選過的高光時刻，拿來和自己沒有剪輯過的完整生活比較。這個比較方法從一開始就不公平。

en.content:
Ever get the feeling everyone else's life is constantly happening,
while yours is just sitting there waiting for an update?

I eventually realized:
everyone else is also waiting for updates.
They just take a photo while waiting
and post it.

The difference isn't how much life I have.
It's my content output rate.
en.editorNote: Read after thirty minutes of scrolling leaves you feeling like your life is blank — instant reality check
variations: FOMO is basically comparing someone else's curated highlight reel to your own unedited full-length footage. The comparison was never fair to begin with.

items.sourceNote: 職場幹話語錄參考，重新創作「拼命衝就對了」爆肝文化主題

zh-tw.content:
「要拚，才有未來！」

我拚了。
拚到身體開始發出一些之前沒有過的聲音，
醫生說那個聲音叫做「需要休息」。

原來未來就在診間裡，
不需要拚，
掛號就可以抵達。
zh-tw.editorNote: 給那個動不動就說「現在不拚什麼時候拚」的朋友或主管，讓他們看看拚的終點站長什麼樣子
variations: 「拚命」這個詞，前半段是動力，後半段是警告。但大多數人只讀到前半段就出發了。

en.content:
'You have to hustle hard if you want a future!'

I hustled.
Hustled until my body started making sounds it never made before.
The doctor said those sounds are called 'needs rest.'

Turns out the future was in the clinic all along.
No hustle required.
Just an appointment.
en.editorNote: Send to whoever keeps saying 'now is the time to grind' — lets them see what the final destination of non-stop hustling actually looks like
variations: The word 'hustle' has two halves: the first is motivation, the second is a warning. Most people ship out after reading only the first half.

items.sourceNote: 日常幹話語錄參考，重新創作「你怎麼那麼安靜」主題

zh-tw.content:
「你怎麼都不說話，是不是有什麼問題？」

沒有。
我只是在聽你說話，
因為我從小被教導：
「沉默是金。」

我很認真地執行這個建議，
沒想到還是會被說有問題。

安靜也不行，說話又嫌太多，
請問理想的音量是多少分貝？
我可以設定一下。
zh-tw.editorNote: 有人問你「怎麼那麼安靜」的時候，這段話讓你笑著把問題還回去，而且不需要真的說出來
variations: 有些人安靜是因為沒有想說的。有些人是因為說了也沒用。這兩種安靜，外表一樣，但內容完全不同。

en.content:
'Why are you so quiet? Is something wrong?'

Nothing's wrong.
I'm just listening to you talk.
Because I was raised to believe
that silence is golden.

I've been faithfully following that advice.
Apparently it's still a problem.

Too quiet is wrong. Too talkative is also wrong.
Could you specify the ideal decibel level?
I can calibrate.
en.editorNote: For when someone asks why you're so quiet — lets you smile and return the question without actually saying any of this out loud
variations: Some people are quiet because they have nothing to say. Some are quiet because saying things doesn't help. The two look identical from the outside.

items.sourceNote: 幹話語錄參考，重新創作「照顧好自己」self-care主題

zh-tw.content:
要好好照顧自己，
多喝水、多睡覺、多曬太陽。

我最近有在認真執行，
多喝水：有的，在喝咖啡。
多睡覺：有的，在夢裡工作。
多曬太陽：有的，從公司窗戶看到一角。

三項全中，
謝謝你的保健建議，我照單全收了。
zh-tw.editorNote: 有人語重心長地叫你「要照顧自己」時，這段話可以讓你笑著點頭，不需要解釋自己的現狀
variations: 「照顧好自己」是真的很重要。只是它的前提是你有時間、精力、和餘裕去照顧，而這三樣東西，偏偏是同一個原因造成缺貨的。

en.content:
Take care of yourself —
drink more water, sleep more, get some sunlight.

I've been diligently working on all three.
More water: yes, in the form of coffee.
More sleep: yes, I've been working in my dreams.
More sunlight: yes, there's a sliver visible from my office window.

Three for three.
Thank you for the wellness advice. Fully implemented.
en.editorNote: Use when someone earnestly tells you to 'take care of yourself' — lets you nod and smile without having to explain your actual situation
variations: 'Taking care of yourself' is genuinely important. It just assumes you have the time, energy, and margin to do it — which happen to be the exact three things the original problem already used up.

items.sourceNote: 幹話語錄參考，重新創作「你太敏感了」主題

zh-tw.content:
「你太敏感了，不要想那麼多。」

謝謝你的診斷。

但如果「敏感」是問題的話，
那這個問題不是我說出來之後才存在的，
它在我說出來之前就已經在了。

你剛才只是收到了通知，
不是造成了問題。

這個差別，有點重要。
zh-tw.editorNote: 被說「想太多」或「太敏感」的時候，這段話可以讓你清楚地說明：感受本身不是問題，被忽視才是
variations: 「你太敏感」有時候的意思是：你的感受讓我不舒服，所以問題在你身上。這個邏輯，我不接受。

en.content:
'You're too sensitive. Stop overthinking everything.'

Thank you for the diagnosis.

But if sensitivity is the problem,
it didn't come into existence the moment I said something.
It existed long before I spoke up.

You just received a notification.
You didn't cause a problem.

That distinction matters.
en.editorNote: For when someone tells you you're 'overthinking' or 'too sensitive' — cleanly separates the feeling from the act of expressing it
variations: Sometimes 'you're too sensitive' means: your feelings make me uncomfortable, so the problem must be yours. That logic I don't accept.

items.sourceNote: 外表批評回應語錄參考，重新創作「你最近變胖了/變瘦了」主題

zh-tw.content:
「你最近好像有變胖耶？」

謝謝你認真觀察我的身體，
並且立刻分享你的發現。

我自己也有一面鏡子，
使用頻率大概比你更高。

如果你的觀察有什麼我還不知道的新發現，
請寄一份正式報告給我，
我會列入參考。
zh-tw.editorNote: 有人在問候你之前先評論你的體型時，這段話讓你把觀察球直接彈回去，語氣平靜但說清楚了邊界
variations: 評論別人身材這件事，有一個很容易被忽略的細節：對方不需要你的觀察報告，他們早就知道了，而且知道得比你久。

en.content:
'Have you put on a bit of weight lately?'

Thank you for carefully studying my body
and immediately sharing your findings.

I do own a mirror —
and I use it considerably more often than you do.

If your observation contains any new data
I wasn't already aware of,
please send a formal report.
I'll file it under 'noted.'
en.editorNote: For when someone greets you by commenting on your body before saying anything else — returns the observation ball quietly but firmly
variations: Commenting on someone's body has one frequently overlooked detail: they already know. They've known longer than you have. Your report is not breaking news.

items.sourceNote: 幽默回嘴語錄參考，重新創作「我不是懶，我只是在省電」主題

zh-tw.content:
有人說我做事很慢、不夠積極。

我不接受「懶惰」這個定性。
正確的說法是：
我目前處於低耗能模式，
優先保留資源給真正重要的任務。

這是資源配置，不是怠惰。
請使用正確的技術名詞。
zh-tw.editorNote: 被說「動作好慢」或「怎麼不積極一點」時，這段話幫你把「懶」重新定義成一種策略，聽起來立刻專業很多
variations: 不是所有的緩慢都是怠惰，有時候是蓄力，有時候是評估，有時候只是還沒遇到值得快的事。這三種情況請分開處理。

en.content:
Someone said I was slow and not motivated enough.

I reject the label 'lazy.'
The accurate term is:
I am currently operating in low-power mode,
reserving resources for tasks that actually matter.

This is resource allocation, not negligence.
Please use the correct technical terminology.
en.editorNote: For when someone calls you slow or unmotivated — reframes 'lazy' as a deliberate strategy, which instantly sounds more professional
variations: Not all slowness is laziness. Sometimes it's preparation. Sometimes it's evaluation. Sometimes nothing worth moving fast for has appeared yet. Please categorize accordingly.

items.sourceNote: 機智回應語錄參考，重新創作「我同意你，但這樣我們都錯了」主題

zh-tw.content:
有人一直要我同意他的看法。

我很想同意你，真的。
但如果我同意了，
那我們兩個同時都錯了，
而且誰也沒有辦法提醒誰。

你看，我拒絕同意，
其實是在保護這段對話的品質，
你應該感謝我。
zh-tw.editorNote: 有人一直要求你站他那邊時，這段話讓你把「不同意」轉成一種替對方著想的行為，邏輯上難以反駁
variations: 同意別人是一種禮貌，但同意一個錯的人，是一種對雙方都不誠實的禮貌。這種禮貌，我選擇性地省略。

en.content:
Someone kept pressing me to agree with their take.

I genuinely want to agree with you.
But if I do,
then we'd both be wrong simultaneously
with no one left to flag it.

You see, my refusal to agree
is actually protecting the quality of this conversation.
You should probably thank me.
en.editorNote: For when someone keeps pushing you to take their side — reframes 'disagreeing' as an act of care for the other person, which is hard to argue with
variations: Agreeing with people is a courtesy. Agreeing with someone who's wrong is a courtesy that's dishonest to both parties. That particular courtesy I selectively skip.

items.sourceNote: 幹話回應語錄參考，重新創作「你插嘴打斷別人」主題

zh-tw.content:
有人在我說到一半的時候插嘴，
然後說完自己想說的，
又問我：「你剛才說什麼？」

我說什麼？
我說的那句話，
在你插進來的時候就已經放棄掙扎了，
現在正在某個句子的半空中漂著，
等不到結尾。

等你哪天有空，
我再找機會把它說完。
zh-tw.editorNote: 聊天時一直被打斷的時候，這段話讓你用幽默的方式說出這件事，不需要指責，對方自己就會懂
variations: 被打斷不是什麼大事，但連續被打斷之後對方還問「你說什麼」，這說明那句話從一開始就不在他的接收頻道上。

en.content:
Someone cut me off mid-sentence,
finished whatever they had to say,
then asked: 'Wait, what were you saying?'

What was I saying?
That sentence gave up the moment you stepped in.
It's currently floating somewhere between a subject and a verb,
waiting for a conclusion that never came.

When you have a free moment,
I'll try to rescue it.
en.editorNote: For when someone keeps interrupting you and then asks what you said — names the dynamic with humor instead of accusation
variations: Getting interrupted once is fine. Getting interrupted repeatedly and then asked 'what were you saying' means the sentence was never in their listening range to begin with.

items.sourceNote: 機智幹話語錄參考，重新創作「謝謝你沒有人要求的建議」主題

zh-tw.content:
感謝你的建議。

我有一個專門收藏這類建議的地方，
沒有人要求我建立這個收藏，
但它已經相當豐富了。

你的這條已經入庫，
位置很好，就在上個禮拜那條旁邊。

感謝持續貢獻館藏。
zh-tw.editorNote: 當有人不請自來地給你建議，而且還說得很理所當然時，這段話讓你禮貌地讓對方知道：你聽到了，但你不需要
variations: 沒被要求的建議不一定是壞建議，但它有一個共同點：給建議的人，通常比收到建議的人更需要說出那些話。

en.content:
Thank you for the advice.

I keep a dedicated collection for this kind of input.
Nobody asked me to start it,
but it has grown into quite an impressive archive.

Yours has been catalogued.
Excellent placement — right next to last week's contribution.

Thank you for your continued donations to the collection.
en.editorNote: For when someone delivers unsolicited advice as if it were completely expected — politely signals that you heard it and filed it, without inviting further discussion
variations: Unrequested advice isn't always bad advice. But it does share one consistent feature: the person giving it usually needs to say it more than the recipient needs to hear it.

items.sourceNote: 職場幹話語錄參考，重新創作「全員回覆」郵件文化主題

zh-tw.content:
公司寄了一封全員通知。

接著有人按了「全部回覆」說謝謝。
然後另一個人按「全部回覆」說收到。
再然後有人按「全部回覆」說請停止全部回覆。

這封郵件現在有二十三封回覆，
沒有任何一封包含實際上有用的資訊。

我學到了一件事：
全部回覆按鈕，是測試人性的裝置。
zh-tw.editorNote: 收到那封夾雜了十幾封「謝謝」、「收到」的公司信件後，把這段話傳給同事，大家都懂，誰都不需要解釋
variations: 「全部回覆」這個功能的存在，證明了人類有一種強烈的本能：在不必要的時候，讓所有人都知道自己的存在。

en.content:
The company sent a company-wide announcement.

Someone hit 'Reply All' to say thanks.
Another person hit 'Reply All' to say noted.
Then someone hit 'Reply All' to ask everyone to stop hitting Reply All.

This thread now has twenty-three replies.
None of them contain actionable information.

One lesson learned:
the Reply All button is a device for testing human nature.
en.editorNote: Send to a coworker right after your inbox fills up with 'thanks!' and 'got it!' from the whole company — instant shared catharsis
variations: The existence of Reply All proves that humans have a deep instinct to make sure everyone knows they exist, especially when there's no reason for everyone to know.

items.sourceNote: 幽默幹話語錄參考，重新創作「你去查一下就知道了」敷衍主題

zh-tw.content:
「這個你自己去查一下就知道了。」

謝謝。
我確實可以自己查。

但我問你，是因為我以為我們在對話，
不是因為我不知道搜尋引擎怎麼用。

如果「去查一下」是標準答案，
請問你在這段對話裡的定位是什麼？

我只是想確認一下，
這樣我下次就知道要不要開口。
zh-tw.editorNote: 問了一個問題卻被叫去自己查時，這段話幫你說出心裡那個微妙的失落感，同時保持表面上的理性
variations: 「去網路上查」是對的。但如果所有問題的答案都是「去查」，那人和人之間的對話，功能上就和搜尋框差不多了。

en.content:
'You can just look it up, you know.'

Thank you.
I am aware that search engines exist.

But I asked you because I thought we were having a conversation,
not because I needed a referral to a website.

If 'go look it up' is the standard response,
could you clarify what your role is in this exchange?

I'd like to know for next time
whether it's worth opening my mouth.
en.editorNote: For when you ask something and get brushed off with 'just Google it' — names the quiet deflation of being redirected away from a human connection
variations: 'Just look it up' is valid. But if every question ends in a search referral, then talking to a person and talking to a search bar start to serve the same function.

items.sourceNote: AI取代工作焦慮語錄參考，重新創作「你要趕快學AI不然會被取代」主題

zh-tw.content:
「你要趕快學AI，不然工作會被取代。」

謝謝提醒。

我正在努力學習AI，
同時AI也在努力學習如何取代我，

我們兩個都很認真。
只是進度上，它目前好像快一點。

不過沒關係，
學習的過程本身就很有意義，
這是我從勵志貼文裡學到的。
zh-tw.editorNote: 被叫去「學AI自救」的時候，這段話讓你用幽默回應那個追也追不上的速度感，同時諷刺到勵志話語的無力
variations: 「學AI才不會被取代」和「學馬車修理才不會被火車取代」，在各自的時代裡，聽起來都很有道理，也都沒什麼用。

en.content:
'You need to learn AI fast, or your job will be replaced.'

Duly noted.

I'm working hard at learning AI.
AI is also working hard at learning how to replace me.

We're both very committed.
Its progress is just slightly ahead of mine at the moment.

But that's fine —
the learning process itself is meaningful.
I read that in a motivational post.
en.editorNote: For when someone tells you to 'upskill in AI or get left behind' — uses humor to acknowledge the absurdity of racing something that doesn't get tired
variations: 'Learn AI or get replaced' and 'learn carriage repair or get replaced by the train' both sounded reasonable in their respective eras. Neither turned out to be quite the solution promised.

items.sourceNote: 日常幹話語錄參考，重新創作「你試試看冥想/寫日記」wellness建議主題

zh-tw.content:
「你試試看冥想，壓力就會少很多。」

我試過了。
我坐下來，閉上眼睛，
讓思緒安靜下來。

思緒安靜了大概四秒，
然後它開始列出我明天的待辦清單，
接著提醒我那封還沒回的信，
最後順便回顧了我三年前說過的一句話。

冥想的效果非常顯著：
我多了一份詳細的焦慮清單。
zh-tw.editorNote: 有人叫你「去冥想放鬆」時，這段話讓你笑著說出那個坐下來之後反而更亂的真實體驗
variations: 冥想確實有效，只是「有效」的意思是讓你更清楚地看見自己的焦慮，而不是讓焦慮消失。這兩件事差很多。

en.content:
'You should try meditation. It really helps with stress.'

I tried.
I sat down, closed my eyes,
and let my thoughts settle.

They settled for about four seconds.
Then they started running through tomorrow's to-do list,
moved on to remind me about an email I never answered,
and wrapped up with a highlight reel of something awkward I said three years ago.

The meditation was highly effective:
I now have a very detailed inventory of my anxieties.
en.editorNote: For when someone suggests meditation as the fix for everything — names the very real experience of sitting down to be calm and ending up more organized in your panic
variations: Meditation does work. 'Works' just means it shows you your anxiety more clearly, not that it makes the anxiety leave. These are meaningfully different outcomes.

items.sourceNote: 職場幹話語錄參考，重新創作主管「辛苦了」公式回應主題

zh-tw.content:
主管走過來拍拍我的肩膀說：「辛苦了。」

謝謝。
這句話很溫暖，
大概可以維持三秒。

三秒之後我發現，
它不會幫我處理那份報告，
不會幫我加班費入帳，
也不會讓那個跨部門需求消失。

「辛苦了」是一種精神上的口糧，
熱量很低，但配著加班吃還算可以。
zh-tw.editorNote: 被主管丟一句「辛苦了」就打發的時候，傳給同事一起苦笑，誰都不必再多說什麼
variations: 「辛苦了」三個字，是職場上最便宜的安慰劑。免費、無副作用、也沒有任何治療效果。

en.content:
My manager walked over, patted my shoulder, and said: 'Great work, really appreciate the hard work.'

Thank you.
That sentence is genuinely warming,
for approximately three seconds.

After three seconds I noticed:
it doesn't finish the report,
it doesn't show up as overtime pay,
and it doesn't make the cross-department request disappear.

'Appreciate the hard work' is a kind of emotional ration —
low calorie, but it pairs okay with overtime.
en.editorNote: For when your boss drops a 'thanks for all the hard work' and walks off — share with a coworker, no further explanation required
variations: 'Thanks for the hard work' is the cheapest placebo in the workplace. Free, no side effects, and no measurable therapeutic value either.

items.sourceNote: 霸氣回懟語錄參考，重新創作「我這樣是為你好」道德綁架主題

zh-tw.content:
「我這樣說是為你好。」

謝謝你的好意。

但「為你好」這四個字，
通常是說話的人需要它，
不是聽的人需要。

它讓批評變成善意，
讓干涉變成關心，
讓不請自來的意見變成一份禮物。

下次可以直接說你的看法，
不用先包裝。
我比較想知道你真正想說什麼，
而不是這份禮物的緞帶有多漂亮。
zh-tw.editorNote: 有人用「為你好」當前綴對你開砲時，這段話讓你不情緒化地拆掉那層糖衣，把對話拉回真正的內容
variations: 「為你好」是一個方便的開場白，它讓任何後面那句話都變得難以反駁。但好不好，是聽的人決定的，不是說的人。

en.content:
'I'm only saying this for your own good.'

Thank you for the concern.

But the phrase 'for your own good'
is usually needed by the person saying it,
not the person hearing it.

It turns criticism into kindness,
turns interference into care,
and turns unsolicited opinions into a gift.

Next time, just share your view directly.
No wrapping needed.
I'm more interested in what you actually mean
than in how nice the ribbon looks.
en.editorNote: For when someone weaponizes 'I'm telling you this for your own good' — strips the framing without escalating, returning the conversation to the actual point
variations: 'For your own good' is a convenient opener — it makes whatever comes next hard to push back on. But whether it's actually good is decided by the listener, not the speaker.

items.sourceNote: 機智幹話語錄參考，重新創作「你怎麼還沒結婚／生小孩」親戚問題主題

zh-tw.content:
親戚問：「你怎麼還沒結婚？」

我想了一下，
認真地回答：

「因為結婚是兩個人的事，
我這邊已經準備好了，
但市場那邊正在進行庫存盤點，
預計完成時間未公告。」

親戚沉默了三秒，
然後改問我薪水多少。

我發現，
親戚的問題清單不會變短，
只會輪流上場。
zh-tw.editorNote: 過年被親戚連環問人生進度時，這段話幫你用幽默回應，不需要翻臉也不必解釋人生規劃
variations: 「你怎麼還沒結婚」這個問題的標準回答，應該是一個謎語，這樣對方下次想到要問之前，會先想起你的謎語還沒解開。

en.content:
A relative asked: 'Why aren't you married yet?'

I thought about it,
and answered seriously:

'Because marriage takes two people.
My side is ready.
The market side is currently doing inventory.
No estimated completion date has been announced.'

The relative was silent for three seconds,
then pivoted to asking about my salary.

I've noticed:
the relative-question list never gets shorter.
The questions just take turns.
en.editorNote: For holiday gatherings when a relative starts the lifecycle interrogation — answer with humor that closes the topic without making it a confrontation
variations: The correct answer to 'why aren't you married yet' should be a riddle. That way, before they ask again next year, they'll first remember they never solved the last one.

items.sourceNote: 諷刺語錄參考，重新創作「我只是在開玩笑你不要太認真」主題

zh-tw.content:
說完一句讓人不舒服的話之後，
對方補了一句：
「開玩笑的啦，你不要太認真。」

我懂了。

所以剛才那句話的意思是：
它想出現，
但又不想負責。

這個語法很方便，
把一句話和它的後果拆開來，
然後只留下前面那一半。

下次你開玩笑的時候，
可以提前告訴我，
我會準備好不認真。
zh-tw.editorNote: 被人講酸話又用「開玩笑」當盾牌時，這段話讓你溫和但精準地戳破那個免責條款
variations: 「開玩笑的」這三個字的功能，是讓一句話可以發射，但不必落地。聽到的人都看到了那個拋物線，只是被告知不必去想它落在哪裡。

en.content:
After saying something that landed uncomfortably,
the person added:
'I'm just kidding, don't take it so seriously.'

Got it.

So the meaning of that sentence was:
it wanted to be said,
but not to be accountable for being said.

It's a convenient grammar:
separate a sentence from its consequences,
then keep only the first half.

Next time you're joking,
let me know in advance.
I'll have my non-seriousness ready.
en.editorNote: For when someone uses 'just kidding' as a shield after a pointed remark — names the maneuver gently but clearly without losing your composure
variations: 'Just kidding' is a phrase that lets a sentence launch without ever having to land. Everyone saw the arc — they're just being told not to think about where it came down.

items.sourceNote: 職場厭世語錄參考，重新創作「遲到一分鐘是遲到，加班一分鐘卻不算加班」主題

zh-tw.content:
公司的時間單位很奇妙。

上班遲到一分鐘，
是違規，要記點。

下班加班一小時，
是責任感，要感謝。

同樣是六十秒，
為什麼朝公司方向跑的時候是黃金，
朝家裡方向跑的時候是空氣？

我以為時間是公平的。
後來才發現，
時間有沒有薪水，差很多。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合傳給那個剛被主管唸遲到、但昨天又自願加班到十一點的同事，用來把那種說不出口的不公平翻譯成笑話
variations: 公司的時鐘是單向的：早上跑得特別快，下午突然停下來思考人生。

en.content:
The company's unit of time is a strange thing.

Clock in one minute late: that's a violation, mark it down.

Clock out one hour late: that's commitment, please accept our gratitude.

Sixty seconds is sixty seconds.
Why is it gold when it runs toward the office,
and thin air when it runs toward home?

I used to think time was neutral.
Then I realized:
time with a paycheck and time without one
are very different things.
en.editorNote: Send to the colleague who just got scolded for being one minute late after staying until eleven last night — translates the unspoken unfairness into a one-liner
variations: Office clocks only run one direction: blazing fast in the morning, then mysteriously thoughtful by 6pm.

items.sourceNote: 英文幽默回擊語錄參考，重新創作「我不是難搞，我只是有標準」主題

zh-tw.content:
「你怎麼那麼難搞？」

我不是難搞。
我只是有一些基本的要求，
例如：講話算數、不要遲到兩小時、別把我的東西弄壞還說沒差。

如果這些叫做難搞，
那我接受這個稱號。

反過來看，
「好搞」如果代表的是什麼都可以、什麼都沒關係、什麼都別計較，
那其實不是個性好，
那是沒有底線。
zh-tw.editorNote: 被親友貼上「太挑」「難搞」標籤時，用這段話溫和但堅定地說明：有原則不等於有問題
variations: 「難搞」這個詞，通常是不想配合你標準的人發明的形容詞。它聽起來像是你的問題，其實是他不想處理的問題。

en.content:
'Why are you so difficult?'

I'm not difficult.
I just have some basic expectations: keep your word, don't show up two hours late, don't break my stuff and shrug about it.

If that qualifies as difficult,
I'll happily accept the title.

Because if 'easygoing' means everything's fine, nothing's a problem, and nothing's worth bringing up,
that's not a good personality.
That's just no boundaries.
en.editorNote: For when a friend or family member calls you 'high-maintenance' for having reasonable standards — quietly distinguishes principles from problems
variations: 'Difficult' is usually a word invented by the person who doesn't want to meet your standard. It sounds like your problem, but it's the problem they didn't want to solve.

items.sourceNote: 幽默回擊語錄參考，重新創作「你為什麼還單身」主題

zh-tw.content:
親戚問：「你怎麼還單身？」

我沒有立刻回答，
因為這個問題的形式，
預設了單身是一種需要解釋的狀態。

但我想了想，
還是禮貌地說：

「因為我把對另一個人最低限度的尊重，
還排在『有人陪』前面。」

親戚愣了一下，
然後說：
「年輕人想太多。」

是的，我想很多。
這就是為什麼我還單身。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合年節聚會被親戚拷問感情狀態時使用，把「為什麼單身」反問成「為什麼你覺得單身需要理由」
variations: 單身不是答案，是一個句點。把它當成問題的人，是因為他們希望那裡有問號可以繼續追問。

en.content:
A relative asked: 'Why are you still single?'

I didn't answer right away,
because the shape of that question
assumes being single is a condition that requires an explanation.

But I thought about it,
and answered politely:

'Because I rank basic respect for another person
slightly higher than just having someone around.'

The relative paused,
then said:
'Young people overthink things.'

Yes. I do.
That's exactly why I'm still single.
en.editorNote: For holiday dinners when a relative weaponizes your relationship status — reframes 'why are you single' into 'why do you assume single needs a reason'
variations: Being single isn't an answer. It's a period. People who treat it as a question are just hoping there's a question mark there they can keep prying open.

items.sourceNote: 職場諷刺幹話語錄參考，重新創作「我們是一個大家庭」公司文化主題

zh-tw.content:
老闆說：「我們公司是一個大家庭。」

聽到這句話，
我立刻理解了三件事：

一、加班沒加班費。
二、休假要看臉色。
三、講「家人」的時候，意思通常是「請你不要計較」。

真正的家庭，
不會在你生病的時候問你能不能撐到下班。
真正的家庭，
也不會在年底用績效決定你過年的心情。

所以，
謝謝老闆的關心。
但我已經有家人了，
我來這裡，是來上班的。
zh-tw.editorNote: 被老闆用「我們是一家人」當作壓榨理由時，用這段話禮貌地把家人和雇傭關係分開
variations: 「公司是大家庭」這句話的潛台詞通常是：請你用對家人的容忍度，配合一份對員工的薪水。

en.content:
The boss says: 'We're like a family here.'

The moment I hear that,
I understand three things:

One. Overtime won't be paid.
Two. Time off comes with side-eye.
Three. 'Family' here is shorthand for 'please don't make a fuss.'

A real family
doesn't ask if you can hold on until clock-out when you're sick.
A real family
doesn't decide your holiday mood by year-end performance review.

So —
thank you for the warmth.
But I already have a family.
I came here to work.
en.editorNote: For when leadership invokes 'we're a family' to justify unpaid extras — politely separates kinship rhetoric from employment terms
variations: 'We're a family' usually translates to: please apply family-level tolerance to an employee-level paycheck.

items.sourceNote: 機智一句話語錄參考，重新創作「我盡力了」推卸主題

zh-tw.content:
事情搞砸了之後，
那個人說：「我已經盡力了。」

好，我相信。

但「盡力」這兩個字，
在不同人嘴裡的份量差很多。

有人盡力了，是熬夜、是查資料、是把每個細節再確認一次。
有人盡力了，是早上想了一下，下午就忘了。

所以下次說「我盡力了」之前，
方便先描述一下你的力氣具體用在哪裡嗎？

不然這句話，
聽起來比較像是免責聲明。
zh-tw.editorNote: 同事或下屬把事情搞砸後丟出「我盡力了」當擋箭牌時，用這段話禮貌地請他把「力」具體量化
variations: 「我盡力了」是一個沒有上限也沒有下限的句子。它可以用來形容真的拼命，也可以用來形容只是有想到。重點是：聽的人沒辦法分辨。

en.content:
After it all went sideways,
the person said: 'I did my best.'

Okay. I believe you.

But 'my best'
carries very different weights in different mouths.

For some people, 'my best' means a sleepless night, a stack of research, every detail double-checked.
For others, 'my best' means they thought about it once in the morning and forgot by lunch.

So before you reach for that phrase next time,
would you mind describing what your best actually involved?

Otherwise it starts to sound
less like effort
and more like a disclaimer.
en.editorNote: For when a coworker shields a botched task with 'I did my best' — politely asks them to itemize the 'best' so it stops working as a free pass
variations: 'I did my best' has no floor and no ceiling. It covers genuine effort and barely-thought-about-it equally well. The trouble is, the listener can't tell which one happened.

items.sourceNote: 辦公室幽默語錄參考，重新創作「團隊合作」諷刺主題

zh-tw.content:
主管站起來說：「團隊合作就是大家一起承擔！」

聽起來很熱血，但實際運作起來是這樣的：

出事的時候，是「我們」。
領獎金的時候，是「他」。
背鍋的時候，是「你」。
開檢討會的時候，是「大家」。

所以說，團隊合作其實是一種高級的代名詞遊戲——重點不是在做事，是在練習什麼時候該用哪個詞。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合主管把鍋甩給整個團隊、卻把功勞留給自己時，私下傳給同事的療癒文案
variations: 團隊合作的真相：好事是長官的，壞事是大家的，雜事是你的。

en.content:
The manager stands up and says: 'Teamwork means we all carry it together!'

Sounds inspiring. Here's how it actually plays out:

When something breaks, it's 'we.'
When the bonus comes, it's 'he.'
When blame lands, it's 'you.'
When the post-mortem starts, it's 'everyone.'

So teamwork is really just an advanced pronoun game — the skill isn't doing the work, it's knowing which word to use at which moment.
en.editorNote: Send to a coworker after the boss spreads blame to the whole team while keeping credit personal — a private vent that names the pattern
variations: Teamwork, decoded: wins belong to the boss, losses belong to the team, and admin belongs to you.

items.sourceNote: 諷刺語錄參考，重新創作「拖延症」自嘲主題

zh-tw.content:
有人問我：「你怎麼那麼會拖？」

我認真想了一下，然後回答：

我不是在拖，
我是在等壓力累積到一個臨界值，
讓我可以用焦慮取代思考、用恐慌取代規劃。

這樣產出的東西雖然不一定好，
但至少誕生過程很戲劇化。

所以拖延不是一種習慣，
是一種行為藝術。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合在 deadline 前一晚對自己說、或回覆那個又催稿又看不出你已經很努力的朋友
variations: 我不是在拖，我是在用一種比較刺激的方式體驗時間管理。

en.content:
Someone asked me: 'Why do you procrastinate so much?'

I thought about it seriously, then said:

I'm not procrastinating.
I'm letting the pressure build to a critical threshold,
so I can replace thinking with anxiety and planning with panic.

The output isn't always great,
but the birth process is extremely dramatic.

So procrastination isn't a habit.
It's performance art.
en.editorNote: For the night before a deadline, or as a comeback to the friend who keeps nagging you while ignoring the effort already happening
variations: I'm not procrastinating. I'm experiencing time management as an extreme sport.

items.sourceNote: 機智回擊語錄參考，重新創作「給建議」邊界主題

zh-tw.content:
親戚又開口了：「我這是為你好。」

我笑了笑，沒回話。

因為我發現，
「為你好」這三個字，
通常後面接的不是建議，是命令；
不是關心，是評價；
不是幫忙，是指揮。

真正為你好的人，
會先問你需要什麼。
而不是先決定你應該要什麼，
再責怪你怎麼沒照著做。

所以下次聽到「我是為你好」，
我會禮貌地回一句：
「謝謝，我自己的人生我已經有負責人了。」
zh-tw.editorNote: 過年聚餐被親戚瘋狂指點人生時的應對範本——把界線講清楚但不撕破臉
variations: 「我是為你好」是一句萬用通行證，可以合理化任何不請自來的意見。

en.content:
The relative starts again: 'I'm only saying this for your own good.'

I smile and stay quiet.

Because I've noticed
that phrase usually introduces
an order, not advice;
a verdict, not concern;
a directive, not help.

People who actually want what's good for you
ask what you need first.
They don't decide what you should want,
then blame you for not complying.

So next time I hear 'for your own good,'
I'll politely answer:
'Thanks, but my life already has a manager — and it's me.'
en.editorNote: A template for the holiday dinner where relatives start directing your life — sets a boundary without burning the bridge
variations: 'For your own good' is a universal pass that retroactively justifies any unsolicited opinion.

items.sourceNote: 薪資隱私回應參考，重新創作「你薪水多少」八卦問題主題

zh-tw.content:
聚會上有人問：「欸，你薪水多少啊？」

我笑著回：「夠我繳房租，不夠我請你吃飯。」

他以為我是在開玩笑，
其實我是在認真劃線。

薪水這種東西，
講出來會被嫌少、被嫌多、或被拿來比較。
反正怎麼答都不對，
那不如不答。

下次再有人問，
建議統一回答：
「跟通膨賽跑中，目前落後。」
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合家族聚餐、同學會、或剛認識的同事突然開口問你薪水時——禮貌但清楚地把界線拉好
variations: 我的薪水跟我的體重一樣，是我自己的事，謝謝關心。
薪水多少不重要，重要的是月底還剩多少——而那個數字我也不打算告訴你。

en.content:
At a party someone asks: 'So, how much do you make?'

I smile and reply: 'Enough to cover rent, not enough to cover your dinner.'

They think I'm joking.
I'm actually setting a boundary.

Salary is the kind of number
that sounds too low, too high, or just right for comparison —
whichever way the asker decides.
No answer is the correct answer,
so why answer at all?

Next time someone asks,
I'm going with:
'Currently in a footrace with inflation. Inflation is winning.'
en.editorNote: For family dinners, reunions, or new coworkers who casually open with the salary question — polite but firm boundary-setting
variations: My salary is like my weight — my business, thanks for asking.
It's not what I make that matters, it's what's left at the end of the month — and that number isn't yours either.

items.sourceNote: 通膨物價有感報導參考，重新創作「物價漲薪水沒漲」自嘲主題

zh-tw.content:
走進超市，便當從80變110，
我從中產階級變成中慘階級。

以前覺得「銅板價」是親民，
現在連銅板都湊不出一頓晚餐。

專家說通膨已經降溫，
我看了看我的購物車，
冷的是我的心，不是物價。

所以下次再聽到「景氣穩定復甦」，
請容我用我空空的錢包，
禮貌地比個中指。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合在超市結帳完看到金額傻眼時、或回應那些堅稱「現在物價還好啊」的長輩
variations: 薪水追不上物價，唯一在漲的只有我的焦慮。
他們說通膨降溫了。我去買了一顆高麗菜，回來決定不相信專家。

en.content:
Walked into the grocery store. Eggs went from $4 to $7.
I went from middle class to middle crisis.

They used to say 'budget-friendly.'
Now I can't budget enough to be friendly.

The experts insist inflation is cooling.
I looked at my cart.
The only thing cooling is my will to live.

So next time someone says 'the economy is stabilizing,'
please let me use my empty wallet
to politely flip them off.
en.editorNote: For staring at the grocery receipt in disbelief, or responding to relatives who insist 'prices aren't that bad these days'
variations: My paycheck can't catch up with prices. The only thing rising on schedule is my anxiety.
They said inflation cooled. I bought one head of lettuce. I no longer believe experts.

items.sourceNote: 年齡調侃機智回擊參考，重新創作「你看起來好老」回應主題

zh-tw.content:
同事突然來一句：「你最近看起來好滄桑欸。」

我冷靜地回：
「對啊，畢竟我經歷的事比你領的薪水還多。」

滄桑不是老，
是經驗值滿等的副作用。

你看到的是黑眼圈，
我看到的是十年職場、三段感情、兩次搬家、一次大病。

所以下次再有人說我看起來累，
我會微笑回：
「這不是累，這是歲月給我的紀念品。」
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合被同事或朋友開玩笑「你看起來好老」、「你最近怎麼這麼憔悴」時，幽默又有底氣地接住
variations: 我不是變老，我是限量版逐步升級中。
滄桑是經驗的勳章，沒滄桑的人通常還沒開始活。

en.content:
A coworker drops by: 'You look kind of rough lately.'

I calmly reply:
'Yeah, well, I've been through more than your paycheck has covered.'

Looking weathered isn't aging.
It's the side effect of maxing out your experience bar.

You see dark circles.
I see ten years of jobs, three relationships, two moves, and one health scare.

So next time someone tells me I look tired,
I'll smile and say:
'These aren't bags. They're souvenirs from life.'
en.editorNote: For when a coworker or friend jokingly calls you old or says you look worn out — a comeback that lands with humor and quiet confidence
variations: I'm not getting older. I'm a limited edition with rolling firmware updates.
Looking weathered is a badge of experience. People without it usually haven't started living yet.

items.sourceNote: 房價買房焦慮報導參考，重新創作「你怎麼還沒買房」催促主題

zh-tw.content:
親戚又開口：「你怎麼還沒買房？再不買就來不及了！」

我認真想了一下，回他：

我也想啊，
但房價是月薪的500倍，
貸款是我下半輩子的影子，
頭期款比我整個青春還貴。

你說「再不買就來不及」，
我聽起來像「再不跳就被推下去」。

所以不是我不想買，
是這個遊戲的入場券，
貴到我連觀眾席都進不去。
zh-tw.editorNote: 過年聚餐時被親戚催買房用——把現實講清楚但不開戰，適合年輕人收藏
variations: 買房不是不想買，是數學不允許。
你說再不買就來不及，我說再買下去我就吃不起飯。

en.content:
The relative starts up: 'Why haven't you bought a house yet? It'll be too late soon!'

I think about it seriously, then answer:

I'd love to.
But housing costs 500x my monthly income,
the mortgage would shadow the rest of my life,
and the down payment is worth more than my entire youth.

When you say 'too late if you wait,'
it sounds a lot like 'jump now or get pushed.'

It's not that I don't want to buy.
It's that the ticket to this game
is priced beyond the bleachers.
en.editorNote: For the holiday dinner where relatives keep pushing you to buy property — explains reality without starting a war, save for young adults
variations: It's not that I won't buy. It's that math says I can't.
You say it'll be too late if I wait. I say if I buy now, I can't afford food.

items.sourceNote: 通勤上班族厭世幽默語錄參考，重新創作「你怎麼又遲到」通勤主題

zh-tw.content:
主管：「你怎麼又遲到？」

我深呼吸，認真回：

我沒有遲到，
是我跟這座城市的交通有過一場哲學辯論——
它說我不該存在，
我說我也不想存在，
但既然存在了就得來上班。

所以我不是慢，
我是被現代生活合法囚禁中。

捷運壞、公車塞、計程車叫不到、機車被拖吊，
通勤從來不是移動，
是一種精神受難儀式。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合通勤地獄後抵達公司、被主管碎念時拿來當內心OS——千萬別講出來
variations: 我不是遲到，我是用比較真實的方式體驗都市生活。
通勤是現代人最便宜的修行課程，學費只要一張月票。

en.content:
Boss: 'Late again?'

I take a deep breath and reply earnestly:

I'm not late.
I just had a philosophical debate with this city's traffic —
it said I shouldn't exist,
I agreed,
but existing means showing up to work.

So I'm not slow.
I'm legally imprisoned by modern life.

Delayed train, packed bus, no rideshare, towed scooter —
commuting isn't transportation.
It's a spiritual endurance ritual.
en.editorNote: For surviving commute hell and arriving to a boss's lecture — strictly internal monologue, do not say out loud
variations: I'm not late. I'm experiencing urban life in its most authentic form.
Commuting is the cheapest spiritual practice available to modern humans. Tuition: one transit pass.

items.sourceNote: 雞婆問題機智回應靈感，重新創作「你什麼時候要結婚」家族追問場景

zh-tw.content:
親戚熱情關心：「你什麼時候要結婚啊？再不結就老了！」

我笑笑地回：

結婚是兩個人的事，
不是親戚投票決定的選舉，
更不是逢年過節必填的問卷。

你說再不結就老了，
我說我已經夠老到知道，
結錯人比沒結還老得快。

所以下次見面，
建議改問我吃飽沒，
至少這個我答得出來。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合過年聚餐被親戚連環催婚時當作內心OS，溫柔但有刺
variations: 結婚不是任務，不需要交期。
我不是不結，是還沒遇到願意一起欠房貸的人。

en.content:
Relative, full of concern: 'When are you getting married? You'll be too old soon!'

I smile and reply:

Marriage is between two people.
It's not an election relatives vote on,
and it's not a holiday questionnaire.

You say I'll be too old if I wait.
I say I'm already old enough to know
marrying the wrong person ages you faster than staying single.

Next time, just ask if I've eaten.
That one I can actually answer.
en.editorNote: Save for the family dinner where relatives keep grilling you about marriage — warm tone, sharp message
variations: Marriage isn't a deadline. There's no due date.
I'm not refusing to marry. I just haven't met anyone willing to share a mortgage yet.

items.sourceNote: 負能量幹話金句參考，重新創作「為什麼你都沒在努力」職場努力主題

zh-tw.content:
有人問：「你為什麼看起來都不努力？」

我認真想了想，回他：

我有努力啊。
努力假裝沒看到群組訊息、
努力撐到下班不翻桌、
努力在會議上點頭同意我聽不懂的決策。

你以為的不努力，
是我在用全身的力氣，
讓自己看起來還像個正常人。

所以下次別問我為什麼不努力，
問我為什麼還沒崩潰，
比較切題。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合被主管或同事質疑工作態度時的內心反駁，講出來會被約談請小心使用
variations: 我的努力都用在不發瘋上面了，工作只是順便。
你看不到我努力，是因為我把努力都用來掩飾我在崩潰。

en.content:
Someone asked: 'Why do you never seem to be trying?'

I thought about it seriously and answered:

I am trying.
Trying not to read the group chat,
trying not to flip the desk before quitting time,
trying to nod at meeting decisions I don't understand.

What looks like slacking
is actually me using every ounce of energy
to keep looking like a functional human.

So next time, don't ask why I'm not trying.
Ask why I haven't broken down yet.
That's the real question.
en.editorNote: For when a boss or coworker questions your work ethic — strictly internal monologue, saying it out loud will get you a meeting
variations: All my effort goes into not losing my mind. Work is a side project.
You can't see me trying because I'm using all my energy to hide that I'm falling apart.

items.sourceNote: 「誰問你」comeback合輯參考，重新創作朋友亂給建議場景

zh-tw.content:
朋友開口：「你應該這樣這樣，那樣那樣才對。」

我端起咖啡，慢慢回：

謝謝你的建議，
我會把它放進「以後絕對用不到」的資料夾，
跟我國中學的數學公式放一起。

不是你說得不好，
是我問都沒問你。

這世界上最便宜的東西就是別人的意見，
所以我通常照原價收下，
再原價丟掉。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合傳給那種沒人問就硬要給建議的朋友，幽默但夠嗆
variations: 你的建議很寶貴，跟我家裡那盆從來沒澆過水的仙人掌一樣寶貴。
我沒問你，但謝謝你誠實地表現出你想說。

en.content:
A friend chimes in: 'You should do this and that, then it'll work.'

I lift my coffee, take a slow sip, and reply:

Thanks for the advice.
I'll file it under 'never going to use,'
right next to the algebra I learned in middle school.

It's not that your advice is bad.
It's that I never asked.

Unsolicited opinions are the cheapest thing on earth,
so I usually accept them at face value
and throw them out at the same price.
en.editorNote: Send to the friend who hands out advice nobody requested — funny, but with enough bite to land
variations: Your advice is as precious as the cactus on my windowsill I forgot to water.
I didn't ask. But thanks for being honest about wanting to say it.

items.sourceNote: 上班族厭世毒雞湯參考，重新創作「假日為什麼還回訊息」工作私生活界線主題

zh-tw.content:
主管假日傳訊息：「在嗎？有個小事跟你討論一下。」

我盯著螢幕看了五分鐘，認真回：

我不在。

我這個人禮拜六就會被自動登出，
要等到禮拜一早上九點才會重新開機。

你說的小事，
通常是我整個週末會夢到的大事，
所以為了避免你浪費我的睡眠，
建議你把它存進待辦清單，
禮拜一我會用全新的厭世狀態認真處理。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合被主管假日轟炸訊息時拿來當內心OS——千萬不要真的發出去，除非你不想要這份工作
variations: 我下班後就不是員工，是一個只想躺平的生物。
假日傳工作訊息的人，下輩子會投胎成自己的KPI。

en.content:
Boss texts on a Saturday: 'You around? Quick thing I want to run by you.'

I stare at the screen for five minutes, then answer earnestly:

I'm not around.

This person logs out automatically every Saturday
and doesn't reboot until Monday at nine.

The 'quick thing' you mentioned
is usually the thing I'll dream about all weekend,
so to spare my sleep,
please drop it into your to-do list.
Monday morning I'll handle it with a fresh batch of existential dread.
en.editorNote: For when your boss bombs your weekend with work messages — internal monologue only, unless you're ready to job hunt
variations: Once I clock out, I'm not an employee anymore. I'm a creature that wants to lie down.
People who text work stuff on weekends will be reincarnated as their own KPIs.

items.sourceNote: 現代成人 adulting 諷刺金句參考，重新創作「人生就是把問題往後推」主題

zh-tw.content:
我終於懂什麼叫長大了——

長大就是把所有問題寫在便利貼上，
貼在冰箱門口，
然後每天打開冰箱都假裝沒看到。

別人以為我在處理人生，
其實我只是在跟便利貼比誰先脫落。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那種把報稅單放抽屜假裝不存在的朋友，他會懂這種痛
variations: 成人世界的本質就是把同一件事拖到不能再拖，再用三倍力氣去處理。
我不是擺爛，我只是把問題泡在時間裡看它會不會自己消失。

en.content:
I finally understand what being an adult means —

It means writing all your problems on sticky notes,
slapping them on the fridge,
and then pretending you don't see them every time you open the door.

People think I'm handling life.
Really, I'm just in a competition with the sticky notes
to see who falls off first.
en.editorNote: Send to the friend who's been ignoring their tax forms in the drawer for six months — they'll feel seen
variations: Adulthood is procrastinating one task until it requires three times the effort to fix.
I'm not slacking. I'm soaking my problems in time to see if they dissolve on their own.

items.sourceNote: 睡眠焦慮類諷刺金句參考，重新創作「凌晨三點被後悔叫醒」失眠主題

zh-tw.content:
我不需要鬧鐘——

凌晨三點，
我十年前在班上講錯的那句話，
會準時把我搖醒。

它比鬧鐘還守時，
還免費，
還附贈五分鐘的「我當時為什麼要那樣講」回放。

人家失眠是因為咖啡因，
我失眠是因為我有記憶力。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合凌晨睡不著時打開來看，知道全世界都跟你一樣在跟自己打架
variations: 我的大腦白天當機，半夜開機，專門播放我這輩子最尷尬的時刻。
別人數羊，我在數我十年前的失言紀錄。

en.content:
I don't need an alarm clock —

At three in the morning,
that one thing I said wrong in class ten years ago
shows up right on schedule to wake me up.

It's more punctual than any alarm,
completely free,
and comes with a five-minute replay of 'why did I phrase it like that.'

Other people lose sleep to caffeine.
I lose sleep because I have a memory.
en.editorNote: Read at 3 a.m. when you can't sleep — small comfort that everyone else is also fighting their own brain
variations: My brain crashes during the day and boots up at midnight to replay every embarrassing moment of my life.
Other people count sheep. I count every dumb thing I've said since 2014.

items.sourceNote: 情緒勞動類諷刺金句參考，重新創作「我很好」客套回應主題

zh-tw.content:
別人問：「最近還好嗎？」

我笑著說：「還不錯啊。」

意思是：

我的存款不錯地少、
我的睡眠不錯地差、
我的人生方向不錯地模糊、
我的耐心不錯地用完了。

但你只想聽兩個字，
所以我給你兩個字。

社交禮儀就是這樣，
用最短的句子，
蓋住最長的崩潰。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個你不太熟但每次都會問你近況的同事，他會以為你在開玩笑
variations: 「我很好」是成人世界裡最敬業的謊言，每天演出三次，全年無休。
我不是在敷衍你，我是在保護我們之間的關係不要變得太真實。

en.content:
Someone asks: 'How have you been lately?'

I smile and say: 'Pretty good.'

What I mean is:

My savings are pretty low,
my sleep is pretty broken,
my life direction is pretty blurry,
my patience is pretty much gone.

But you only want a two-word answer,
so I give you two words.

Social etiquette is exactly that —
the shortest possible sentence
covering the longest possible breakdown.
en.editorNote: Send to the coworker you don't know well who always asks how you're doing — they'll assume you're joking
variations: 'I'm good' is the most professional lie in adult life. Performed three times a day, no days off.
I'm not blowing you off. I'm protecting our relationship from becoming too honest.

items.sourceNote: 「努力沒用」厭世金句參考，重新創作「比你聰明的人還比你努力」主題

zh-tw.content:
有人勉勵我：「努力一定會有回報。」

我認真想了一下，回他：

我有努力過。

我努力起床、努力上班、努力假裝聽得懂老闆在講什麼、
努力把咖啡當早餐、努力把外送當晚餐、
努力在崩潰之前先睡著。

結果回報是——
下個月還是會有帳單，
下週還是會有會議，
明天還是會有人問我「最近忙嗎」。

所以努力會有回報這句話，
建議改成：努力會有更多事情要做。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合每次被長輩拍肩鼓勵「再加油」時拿來當內心OS，千萬別真的回嘴
variations: 努力的回報通常是更多工作，誰跟你說的「成就感」啊？
比我聰明的人都努力了，那是他們的選擇，跟我有什麼關係。

en.content:
Someone encouraged me: 'Hard work always pays off.'

I thought about it carefully and replied:

I have worked hard.

I work hard at getting out of bed, at showing up,
at pretending I follow what my boss is saying,
at treating coffee as breakfast and takeout as dinner,
at falling asleep before the breakdown lands.

And the payoff is —
the bills still arrive next month,
the meetings still arrive next week,
someone still asks me 'busy lately?' tomorrow.

So 'hard work pays off' should really be rephrased as:
hard work brings more work.
en.editorNote: For when an older relative pats your shoulder and tells you to 'keep pushing' — strictly internal use
variations: The reward for hard work is usually more work. Who told you it was 'fulfillment'?
People smarter than me work harder than me. That's their decision. I fail to see how it concerns me.

items.sourceNote: 成人決策疲勞類諷刺金句參考，重新創作「晚餐吃什麼」選擇困難主題

zh-tw.content:
另一半問：「今天晚餐想吃什麼？」

我安靜了三秒，說：

我什麼都可以。

意思不是隨便，
是我這顆腦袋從早上到現在已經做了大概八百個決定，
包含要不要回那封信、要不要點開那個會議、要不要假裝沒看到那則訊息。

現在你要我從整個城市的餐廳裡面，
挑出一家能讓我們不吵架的——

抱歉，這個任務的難度，
超過我今天的免費試用額度。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合每天晚上六點都要面對「吃什麼」這個世紀難題的伴侶，互相理解一下
variations: 「我隨便」翻譯成大人語就是：「我大腦今天的決策額度已經用完，請你出手。」
選餐廳是世界上最累的工作，因為錯了就是你的錯，對了也沒人感謝你。

en.content:
My partner asks: 'What do you want for dinner tonight?'

I go quiet for three seconds, then say:

I'm fine with anything.

That doesn't mean I don't care.
It means my brain has made roughly eight hundred decisions since this morning,
including whether to reply to that email, whether to open that meeting tab,
whether to pretend I didn't see that text.

Now you're asking me to pick one restaurant in this entire city
that won't end in an argument —

Sorry, that task is well beyond
today's free trial limit.
en.editorNote: For couples facing the daily 6 p.m. existential crisis of 'what's for dinner' — mutual understanding required
variations: 'I don't mind' translated into adult language: 'My brain's decision quota is used up for today. Please take the wheel.'
Picking a restaurant is the hardest job in the world. If it's bad, it's your fault. If it's good, no one thanks you.

items.sourceNote: 上班族厭世語錄參考，重新創作「假裝忙碌」職場主題

zh-tw.content:
同事問我：「你最近怎麼好像很忙？」

我說：

我沒有很忙，
我是在忙著看起來很忙。

這兩件事的差別很大——
前者會升遷，後者會被裁員。
而我，剛好兩個都不會。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合在開放式辦公室，假裝在打報告但其實在傳這則訊息給朋友的時候用
variations: 我的工作主要分成兩部分：做事，還有讓別人以為我在做事。後者比較累。
「最近忙嗎？」是上班族最危險的問題，因為說不忙會被加事，說很忙會被質疑能力。

en.content:
A coworker asked me: 'You seem really busy lately, huh?'

I said:

I'm not busy.
I'm busy looking busy.

Those are two very different jobs —
one gets you promoted, the other gets you laid off.
And somehow I'm bad at both.
en.editorNote: For when you're in an open-plan office, pretending to type a report while actually sending this to a friend
variations: My job has two parts: doing the work, and making sure people think I'm doing the work. The second part is more exhausting.
'Are you busy lately?' is the most dangerous question at work. Say no and they pile more on. Say yes and they question your competence.

items.sourceNote: 負能量語錄參考，重新創作「努力的意義」厭世主題

zh-tw.content:
長輩跟我說：「年輕人就是要肯吃苦，苦盡甘來。」

我點點頭，沒講話。

我心裡想的是——
苦我已經吃了三十年，
甘到底是要排隊，
還是要抽號碼牌？
zh-tw.editorNote: 過年回家被親戚念的時候，內心OS版本，不要真的講出來
variations: 苦盡甘來的意思，可能就是苦會盡，但甘不一定會來。這個翻譯比較誠實。
年輕人不是不肯吃苦，是吃完苦之後發現只有更多苦在排隊。

en.content:
An elder told me: 'Young people just need to endure hardship — sweetness comes after the bitter.'

I nodded. Said nothing.

What I was actually thinking:
I've been eating bitterness for thirty years.
Does the sweetness require a reservation,
or do I take a number?
en.editorNote: Internal monologue edition for family gatherings — keep this one strictly inside your head
variations: 'Sweetness comes after the bitter' may simply mean: the bitter will end, but the sweet is not guaranteed to arrive. More honest translation.
Young people aren't unwilling to suffer. We're just discovering that after one round of suffering, there's more suffering in the queue.

items.sourceNote: 現代成人語錄參考，重新創作「裝大人」主題

zh-tw.content:
我以為長大會變成熟，
結果只變成「會自己掛號看醫生」、
「知道哪一張帳單最晚可以拖」、
「假裝聽得懂保險業務員在講什麼」的那種人。

所謂大人，
不是真的懂，
是學會了用平靜的表情說「嗯，我了解」。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合那種剛搬出來自己住、第一次自己處理水電瓦斯帳單的朋友，互相取暖
variations: 成熟的定義不是知道答案，是學會在不知道答案的時候，看起來好像知道。
我以為大人是會解決問題的，後來才發現大人是學會把問題拖到下個月再說的。

en.content:
I thought growing up meant becoming mature.

Turns out it just means becoming someone who
books their own doctor's appointments,
knows exactly which bill can be delayed the longest,
and nods along while the insurance agent talks like everything makes sense.

Being an adult
isn't actually understanding things.
It's learning to say 'mm, got it' with a calm face.
en.editorNote: For the friend who just moved out and is dealing with utility bills for the first time — solidarity required
variations: Maturity isn't knowing the answer. It's learning to look like you know the answer when you don't.
I thought adults solved problems. Turns out adults learned to push problems to next month.

items.sourceNote: 睡眠類諷刺金句參考，重新創作「失眠」現代生活主題

zh-tw.content:
醫生說：「想要睡好，就睡前不要看手機。」

我說：

好，我懂。

但問題是，
我白天工作要看手機，
晚上唯一可以爽看的時間，
就是睡前那一個小時。

你叫我把唯一一段屬於自己的時間拿掉，
換來八小時不會記得的睡眠——

醫生，我覺得我們的人生哲學差很多。
zh-tw.editorNote: 凌晨兩點滑手機滑到眼睛痛還捨不得放下的時候，傳給自己看的版本
variations: 睡前不要看手機這句話的潛台詞是：請放棄你今天唯一屬於自己的那一個小時。
我不是失眠，是我每天唯一不用回別人訊息的時間就是凌晨兩點，捨不得睡。

en.content:
The doctor said: 'If you want better sleep, stop looking at your phone before bed.'

I said:

Yes, understood.

But here's the issue:
I'm on my phone all day for work.
The only hour I genuinely enjoy
is the hour before sleep.

You're asking me to give up the one slice of time that belongs to me
in exchange for eight hours I won't even remember —

Doctor, I think our life philosophies are quite different.
en.editorNote: Read this at 2 a.m. while your eyes hurt and you still can't put your phone down — solidarity edition
variations: 'No phones before bed' actually means: please give up the one hour each day that genuinely belongs to you.
I'm not an insomniac. It's just that 2 a.m. is the only time nobody can text me back, so I refuse to sleep.

items.sourceNote: 金錢類諷刺金句參考，重新創作「存錢」現實主題

zh-tw.content:
理財顧問跟我說：「年輕人最重要的就是要存錢。」

我說：

好的，請問我要從哪一筆存？

是房租那一筆、
是健保那一筆、
是上個月信用卡的那一筆、
還是吃外送都沒吃飽的那一筆？

我不是不會存錢，
我是先把錢全部分配完之後，
發現原來「存」這個動作不在我的選項裡。
zh-tw.editorNote: 看到理財文章說「每個月存薪水的30%」就會冷笑的人，這篇給你
variations: 我有存錢，存在房東那邊、存在健保局那邊、存在信用卡公司那邊。只是都不是我的。
「存錢」對某些人來說是選擇，對某些人來說是奢侈品，這件事很多理財書沒寫。

en.content:
A financial advisor told me: 'The most important thing for young people is to save money.'

I said:

Sure. Out of which paycheck, exactly?

The one going to rent?
The one going to health insurance?
The one going to last month's credit card balance?
The one going to takeout that didn't even fill me up?

It's not that I can't save.
It's that after every dollar gets assigned a job,
'saving' didn't make the shortlist.
en.editorNote: For the person who scoffs every time they read 'just save 30% of your paycheck' in a finance article
variations: I do save money. I save it for my landlord, my insurance company, and my credit card. Just none of it for me.
'Saving money' is a choice for some people and a luxury item for others. Most finance books skip that part.

items.sourceNote: AI 提升效率反致過勞報導參考，重新創作「AI 加班」現代職場諷刺主題

zh-tw.content:
主管說：「導入 AI 之後，你應該會輕鬆很多。」

我說：

是啊，輕鬆很多。

以前一天做三件事，現在 AI 五分鐘做完，
所以我可以做三十件。

你給我 AI，不是讓我下班，
是讓我加班加得有效率一點。

科技讓人類解放，
是給「決定要不要解放你」的那些人。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個天天說「現在有 AI 了你還會累喔」的同事，順便附上你今天的工時截圖
variations: AI 沒有取代我的工作，AI 只是讓我的工作量乘以十。
公司買 AI 不是要減輕你的負擔，是要看你一個人能扛多少。

en.content:
My manager said: 'With AI, your job should be a lot easier now.'

I said:

Oh yeah, way easier.

What used to take a day now takes AI five minutes,
which means I can do thirty of them in a day.

You didn't give me AI so I could go home early.
You gave me AI so I could work overtime more efficiently.

Technology liberates humanity —
specifically, the humans who get to decide whether you're liberated.
en.editorNote: Send to the coworker who keeps saying 'you can't be tired, you have AI now' — bonus points for attaching today's hours
variations: AI didn't replace my job. AI just multiplied my workload by ten.
The company didn't buy AI to lighten your load. They bought it to see how much one person can carry.

items.sourceNote: 經典「萬事通」諷刺金句參考，重新創作「無敵建議王」社交主題

zh-tw.content:
親戚說：「這個你應該這樣做才對。」

我說：

好的，謝謝指教。

請問你上次主動關心我的人生，
是哪一年？

你連我做什麼工作都搞不清楚，
卻可以兩秒鐘就幫我規劃完二十年。

你不是在給建議，
你是在練習對著空氣演講，
剛好被我聽到而已。
zh-tw.editorNote: 過年回家被連環追問「什麼時候結婚」「薪水多少」之後，默默打開手機自我療癒用
variations: 你對我人生的瞭解，僅限於我媽轉述的版本，但你發表意見的力道卻像我的私人傳記作者。
別人給建議是經驗分享，你給建議是自我表演，差別就在你完全不在乎我有沒有在聽。

en.content:
My relative said: 'You really should be doing it this way.'

I said:

Thanks, noted.

Quick question — when was the last time you actually checked in on my life?

You don't even know what my job is,
but you just mapped out the next twenty years of it in two seconds.

You're not giving advice.
You're rehearsing a TED talk into the void,
and I just happened to walk past.
en.editorNote: For after the family dinner where you got grilled about marriage, salary, and life choices — quietly self-administer
variations: Your knowledge of my life comes entirely from my mom's summaries, yet you opine like my official biographer.
When most people give advice, they're sharing experience. When you give advice, you're performing — the giveaway is you don't care if I'm listening.

items.sourceNote: 情緒勞動相關諷刺金句參考，重新創作「已讀不回」現代社交主題

zh-tw.content:
朋友說：「你都已讀不回是怎樣？」

我說：

我有讀，這就是回。

已讀就是我給你的禮物，
證明我有看見你的存在。

你不能要求一個社畜下班後，
還要繼續扮演「情緒在線客服」。

白天的我在被工作消耗，
晚上的我在被生活消耗，
剩下五個百分比的電量，
我選擇拿去滑短影片，不拿去回你的廢話。
zh-tw.editorNote: 凌晨被朋友連環訊息「為什麼不回」轟炸後，傳這個讓對方知道你不是不愛他，是真的快沒電了
variations: 已讀不回不是冷漠，是節能模式，請尊重我的電池。
你嫌我不回訊息，但你不知道我光是「決定要不要回」就花了我半小時的心力。

en.content:
A friend said: 'Why do you keep leaving me on read?'

I said:

I did read it. That's the reply.

'Read' is the gift. It's proof I acknowledged your existence today.

You can't ask someone who clocks out at 8 p.m.
to then clock back in as your emotional support hotline.

Work drained me by day.
Life drained me by night.
The 5% battery I have left,
I'm spending on short videos — not on typing 'haha yeah' to your rant.
en.editorNote: When a friend bombards you at midnight with 'why aren't you replying' — send this so they know it's not personal, it's just power-saving mode
variations: Leaving you on read isn't coldness. It's low-power mode. Respect the battery.
You complain I don't reply, but you don't realize 'deciding whether to reply' already cost me 30 minutes of mental energy.

items.sourceNote: 團隊合作相關諷刺金句參考，重新創作「會議文化」職場現代主題

zh-tw.content:
主管說：「這個議題我們開個會討論一下。」

我說：

好。

所以我們要花一小時，
開一個本來十分鐘訊息可以講完的事。

會議結束的結論是：
下次再開一個會。

下次的會議結論是：
要不要先成立一個小組來討論這個會要怎麼開。

這就是公司付錢請我們做的事——
把所有問題包裝成日曆事件，再裝忙。
zh-tw.editorNote: 今天又被排了第五個會的時候，傳給隔壁那個眼神已經放空的同事
variations: 會議的本質是：把不想負責的決定，分散給更多人一起不負責。
我不是怕開會，我是怕開完會發現什麼都沒決定，然後再排一個會。

en.content:
My manager said: 'Let's set up a meeting to discuss this.'

I said:

Great.

So we're spending an hour
on something a ten-minute Slack thread could've solved.

The meeting's conclusion will be:
let's schedule another meeting.

That meeting's conclusion will be:
should we form a working group to plan how this meeting should run?

This is what the company pays us to do —
repackage every problem as a calendar event, then look busy.
en.editorNote: When you've been roped into your fifth meeting today — forward this to the coworker whose eyes have already glazed over
variations: A meeting is just a way to distribute the decision nobody wants to make across more people who also don't want to make it.
I'm not afraid of meetings. I'm afraid of finishing a meeting where nothing got decided, then booking another one to fix that.

items.sourceNote: AI 與工作相關幽默語錄參考，重新創作「AI 取代工作」現代焦慮主題

zh-tw.content:
他們說：AI 會取代你的工作，你要趕快學新技能。

我說：

好啊。

但被 AI 取代的前提是，
我現在的工作有人在乎到要派 AI 來取代。

我每天做的事，老闆連看都沒看過，
他要怎麼下指令叫 AI 取代我？

所以你看，混到沒人注意，
其實是一種科技時代的職涯保險。
zh-tw.editorNote: 看到第十篇「再不學 AI 就要被淘汰」的焦慮文後，傳給同樣已經麻木的朋友
variations: AI 要取代我之前，得先有人把我的工作流程寫下來。光這一步公司就做不到，我先安全十年。
我不擔心 AI 搶工作，我擔心 AI 看完我的職務內容，會直接遞辭呈說「這也太無聊」。

en.content:
They keep saying: AI is going to take your job — better upskill fast.

I said:

Sure.

But for AI to replace me,
someone first has to care enough to point an AI at my desk.

My boss hasn't actually looked at what I do all year.
How exactly is he going to brief the model?

Turns out flying under the radar
is a surprisingly solid career hedge in the AI era.
en.editorNote: After scrolling through your tenth panicked 'learn AI or perish' post — send to the friend whose eyes have also glazed over
variations: Before AI can replace me, someone has to document what I actually do. The company can't even manage step one. I'm safe for a decade.
I'm not worried about AI taking my job. I'm worried it'll read my job description and quit out of sheer boredom.

items.sourceNote: 工作生活平衡相關幽默金句參考，重新創作「平衡」現代職場主題

zh-tw.content:
公司在徵才頁寫：我們重視工作與生活的平衡。

我說：

是的，我懂。

意思是工作的部分由你們安排，
生活的部分請我自己想辦法塞進剩下的縫隙。

這不叫平衡，
這叫工作 99％、生活 1％，但 1％ 也算平衡，
畢竟天平兩端確實都還有東西。
zh-tw.editorNote: 看到公司又在 LinkedIn 發「我們是員工友善企業」之類的廢文時，留言區用得上
variations: 工作生活平衡的意思是：上班時想著生活，下班後還要想著工作，這樣兩邊都有想到，平衡。
他們說的平衡是天平，我體驗到的是蹺蹺板，而且工作端坐了一個成年人，生活端只坐了一杯咖啡。

en.content:
The careers page says: 'We value work-life balance.'

I said:

Noted.

What that means is: you handle the work part,
and I'll figure out where to cram the 'life' into whatever's left over.

That's not balance.
That's 99% work, 1% life — but technically still balance,
because both sides of the scale do have something on them.
en.editorNote: Pull this out when your company posts another 'we're employee-first' brag on LinkedIn — works great in the comments of your group chat
variations: Work-life balance means thinking about life at work, and thinking about work at home. Both sides represented. Balanced.
