They promised a balanced scale. I got a seesaw — with a full-grown adult on the 'work' side and a single iced latte on 'life'.

items.sourceNote: 厭世負能量金句參考，重新創作「週末耍廢」現代生活主題

zh-tw.content:
他們說：年輕人要好好利用週末，多充實自己。

我說：

我有充實啊。

我把躺在沙發上的姿勢，
從仰躺練到側躺，再練到趴著刷手機，
每一個姿勢都需要不同的肌群。

這叫做耍廢瑜珈，
比你那種要起床換衣服才能做的運動，
門檻高多了。
zh-tw.editorNote: 週日晚上被親戚問「週末做了什麼」的時候，心裡默念這段，再露出一個專業的微笑
variations: 我週末很充實，從早到晚跟手機建立深厚感情，這也是一種長期關係經營。
別人週末爬山充電，我週末躺床充電，差別是他用太陽能，我用 USB-C。

en.content:
They say: young people should make the most of their weekends — invest in yourself.

I said:

I am.

I've upgraded my couch posture
from flat-on-back, to side-lying, to face-down with one thumb scrolling.
Each position activates a completely different muscle group.

It's called slacker yoga.
The entry barrier is way higher than whatever workout
requires you to first stand up and put on pants.
en.editorNote: When the relatives ask 'so what did you do this weekend?' on Sunday night — recite this internally, then deliver a polite smile
variations: My weekend was very productive. I built a deep, lasting relationship with my phone — that's long-term commitment.
Other people recharge by hiking. I recharge by lying down. Difference is they use solar, I use USB-C.

items.sourceNote: 幽默回應社交比較相關金句參考，重新創作「IG 完美人生」社群主題

zh-tw.content:
朋友說：你看那個誰，IG 上又出國又買房，人生勝利組。

我說：

對。

但你要記得，IG 是精選集，不是日記。

他放出來的是飛機餐＋海景房，
沒放出來的是貸款、信用卡帳單、和半夜失眠盯著天花板的那一刻。

你拿你的 NG 片段，
去比別人的精華剪輯，
當然會輸——這場比賽的規則本來就不公平。
zh-tw.editorNote: 晚上滑 IG 滑到開始懷疑人生時，自己存一份起來複習用
variations: IG 是別人替自己拍的廣告片，你卻當紀錄片在看，難怪會難過。
你以為你在輸給別人的人生，其實你是在輸給別人的剪輯軟體。

en.content:
A friend said: 'Look at her Instagram — vacations, new apartment, total winner.'

I said:

Sure.

But remember: Instagram is a highlight reel, not a diary.

What she posts is the in-flight meal and the ocean view.
What she doesn't post is the loan repayment, the credit card statement,
and the 3 a.m. ceiling-staring session.

You're comparing your bloopers
to someone else's final cut.
Of course you're losing — the contest was never fair to begin with.
en.editorNote: When you've spiraled into a 'why is everyone else's life better' Instagram hole at midnight — save this and reread later
variations: Instagram is the commercial someone made for themselves. You're treating it like a documentary. That's why it hurts.
You think you're losing to someone else's life. Really, you're losing to their editing app.

items.sourceNote: 諷刺類金句參考，重新創作「我沒空運動」現代藉口主題

zh-tw.content:
他說：你應該每天運動三十分鐘，對身體好。

我說：

我有運動啊。

我每天從床走到馬桶、馬桶走到沙發、
沙發走到冰箱、冰箱走回沙發，
這樣循環六次，剛好三十分鐘。

這叫居家組合訓練，
結合心肺、平衡、和開冰箱前的內心交戰，
比健身房難多了。
zh-tw.editorNote: 被健身狂朋友連續第三次問「今天有沒有動一下」時，認真回他這個
variations: 我不是不運動，我是不選擇那種需要換衣服、出門、見人的運動。
你說的「動一下」我有做，我從躺平動到坐起，動作幅度跟你的深蹲差不多。

en.content:
He said: you should exercise thirty minutes a day, it's good for you.

I said:

I do exercise.

Every day I walk from bed to toilet, toilet to couch,
couch to fridge, fridge back to couch.
Six laps. Exactly thirty minutes.

It's a home circuit. Cardio, balance,
and the internal moral debate before opening the fridge —
harder than anything you'd find at the gym.
en.editorNote: When your gym-bro friend asks 'did you move today?' for the third time this week — fire this back, fully sincere
variations: I'm not anti-exercise. I'm just anti the kind of exercise that requires changing clothes, leaving the house, and seeing people.
You said 'get up and move.' I did. I moved from horizontal to vertical. Same range of motion as your squats.

items.sourceNote: 成年人厭世金句參考，重新創作「拖延症與帳單」主題

zh-tw.content:
他們說：成年人要學會處理問題，不要逃避。

我說：

我有處理啊。

我把帳單放進抽屜，蓋上一本書，再壓一個馬克杯，
眼不見為淨，這就是我的處理方式。

我不是在逃避，
我是在等待一個更成熟、更有錢的我，
從未來穿越回來幫我處理——
他遲到很久了，但我還相信他。
zh-tw.editorNote: 月底看到信用卡帳單想假裝它不存在的時候，傳給自己的後備人格看
variations: 我的處理問題方式叫做「先冰起來」，等問題自己融化或變成別人的問題。
拖延不是逃避，是給未來的自己更多挑戰。我對他很有信心。

en.content:
They say: adults handle their problems instead of running from them.

I said:

I am handling them.

I put the bill in a drawer, covered it with a book, and stacked a mug on top.
Out of sight, out of mind. That's my system.

I'm not avoiding it.
I'm waiting for a more mature, better-paid version of me
to travel back from the future and deal with it.
He's running late, but I still believe in him.
en.editorNote: When the credit card statement hits and you want to pretend it doesn't exist — send this to your backup personality
variations: My problem-solving method is 'put it in the freezer' — let it melt on its own or become someone else's problem.
Procrastination isn't avoidance. It's giving Future Me more character development. I have full faith in him.

items.sourceNote: 諷刺生活金句參考，重新創作「假裝忙碌」上班族主題

zh-tw.content:
主管問：你今天有沒有很忙？

我說：

有。

我忙著切換視窗讓螢幕看起來很豐富，
忙著打字假裝在寫信，其實只是按了一行 asdfasdf，
忙著皺眉看 Excel，雖然那是上週的檔案，
還忙著喝水，補充我假裝忙碌時流失的水分。

我不是在划水，
我是在演一齣叫做《敬業》的舞台劇，
而且我演得很投入。
zh-tw.editorNote: 下午三點被主管路過工位問近況時，心裡默念的版本
variations: 上班最忙的不是工作，是在被看見的瞬間切到正確的視窗。
我不是沒在做事，我是在做「讓你以為我在做事」這件事，這也很累。

en.content:
My manager asks: are you busy today?

I say:

Yes.

I'm busy alt-tabbing between windows to make my screen look rich.
Busy typing fake emails — really just asdfasdf on a loop.
Busy frowning at a spreadsheet from last week.
And busy hydrating, because pretending to be busy is dehydrating work.

I'm not slacking.
I'm starring in a one-person play called 'Dedicated Employee.'
And my performance is committed.
en.editorNote: When your boss walks past your desk at 3pm and asks how things are going — recite this internally
variations: The hardest part of office work isn't the work. It's snapping to the right window the second someone walks by.
I'm not doing nothing. I'm doing the thing called 'looking like I'm doing something.' It's exhausting too.

items.sourceNote: 諷刺類幽默金句參考，重新創作「人際社交電量」主題

zh-tw.content:
朋友說：你應該多出去走走，多認識人，對你比較好。

我說：

好，謝謝你的關心。

但我這個人比較像手機，社交是高耗電 App。
出去一個下午，我電量直接從 80% 掉到 5%，
還會跳出「省電模式」，變成只想吃飯睡覺看劇的省電版本。

我不是孤僻，
我是電池容量小，但內建系統穩定，
獨處就是我的無線充電。
zh-tw.editorNote: 週末被朋友連環邀約聚會、想婉拒又怕被說邊緣時用
variations: 別人是社交充電，我是社交放電——同樣是充電線，方向卻完全相反。
我跟人相處不是不開心，是會耗電。我需要回家把自己插上插座。

en.content:
A friend says: you should get out more, meet people, it'd be good for you.

I say:

Thanks, I appreciate the concern.

But I'm built like a phone — socializing is a battery-draining app.
After one afternoon out, I'm at five percent.
'Low Power Mode' kicks in, and I downgrade to a version of me
that can only eat, sleep, and watch shows.

I'm not antisocial.
I just have a small battery and a stable OS.
Being alone is how I wirelessly charge.
en.editorNote: When your friend group keeps inviting you to weekend hangs and you want to politely decline without sounding like a hermit
variations: Some people charge by socializing. I discharge by socializing. Same cable, opposite direction.
It's not that I dislike people. It's that they drain my battery. I need to go home and plug in.

items.sourceNote: 諷刺人生金句參考，重新創作「自我成長焦慮」主題

zh-tw.content:
他說：你都幾歲了，怎麼還沒開始好好規劃人生？

我說：

我有規劃啊。

我規劃過要早睡、要存錢、要學新技能、要少喝飲料、
要每天運動、要讀完那本買了三年的書——
計畫做得很完整，每一份都印出來貼在牆上。

你看我牆上滿滿的都是計畫，
那叫做人生規劃藝術展，
參觀免費，動工要錢。
zh-tw.editorNote: 過年回家被親戚追問「你有沒有規劃」的時候，心裡播放這段
variations: 我規劃了一個很棒的人生，目前處於「永久籌備中」階段。
別人是把計畫做出來，我是把計畫做出來再做一份新的，這也叫做產出。

en.content:
He says: you're how old now? Why haven't you started planning your life properly?

I say:

I have plans.

I've planned to sleep earlier, save money, learn new skills, cut back on sugary drinks,
work out every day, finally finish that book I bought three years ago —
the plans are thorough. Each one is printed and pinned to my wall.

My wall is covered in plans.
It's basically a Life Planning Art Exhibit.
Admission is free. Execution costs extra.
en.editorNote: When relatives at the family gathering ask 'so what's your plan?' — play this in your head while you nod politely
variations: I've designed a beautiful life. It's currently in 'permanent pre-production.'
Other people execute their plans. I make plans, and then I make new plans about the old plans. That's also output.

items.sourceNote: 拖延症金句參考，重新創作「明天的我」主題

zh-tw.content:
今天的我對明天的我說：

沒關係啦，這件事交給你做就好。

結果隔天醒來，明天的我變成今天的我，
看著同樣那件事，又默默地對明天的我說了同樣的話。

我們三個人一直在傳球，
但這顆球從來沒有被踢進球門。

我不是在拖延，
我是在跟未來的自己進行一場永遠不結束的接力賽。
zh-tw.editorNote: 凌晨兩點面對明天的截稿日，把待辦事項往後再推一天時的內心戲
variations: 我對明天的我有信心，因為我把所有事情都交給他了，他應該很有實力。
拖延不是不做事，是把工作量平均分給未來的每一個自己，這叫風險分散。

en.content:
Today-me said to tomorrow-me:

Don't worry, I'll leave this one to you.

The next morning, tomorrow-me woke up as today-me,
looked at the same task, and quietly said the same thing to tomorrow-me.

The three of us have been passing the ball forever.
Nobody has actually scored.

I'm not procrastinating.
I'm running a relay race with my future selves
that has no finish line.
en.editorNote: When it's 2am the night before a deadline and you decide to push the task one more day — this is the inner monologue
variations: I have a lot of faith in tomorrow-me. I keep handing him my work, so he must be very capable.
Procrastination isn't avoiding work. It's distributing the workload across all your future selves. That's risk diversification.

items.sourceNote: 諷刺金句參考，重新創作「成年人崩潰」主題

zh-tw.content:
別人問我：當大人是什麼感覺？

我想了想，認真回答：

就是早上起床要決定今天要崩潰大事還是小事。

大事崩潰是看到帳單、健保費、信用卡帳單、房租同時到期；
小事崩潰是發現家裡衛生紙剩半捲，但今天剛好不想出門。

我以為長大會學到怎麼解決問題，
後來才發現我只是學到怎麼把問題排優先順序，
然後一個一個慢慢崩潰。
zh-tw.editorNote: 週一早上面對一週待辦清單時的精神狀態實況轉播
variations: 成年人不是不會崩潰，是學會了挑時間崩潰，最好挑沒人看到的時候。
我每天的精神能量只夠處理一件事，剩下的問題請排隊到明天。

en.content:
Someone asked me: what does being an adult feel like?

I thought about it. Then I answered honestly:

It feels like waking up and having to decide whether today's breakdown will be a big one or a small one.

Big breakdown: bills, insurance, credit card, and rent all due the same week.
Small breakdown: realizing you have half a roll of toilet paper left and you really don't want to leave the house today.

I thought growing up meant learning to solve problems.
Turns out I just learned to rank them,
and then break down over each one in order.
en.editorNote: The mental state live-broadcast as you face Monday morning's to-do list
variations: Adults don't stop having breakdowns. They just learn to schedule them — preferably for moments no one's watching.
My daily mental energy only covers one problem. The rest will please form a line and try again tomorrow.

items.sourceNote: Instagram諷刺金句參考，重新創作「假裝有在運動」主題

zh-tw.content:
我跟健身房的關係很穩定。

每個月我準時繳會費，
它準時不出現在我生活裡。

我們互相尊重，
它從不打擾我，
我也從不打擾它。

別人說我浪費錢，
我說這是給未來的我留一條後路：
哪天我終於想去運動的時候，
發現身分還在、卡還在、會員資格還在，
我就可以非常感性地走進去，
再非常感性地走出來，
然後繼續繳下一個月的會費。
zh-tw.editorNote: 看到信用卡帳單上的健身房月費時，誠實地對自己解釋這筆支出
variations: 我不是不運動，我是用付錢的方式參與運動產業，這也是一種貢獻。
我的健身房會員卡已經養了三年，再養一年它就可以自己去上課了。

en.content:
My relationship with my gym is very stable.

Every month I pay the fee on time,
and the gym, on time, doesn't appear in my life.

We respect each other.
It never bothers me.
I never bother it.

People say I'm wasting money.
I say I'm leaving a back door open for my future self:
the day I finally feel like working out,
my account is still active, my card still works, my membership still valid.
I can walk in very emotionally,
walk out very emotionally,
and continue paying next month's fee.
en.editorNote: What you tell yourself, very honestly, when you see the gym charge on your credit card statement
variations: I'm not avoiding exercise. I'm participating in the fitness industry through payment. That's also contribution.
My gym membership has been alive for three years. One more year and it can take its own classes.

items.sourceNote: 厭世幽默語錄參考，重新創作「假日補眠悖論」主題

zh-tw.content:
週五晚上我跟自己約好：

明天一定要好好睡到自然醒，補回這一週的睡眠債。

結果週六早上六點半，我的眼睛準時打開，
比上班的鬧鐘還要早，
比上班的我還要清醒。

我躺在床上瞪著天花板想：
身體啊，你平常上班不肯醒，
放假倒是非常敬業，
你是不是搞錯了你的工作內容？

我跟我的生理時鐘關係不好，
它從不聽我的意見。
zh-tw.editorNote: 週六清晨自然醒來看著時鐘想哭時，可以拿出來自我嘲諷一下
variations: 上班鬧鐘響五次都叫不起，假日鬧鐘沒響也能六點起床，身體真的很有想法。
我以為睡眠債可以週末還，後來發現銀行週末不營業。

en.content:
Friday night, I made a deal with myself:

Tomorrow I will sleep in properly and pay back this week's sleep debt.

Saturday morning, 6:30am sharp, my eyes opened on schedule —
earlier than my weekday alarm,
more awake than my weekday self.

I lay there staring at the ceiling thinking:
Body, you refuse to wake up for work all week,
but on the weekend you're suddenly extremely dedicated.
Did you misread your job description?

My relationship with my body clock is bad.
It has never once listened to me.
en.editorNote: For the Saturday morning when you wake up at dawn, look at the clock, and almost cry — pull this out for some self-deprecating comfort
variations: My alarm rings five times on weekdays and I can't move. On weekends, no alarm, but I'm up at 6am. The body really has its own opinions.
I thought I could repay sleep debt on the weekend. Turns out the bank is closed on Saturdays.

items.sourceNote: 諷刺金句參考，重新創作「會議地獄」主題

zh-tw.content:
我每天上班最大的成就，
就是參加完一場本來可以用一封信解決的會議。

會議的功能很特別：
它讓本來十分鐘可以講完的事，膨脹成一個小時；
讓本來可以一個人決定的事，變成八個人一起不決定。

散會的時候大家會說：
「下次再開一次會討論。」

我才終於懂了，
會議不是用來解決問題的，
是用來繁殖更多會議的。
zh-tw.editorNote: 週三下午連開三場會結束後，回到位子上對著電腦發呆時的心境
variations: 今天會議的結論是：我們約下週再開一場會討論這次會議的結論。
如果開會能瘦身，我現在應該已經是模特兒。

en.content:
My biggest accomplishment at work each day
is surviving a meeting that should have been an email.

Meetings have a special function:
they take a ten-minute issue and stretch it into an hour,
and take a one-person decision and turn it into eight people not deciding.

At the end, someone always says:
"Let's set up another meeting to discuss this further."

That's when I finally understood —
meetings aren't designed to solve problems.
They're designed to reproduce more meetings.
en.editorNote: The vibe after three back-to-back Wednesday afternoon meetings, sitting at your desk staring at your monitor
variations: Today's meeting concluded with: let's schedule another meeting next week to discuss the conclusions of this meeting.
If meetings burned calories, I'd be a supermodel by now.

items.sourceNote: 諷刺人生金句參考，重新創作「發薪日的短暫快樂」主題

zh-tw.content:
發薪日當天，我是有錢人。

發薪日後第三天，我是普通人。

發薪日後第七天，我開始研究冰箱裡剩什麼可以煮兩餐。

發薪日後第十四天，我會跟自己說：
「沒關係，下個月一切會好起來。」

下個月發薪日當天，我又是有錢人。
然後新的一輪輪迴又開始。

別人的人生有春夏秋冬，
我的人生只有：剛發薪、快沒錢、沒錢、終於發薪。
zh-tw.editorNote: 看著銀行 App 餘額從五位數慢慢變回四位數時，內心的旁白
variations: 薪水進帳那一刻是我最快樂的時候，因為接下來這筆錢只會越來越少。
我跟錢的關係很真誠：它從來不騙我，每個月都準時離開。

en.content:
On payday, I am rich.

Three days after payday, I am normal.

Seven days after payday, I am studying what's in the fridge to stretch into two meals.

Fourteen days after payday, I tell myself:
"It's okay. Next month will be better."

Next month on payday, I am rich again.
And the cycle restarts.

Other people's lives have spring, summer, fall, winter.
My life only has: just got paid, almost broke, broke, finally got paid.
en.editorNote: Inner monologue while watching your banking app balance shrink from five digits back down to four
variations: The happiest moment of my month is when payday hits, because from that point on, the number can only go down.
My relationship with money is honest. It never lies to me. It leaves on time every month.

items.sourceNote: 成年人幽默語錄參考，重新創作「大人就是一直 Google」主題

zh-tw.content:
我以為長大會變成一個什麼都懂的人。

結果長大之後，
我每天的工作是：一直 Google。

Google 怎麼報稅、Google 馬桶為什麼會漏水、
Google 這個食材過期幾天還能吃、
Google 這個合約這句話到底什麼意思、
Google 為什麼我的肩膀痠痛是不是要去看醫生。

我以前覺得大人很厲害，
後來才發現，
大人不是什麼都會，
是大人比較會搜尋。
zh-tw.editorNote: 凌晨一點對著一份不會填的表格時，誠實面對自己的成長狀態
variations: 我不是專業人士，我是一個 Google 用得很熟的人。
成年人的核心技能不是解決問題，是會問對的關鍵字。

en.content:
I thought growing up meant becoming someone who knows things.

Instead, my actual job as an adult is:
Googling. All day. Every day.

Google how to file taxes. Google why the toilet is leaking.
Google if this food is still safe two days past the date.
Google what this clause in the contract actually means.
Google if this shoulder pain means I need to see a doctor.

I used to think adults were impressive.
Then I realized:
adults don't know everything.
Adults are just slightly better at searching.
en.editorNote: Honestly confronting your growth stage at 1am while staring at a form you don't know how to fill out
variations: I'm not a professional. I'm just a person with strong Google skills.
The core competency of adulthood isn't solving problems. It's knowing the right keywords.

items.sourceNote: 週一幽默語錄參考，重新創作「跟星期一和解」主題

zh-tw.content:
我跟星期一的關係很複雜。

它每週準時出現一次，
從來不請假，從來不遲到，
是我認識的所有對象裡面最敬業的。

問題是，我從來沒有邀請過它。

我試過討厭它、迴避它、咒罵它、
甚至假裝今天還是星期日。
但它永遠都會在我醒來的那一刻
非常禮貌地坐在我床邊，
說：「早安，又是我。」

我終於放棄了。
我跟星期一的關係不會變好，
但我們可以保持禮貌的點頭之交。
zh-tw.editorNote: 週一早上鬧鐘響的瞬間，告訴自己今天就是會這樣，然後繼續刷牙
variations: 星期一不是被討厭的，是被需要的，每個禮拜都有人靠它合理化自己的爛心情。
我不是不喜歡星期一，我是不喜歡所有沒有放假的日子，星期一只是第一個受害者。

en.content:
My relationship with Monday is complicated.

It shows up on time every week.
It never calls in sick, never runs late.
It is, by far, the most dedicated entity in my life.

The problem is, I never invited it.

I've tried hating it, avoiding it, cursing it,
even pretending today is still Sunday.
But every week, the moment I open my eyes,
it is sitting politely at the edge of my bed saying:
"Good morning. It's me again."

I've finally given up.
My relationship with Monday will never improve,
but we can be polite nodding acquaintances.
en.editorNote: The moment the Monday alarm rings — accept that today will just be like this, and keep brushing your teeth
variations: Monday isn't hated. Monday is needed. Every week, someone uses it to justify a bad mood.
I don't dislike Monday specifically. I dislike all days without a holiday. Monday is just the first victim.

items.sourceNote: 千禧世代日常幽默參考，重新創作「待辦事項輪迴」主題

zh-tw.content:
我的待辦事項清單上有一件事，
從去年四月寫到現在還沒完成。

我每次打開清單都會看到它，
每次都跟自己說：「明天再說。」

它不是什麼很難的事，
它只是被我冷凍了十三個月。

我跟我的待辦事項清單關係很微妙：
我把它當記事本，
它把我當笑話。

如果拖延算是一門才藝，
我應該已經可以辦個人巡迴展覽。
zh-tw.editorNote: 週日晚上打開記事本，看到那條從去年延續到今年的任務時，誠實地笑一下自己
variations: 我的待辦事項清單比較像是一個願望池，丟進去就好，不一定要實現。
我不是不想做，我是把它放在「之後」這個資料夾，那個資料夾永遠不會被打開。

en.content:
There's one item on my to-do list
that I wrote down last April and still haven't done.

Every time I open the list, I see it sitting there.
Every time, I tell myself: "Tomorrow."

It's not even a hard task.
It has just been on ice for thirteen months.

My relationship with my to-do list is delicate:
I treat it like a notebook.
It treats me like a joke.

If procrastination counted as a skill,
I could probably book a solo gallery tour at this point.
en.editorNote: Sunday night, opening your notes app and seeing that one task that survived from last year — laugh at yourself, honestly
variations: My to-do list is less of a list and more of a wishing well. You toss things in. They don't have to come true.
It's not that I don't want to do it. I filed it under "later," and that folder is never opened.

items.sourceNote: 千禧世代推文集參考，重新創作「下班後的休息悖論」主題

zh-tw.content:
我每天下班回到家最大的計畫，
就是好好休息。

結果晚上九點，
我躺在沙發上滑手機；
晚上十一點，
我還在沙發上滑手機；
凌晨一點，
我終於想到要去洗澡。

洗完澡躺在床上的時候，
我會非常認真地後悔：
「我今天怎麼什麼事都沒做？」

結論是：
我沒有不休息，
我是把休息和擺爛搞混了，
結果兩件事都沒做好。
zh-tw.editorNote: 凌晨兩點關燈躺下，發現今天什麼都沒做的那一刻，可以拿出來自我和解
variations: 我以為下班就是休息的開始，後來才發現下班是滑手機的開始。
休息和耍廢的差別在於：休息之後你會變好，耍廢之後你只會變晚睡。

en.content:
Every day after work, my biggest plan
is to rest properly.

Then at 9pm,
I'm on the couch scrolling.
At 11pm,
I'm still on the couch scrolling.
At 1am,
I finally remember I need to shower.

And as I lie in bed afterward,
I very seriously regret it all:
"How did I do nothing today?"

The conclusion:
I'm not failing to rest.
I've just confused resting with rotting,
and ended up doing neither well.
en.editorNote: 2am, lights off, lying in bed realizing you did nothing today — pull this out for some gentle self-reconciliation
variations: I thought clocking out meant the start of rest. Turns out it's just the start of scrolling.
The difference between resting and rotting: resting makes you better. Rotting just makes you sleep later.

items.sourceNote: 千禧世代笑話變現實參考，重新創作「自我成長的諷刺」主題

zh-tw.content:
我這兩年看了很多自我成長的書、
聽了很多 Podcast、
追蹤了很多正能量帳號。

結果我學到最重要的一件事是：
看自我成長的東西本身，
會讓你覺得自己在成長，
但其實你只是坐著。

書翻完了，我還是同一個人；
Podcast 聽完了，我還是會拖延；
語錄存了八百則，我還是會跟同樣的事情生氣。

後來我終於懂了，
讀心靈雞湯不會改變你的人生，
但會讓你的相簿截圖變很多。
zh-tw.editorNote: 翻看手機相簿發現存了一堆「未來會用到」的勵志語錄截圖時的內心 OS
variations: 成長類書籍最有效的功能是：讓你以為自己在成長。
我存了一千句佳句，但我的人生還是沒有變好，看來句子不會自己跑去執行。

en.content:
Over the past two years I've read
a lot of self-improvement books,
listened to a lot of podcasts,
followed a lot of motivational accounts.

The most important thing I learned:
consuming self-improvement content itself
makes you feel like you're growing,
but actually you're just sitting there.

The book ends, I'm still the same person.
The podcast ends, I still procrastinate.
I've saved 800 quotes, and I still get angry at the same things.

Eventually I figured it out —
reading inspiration won't change your life,
but it will absolutely fill up your camera roll.
en.editorNote: Scrolling through your phone gallery and realizing it's 90% screenshots of motivational quotes you'll never reread — that exact internal monologue
variations: The most effective function of self-help books is making you feel like you're improving.
I've saved a thousand great quotes and my life still hasn't gotten better. Apparently the quotes won't execute themselves.

items.sourceNote: 幽默金句參考，重新創作「手機通知焦慮」主題

zh-tw.content:
我的手機每天響三百次。

其中兩百次是廣告，
八十次是不重要的群組訊息，
十九次是 App 自己想被打開，
剩下的一次，是我媽問我吃飽沒。

我曾經以為通知很多代表我很受歡迎。
後來才發現，
那只代表我被很多 App 騷擾。

我跟我的手機關係很特別：
我以為我擁有它，
但它每天都用震動提醒我，
誰才是這段關係裡比較主動的那一方。
zh-tw.editorNote: 看著手機螢幕一整排紅點通知，深呼吸點開 LINE 卻只是有人傳貼圖時的心情
variations: 手機通知 99% 是雜訊，但我們還是每次都認真點開，這就是現代人的浪漫。
我的手機比較像是一個吵鬧的同居人，租金我繳，主控權它有。

en.content:
My phone goes off 300 times a day.

Two hundred of those are ads.
Eighty are group chats I don't care about.
Nineteen are apps demanding to be opened.
The remaining one is my mom asking if I've eaten.

I used to think a lot of notifications meant I was popular.
Then I realized,
it just means I'm being harassed by a lot of apps.

My relationship with my phone is interesting:
I thought I owned it,
but it reminds me every day with a buzz
which one of us is the more active party in this relationship.
en.editorNote: Staring at a row of red dot notifications, taking a deep breath, opening the chat — and it's just someone sending a sticker
variations: Ninety-nine percent of phone notifications are noise, but we open every single one. That's modern romance.
My phone is less of a device and more of a loud roommate. I pay rent. It has the remote.

items.sourceNote: 幽默單句集參考，重新創作「省錢的奇怪邏輯」主題

zh-tw.content:
我為了省 60 元的外送費，
決定自己出門去買。

結果我在便利商店多買了一杯咖啡，
又順便買了一包餅乾，
回家路上經過手搖飲忍不住又點了一杯，
最後總共花了 280 元。

我看著收據，認真地對自己說：
「但是外送費省下來了。」

我跟我的錢包關係很坦白：
它知道我什麼都會省，
除了「為了省錢而花的錢」。
zh-tw.editorNote: 結完帳走出便利商店、發現自己又買多時，用這句自我嘲諷一下，繼續過日子
variations: 我省下的錢都用在「順便」買的東西上，總體來說我是在做一個負循環的回收。
便利商店是我意志力的墳場，外送費只是上面那塊墓碑。

en.content:
To save the $2 delivery fee,
I decided to walk to the store myself.

Then I bought an extra coffee at the convenience store,
grabbed a bag of cookies on the way,
stopped at a bubble tea shop because it was right there,
and spent a total of $9.

Looking at the receipt, I told myself, seriously:
"But I saved on delivery."

My relationship with my wallet is honest:
it knows I'll save on anything
except the money I spend trying to save money.
en.editorNote: Walking out of the convenience store realizing you bought way too much — say this to yourself and keep moving
variations: All the money I save gets spent on things I bought "while I was already there." It's a closed-loop economy of bad decisions.
The convenience store is where my willpower goes to die. The delivery fee is just the headstone.

items.sourceNote: 職場壓力幽默金句參考，重新創作「會議與生產力」主題

zh-tw.content:
今天又開了一個會。

會議結束後我看著我的筆記，
只有一句話：
「這個會其實可以用 email 解決。」

更殘酷的是，
我寫這句話的時間，
比看一封 email 還久。

我終於明白為什麼公司會議這麼多——
因為有人需要一個地方，
光明正大地什麼都不做。
zh-tw.editorNote: 從會議室走出來、回到自己座位的那 30 秒裡，最想說但永遠不會說出口的真心話
variations: 開會的本質，是讓大家一起確認：原來這件事沒人想負責。
我不怕加班，我怕的是開一個小時的會，然後被告知「我們下週再討論」。

en.content:
Had another meeting today.

After it ended I looked at my notes,
and there was only one line:
"This could've been an email."

The cruel part?
Writing that line
took longer than reading an email would have.

I finally understand why companies have so many meetings —
some people need a place
where doing nothing is completely socially acceptable.
en.editorNote: The 30-second walk back to your desk after a pointless meeting — exactly what you're thinking but will never say out loud
variations: The real purpose of meetings is letting everyone confirm together that no one actually wants to own this.
I'm not afraid of overtime. I'm afraid of a one-hour meeting that ends with 'let's revisit this next week.'

items.sourceNote: 負能量語錄參考，重新創作「假日結束的悲傷」主題

zh-tw.content:
週日晚上八點。

我躺在沙發上，
看著電視，
手裡拿著零食，
但我的心已經在公司了。

這就是社畜版的時差：
身體還在週末，
靈魂已經先去上班了。

以前我以為週日晚上的憂鬱是一種情緒，
後來才知道，
那是身體比腦袋更早知道明天要面對什麼的警告聲。
zh-tw.editorNote: 週日晚上邊看影集邊滑手機、心跳卻越來越快時，傳給也在裝沒事的同事
variations: Sunday night 的憂鬱不是病，是現代人的生理時鐘自帶的提醒功能。
我不討厭工作，我只是討厭工作來找我的那一刻。

en.content:
Sunday night, eight p.m.

I'm on the couch,
watching TV,
holding a snack,
but my mind has already clocked into the office.

This is the corporate version of jet lag:
the body is still in the weekend,
the soul left early for Monday.

I used to think Sunday-night dread was an emotion.
Now I know
it's just your body warning you about Monday
before your brain has caught up.
en.editorNote: When you're scrolling on Sunday night pretending to relax but your heart rate keeps climbing — send to the coworker who's also faking it
variations: Sunday-night dread isn't a disorder. It's the built-in alert system of every modern adult.
I don't hate work. I just hate the exact moment work comes back to find me.

items.sourceNote: 短篇諷刺金句參考，重新創作「拖延症患者的自白」主題

zh-tw.content:
我不是拖延，
我只是在等一個更有靈感的自己出現。

結果他從來沒出現過，
出現的都是更焦慮的我，
還有時間。

我跟「明天的我」關係很差：
我每天把事情丟給他，
他每天罵我為什麼又丟過來。

但他從來不還手，
因為他知道，
下一秒他就要變成「今天的我」，
然後繼續把事情丟給「明天的他」。

這就是一個負責任的循環——
沒人負責。
zh-tw.editorNote: 凌晨兩點還在看 YouTube，明明知道明天要交報告的那種微笑式絕望
variations: 拖延的本質是相信未來的自己會變強，但未來的自己只會變累。
我不是不做，我是在等截止日告訴我什麼最重要。

en.content:
I'm not procrastinating.
I'm just waiting for a more inspired version of me to show up.

He never does.
The one who shows up is just a more anxious version of me
with less time.

My relationship with Tomorrow Me is rough:
I keep dumping things on him,
he keeps yelling at me for dumping things on him.

But he never fights back,
because he knows
in one second he becomes Today Me,
and he'll do the exact same thing to Tomorrow Him.

It's a fully accountable loop —
with no one accountable.
en.editorNote: 2 a.m., still on YouTube, knowing the report is due tomorrow — that exact smiling-into-the-void feeling
variations: Procrastination is trusting that future you will be stronger. Future you will just be more tired.
I'm not refusing to start. I'm waiting for the deadline to tell me what actually matters.

items.sourceNote: 諷刺金句參考，重新創作「薪水與物價」主題

zh-tw.content:
我今年加薪了。

加了 3%。

同一時間：
房租漲 8%，
便當漲 15%，
手搖飲漲了一個我看不懂的百分比，
電費直接翻倍。

我手上的那 3% 就像一個禮貌的安慰，
在告訴我：
「我們有看到你的努力喔，
但我們不打算幫你解決什麼。」

加薪不是讓我變有錢，
只是讓我變窮的速度慢了一點。

這年頭，加薪 3% 跟說「祝你身體健康」差不多——
都是一句客氣話。
zh-tw.editorNote: 看到調薪通知的當下，先是欣慰、五秒後想起房租帳單，那種瞬間冷掉的表情
variations: 我的薪水成長曲線跟物價成長曲線正在比賽，物價已經跑去終點等我了。
加薪 3% 的意思是：恭喜你，今年比去年更慢地走向破產。

en.content:
I got a raise this year.

Three percent.

Meanwhile:
rent went up 8%,
lunch went up 15%,
my coffee went up by some percentage I can't even parse,
and the electric bill literally doubled.

That 3% in my hand feels like a polite condolence card,
saying:
"We see your hard work,
we just have no intention of doing anything about it."

A raise doesn't make me richer.
It just slows down how fast I'm getting poorer.

These days a 3% raise is basically a friendly greeting —
nice of you to say, doesn't pay for anything.
en.editorNote: The exact face you make when the raise email arrives, you feel grateful for five seconds, then remember rent is due
variations: My salary growth is racing inflation, and inflation already finished the race and is waiting at the finish line.
A 3% raise means: congratulations, you're going broke slightly slower than last year.

items.sourceNote: 負能量勵志語錄參考，重新創作「努力與選擇」主題

zh-tw.content:
他們說：選擇比努力更重要。

所以我選擇了——
不努力。

結果發現，
不努力也是一種需要努力維持的狀態：

要努力假裝不在乎，
要努力說服自己這樣很好，
要努力忍住別去看同學的薪水，
還要努力解釋給家裡聽，
為什麼你過得「也算過得去」。

原來不努力的人，
比努力的人還累。

人生最殘忍的真相是：
你以為躺平是放棄賽道，
其實只是換了一個更擠的賽道，
大家都在比誰躺得更穩。
zh-tw.editorNote: 假日早上滑手機看到別人發文「終於 offer 拿到了」，那一刻自我懷疑全面開機時可以默念
variations: 選擇比努力重要——前提是你有得選。沒得選的人，選擇就是繼續努力。
躺平是一門技術活，躺得好的人其實一直在偷偷站著。

en.content:
They say: choices matter more than effort.

So I chose —
not to put in any effort.

Then I discovered
that not trying is also a state
that takes effort to maintain:

effort to pretend you don't care,
effort to convince yourself this is fine,
effort to not look up your classmates' salaries,
effort to explain to your family
why you're "doing okay, I guess."

It turns out the people not trying
are more tired than the people who are.

The cruel truth about life:
you thought lying flat meant leaving the race.
It just put you on a more crowded track,
where everyone is competing
to lie flatter than the next person.
en.editorNote: Weekend morning, scrolling and seeing a classmate post 'finally got the offer' — read this in the exact moment your self-doubt turns on
variations: Choices matter more than effort — assuming you have choices. For everyone else, the only choice is to keep trying.
Lying flat is a skill. The people who do it well are quietly standing up the whole time.

items.sourceNote: 社畜厭世語錄參考，重新創作「下班才是上班的開始」主題

zh-tw.content:
公司說：我們很重視 work-life balance。

然後給我一台公務筆電、
一個 24 小時都會響的工作群組、
還有一句「你下班不用回喔，但看到也可以回一下」。

我才發現，
所謂的 balance，
是工作從早上九點開始，
生活從晚上十一點開始，
中間用一份外送便當當作分界線。

以前我以為下班是回家，
後來才知道下班是換一個地點繼續上班，
只是這個地點剛好有床。

真正的 balance，
是老闆 balance，員工 lance（沒了）。
zh-tw.editorNote: 晚上十點主管丟訊息「在嗎？小事而已」的那一秒，無聲尖叫的同時可以發給同部門的戰友
variations: Work-life balance 是公司給的禮物，但禮物盒裡只有 work，life 要自己想辦法生出來。
我下班了，但我的工作群組沒下班，所以我也沒下班。

en.content:
The company says: we really care about work-life balance.

Then they hand me a work laptop,
a chat group that never sleeps,
and a casual "you don't have to reply after hours, but if you see it, feel free to reply."

That's when I realized
the balance they mean
is work from 9 a.m.,
life from 11 p.m.,
with a takeout dinner as the border between them.

I used to think clocking out meant going home.
Now I know clocking out
just means continuing work in a new location —
the new location happens to have a bed.

Real work-life balance:
the boss has the balance,
the employee has the lance — straight through the chest.
en.editorNote: When the manager pings 'you up? quick thing' at 10 p.m. — scream internally, then forward this to your team-chat survivors
variations: Work-life balance is a gift from the company. Open the box and there's only work inside — the life part is your problem.
I clocked out. My team chat didn't. So I didn't either.

items.sourceNote: 現代成人生活諷刺金句參考，重新創作「成年人的崩潰是無聲的」主題

zh-tw.content:
小時候哭，全世界都會來哄你。

長大後哭，
要先確認門關了、
音樂夠大聲、
手機調靜音、
隔壁室友還沒回來，
才能放心地哭三分鐘。

然後擦乾眼淚，
打開冰箱拿一瓶可樂，
滑兩支搞笑短影片，
回到電腦前繼續加班。

成年人的崩潰是有 SOP 的：
崩潰、整理、上線、微笑、繼續。

我們不是不脆弱，
只是學會把脆弱排進行事曆，
而且通常會被會議蓋掉。
zh-tw.editorNote: 深夜洗澡時無聲流淚、隔天還是準時打卡上班的那種人，可以默默把這段傳給自己
variations: 大人不是不哭，只是哭完還要記得明天九點開會。
成年人的眼淚自帶吸水紙巾、定時器跟一杯溫水。

en.content:
As a kid, when you cried,
the whole world rushed to comfort you.

As an adult, before you cry,
you first check that the door is locked,
the music is loud enough,
your phone is on silent,
and your roommate isn't home yet —
then you allow yourself three minutes.

After that, you dry your face,
grab a soda from the fridge,
scroll two short videos,
and return to your laptop to keep working.

An adult breakdown has a standard procedure:
break, recover, log on, smile, continue.

We're not less fragile.
We just learned to schedule fragility into the calendar,
and it usually gets moved for a meeting.
en.editorNote: For the person who quietly cries in the shower at night and still clocks in on time the next morning — send to yourself, no one else needs to see
variations: Adults still cry. We just remember we have a 9 a.m. meeting tomorrow.
Grown-up tears come with built-in tissues, a timer, and a glass of warm water.

items.sourceNote: 諷刺金句參考，重新創作「社群媒體上的人生」主題

zh-tw.content:
打開 IG。

第一篇：朋友升職。
第二篇：朋友訂婚。
第三篇：朋友在峇里島喝椰子水。
第四篇：朋友的狗剛開個展。

我關掉手機，看了看四周：
泡麵、未洗的碗、明天要交的報告，
還有一隻不肯出門的我。

後來我才想通——
他們不是過得比我好，
他們只是把 23 小時的爛日子摺起來，
用最後一小時拍照上傳。

我也有那一小時，
只是我用來補眠了。

社群是這個時代的精修濾鏡：
每個人都在過自己編輯後的人生。
zh-tw.editorNote: 睡前手滑滑到別人去歐洲、自己還在床上的瞬間，可以默念這段壓壓驚
variations: IG 上的快樂是真的，只是它只佔當天的 4%，剩下的 96% 沒人會上傳。
別人發限時動態時，沒有發出來的那 23 小時，跟你今天一樣難。

en.content:
I open Instagram.

Post one: a friend got promoted.
Post two: a friend just got engaged.
Post three: a friend in Bali, holding a coconut.
Post four: a friend's dog had a gallery opening.

I close the app and look around:
instant noodles, unwashed dishes, a report due tomorrow,
and one me who refuses to leave the house.

Then it hit me —
they're not living better lives than mine.
They just folded up their 23 ugly hours
and uploaded a photo from the last one.

I also have that one good hour.
I just spent mine sleeping.

Social media is the filter of this era:
everyone is living an edited version of their life.
en.editorNote: When you scroll past someone in Europe right before bed and you're still in the same hoodie — read this, then put the phone down
variations: The happiness on Instagram is real. It's just 4% of someone's day. The other 96% never gets uploaded.
When someone posts a story, the 23 hours they didn't post about were probably as hard as yours.

items.sourceNote: 負能量金句參考，重新創作「家人的關心是雙面刃」主題

zh-tw.content:
家人的關心很特別——

它總是包裝成「我們是為你好」，
但拆開以後裡面是：
「什麼時候結婚？」
「薪水多少？」
「隔壁阿姨的兒子升副總了你知道嗎？」

我終於明白為什麼大家不想回家吃飯。
不是不想吃，
是那頓飯通常需要附帶一份人生簡報。

我準備在過年前先列印 FAQ：
第一頁，感情狀況，
第二頁，薪水級距，
第三頁，未來規劃（草稿）。

發完之後我就可以好好夾菜，
而不是邊夾菜邊解釋我整個人生。
zh-tw.editorNote: 過年回家前一週、想到飯桌上要被連環問三十題時，傳給也在準備閃避話題的朋友
variations: 家人的關心是一種免費服務——只是它不會問你想不想要。
我不是怕回家吃飯，我是怕飯桌變成人生績效檢討會。

en.content:
Family concern is a special kind of thing —

it's always wrapped in "we just care about you,"
but once you open it, inside you find:
"When are you getting married?"
"How much do you make?"
"Did you hear Auntie's son just made VP?"

I finally understand why people don't want to go home for dinner.
It's not the food.
It's that the meal usually comes with a mandatory life presentation.

This year I'm preparing an FAQ in advance:
page one — relationship status,
page two — salary range,
page three — future plans (draft).

Then I can actually focus on the food,
instead of explaining my entire life between bites.
en.editorNote: The week before a big family gathering when you can already feel the thirty-question interrogation coming — send to the friend who's also bracing
variations: Family concern is a free service. It just never asks whether you wanted it.
I'm not afraid of family dinner. I'm afraid the dinner table will turn into a performance review.

items.sourceNote: 厭世金句參考，重新創作「鬧鐘與床的拉鋸戰」主題

zh-tw.content:
早上六點半，鬧鐘第一次響。

我說：再睡五分鐘。
身體說：好。
良心說：好。
責任感說：好。

沒有人在這場會議裡投反對票。

等到第七次響的時候，
我終於從床上彈起來——

不是因為我想上班，
是因為我終於害怕到一個程度，
超越了我對被子的依戀。

上班族的早晨從來不是被陽光叫醒的，
是被「再不起來就要遲到了」的恐懼叫醒的。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合在連按七次貪睡鈕之後，傳給也在跟床搏鬥的同事，互相確認彼此都還活著
variations: 鬧鐘的功能不是叫你起床，是讓你每隔五分鐘體驗一次心碎。
我不是賴床，我只是在跟「上班」這件事做最後的告別儀式。

en.content:
6:30 AM. The alarm goes off for the first time.

Me: just five more minutes.
My body: agreed.
My conscience: agreed.
My sense of responsibility: agreed.

Nobody voted against it in this meeting.

By the seventh alarm,
I finally launched out of bed —

not because I wanted to go to work,
but because my fear of being late
finally outweighed my attachment to the blanket.

A working adult's morning is never woken by sunlight.
It's woken by the panic of 'if I don't move now, I'm screwed.'
en.editorNote: Send to a coworker after you both hit snooze seven times — a small ritual to confirm you are both, technically, alive
variations: The alarm's job isn't to wake you up. It's to break your heart every five minutes.
I'm not lazy. I'm just performing one last farewell ceremony to my bed before work.

items.sourceNote: 上班族厭世語錄參考，重新創作「通勤就是免費的耐力訓練」主題

zh-tw.content:
通勤的本質，是公司讓你免費接受耐力訓練。

你不是在搭捷運，
你是在跟一百個跟你一樣不想上班的人，
用體溫在交流今天的厭世指數。

門打開的瞬間沒有禮讓，只有競爭。
座位被搶走的時候沒有傷心，只有麻木。
你站在中間，扶著吊環，像個被生活掛起來晾乾的衣服。

下車那一刻你以為解脫了，
結果你發現你只是從一個地獄，
換到下一個地獄的入口。
zh-tw.editorNote: 尖峰時段被擠在捷運中央、距離最近的吊環還有三公分時，腦中默念這段，至少會比較好笑
variations: 通勤是公司給的福利——讓你在還沒上班之前，先把今天的力氣用掉一半。
捷運不是交通工具，是每天早上提醒你「人生不會更好」的儀式現場。

en.content:
Commuting is, fundamentally, free endurance training your company makes you do.

You're not riding the subway,
you're sharing body heat with a hundred other people
who also don't want to go to work,
silently exchanging today's misery index.

When the doors open there's no politeness — only competition.
When your seat gets snatched, there's no sadness — only numbness.
You stand in the middle, holding the strap, like laundry the universe hung out to dry.

When you finally step off, you think you're free.
Then you realize you've just walked
from one hell to the entrance of the next.
en.editorNote: Wedged in the middle of a packed train, three centimeters from the nearest handhold — repeat this internally, it gets a little funnier
variations: Commuting is a company perk — they let you burn half your daily energy before you even clock in.
Public transit isn't transportation. It's the daily ceremony reminding you that life doesn't actually get better.

items.sourceNote: 千禧世代幽默參考，重新創作「當自己的老闆才發現老闆有多難當」主題

zh-tw.content:
他們說：當自己的老闆，才是真正的自由。

沒人告訴我的是——

當我成為自己的老闆之後，
我有了一個非常嚴格的主管，
一個從不請假的下屬，
一個沒有勞健保的員工，
還有一個永遠在 deadline 前夕崩潰的客服。

而以上四個職位，都是我。

我以為我擺脫了打卡，
結果我變成了一個永遠在加班、不能拒絕加班、
還會半夜罵自己「怎麼這麼沒效率」的人。

自由的代價，原來是 24 小時都得自己撐。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個剛離職想創業的朋友，讓他在燃燒熱情之前先看清楚地形
variations: 當老闆最大的好處是不用看老闆臉色——但你會 24 小時看自己的臉色。
自由業的自由，是自由地在週末工作、自由地在凌晨回信、自由地懷疑人生。

en.content:
They said: being your own boss is true freedom.

What nobody mentioned was —

the moment I became my own boss,
I also got a very strict manager,
a subordinate who never takes time off,
an employee with no health insurance,
and a customer service rep who breaks down before every deadline.

All four of those positions are also me.

I thought I escaped the time clock.
Instead I became someone who works overtime constantly,
can't refuse overtime,
and yells at himself at 2 AM for being inefficient.

The price of freedom, it turns out, is being on call 24 hours a day — to yourself.
en.editorNote: Send to the friend who just quit their job to launch a startup — let them survey the terrain before the passion catches fire
variations: The best part of being your own boss is not having to read your boss's mood. The worst part is reading your own mood 24/7.
Freelance freedom means freely working weekends, freely answering emails at midnight, and freely questioning your life choices.

items.sourceNote: 成年人幽默語錄參考，重新創作「AI 沒有幫我變輕鬆，只是讓我加更多班」主題

zh-tw.content:
他們說：AI 會幫你省下時間，讓你下班更早。

結果——

AI 確實幫我寫完了報告，
所以老闆叫我再多寫三份。

AI 確實幫我整理了會議紀錄，
所以老闆說那你可以多開兩場會。

AI 確實幫我五分鐘完成了昨天要做兩小時的事，
所以老闆把那一小時五十五分鐘
塞進另一個專案裡。

科技的進步沒有讓人類變輕鬆，
它只是讓人類有時間
做更多本來不該做的事。

下班時間還是一樣晚，
只是現在我跟 AI 一起加班。
zh-tw.editorNote: 在加班到很晚、旁邊筆電還開著三個 AI 工具時，傳給同部門的同事，他會懂
variations: AI 不是來減輕你的工作的，是來證明你原本就還可以承擔更多工作的。
從前是一個人做三件事，現在是一個人加 AI 做十件事——薪水沒有變十倍。

en.content:
They said: AI will save you time, you'll get off work earlier.

In reality —

AI did help me finish the report,
so my boss told me to write three more.

AI did help me clean up the meeting notes,
so my boss said great, you can sit in on two more meetings.

AI did help me finish in five minutes what used to take two hours,
so my boss took those one hour and fifty-five minutes
and shoved them into another project.

Technology didn't make humans' lives easier.
It just gave humans time
to do more things they shouldn't have been doing.

Clocking-out time is exactly as late as before.
Now I'm just doing overtime with AI as my coworker.
en.editorNote: Working late with three AI tools open on your laptop — send to a teammate who's also still online, they'll get it
variations: AI didn't show up to reduce your workload. It showed up to prove you could always have handled more.
It used to be one person doing three things. Now it's one person plus AI doing ten things — and the paycheck did not multiply by ten.

items.sourceNote: 精緻窮主題參考，重新創作「月初像人、月底吃土」金句

zh-tw.content:
他們說：你要學會理財。

好啊，我已經把錢分成三份了——

第一份是房租，月初出去就再也沒回來過。
第二份是吃飯，吃完就消化掉了，連存根都沒有。
第三份是儲蓄，命名為「下個月再開始」，已經連續命名 36 個月。

所謂理財，原來就是
把同一筆錢，
用三種角度看著它消失。
zh-tw.editorNote: 月底打開銀行 App 看到三位數餘額時自言自語用，配一杯白開水更有層次
variations: 理財書一直叫我先付給自己——但我先付給自己之後，就沒錢付給房東了。
月初的我是個有規劃的成年人，月底的我是個吃泡麵的哲學家，這之間只隔了 27 天。

en.content:
They said: you need to learn how to manage your money.

Fine. I've already split it into three parts —

Part one is rent. It leaves on the first and never comes back.
Part two is food. It gets digested, no receipt survives.
Part three is savings, currently named "starting next month," a title it has held for 36 consecutive months.

Apparently managing money
just means watching the same money disappear
from three different angles.
en.editorNote: Mutter this to yourself at end of month when your bank app shows a three-digit balance — best paired with a glass of tap water
variations: Every finance book says pay yourself first. I tried. Then I had nothing left to pay the landlord with.
On the 1st I'm a responsible adult with a plan. By the 30th I'm a philosopher eating instant noodles. The two are 27 days apart.

items.sourceNote: 成人生活諷刺語錄參考，重新創作「凌晨三點的鬧鐘是後悔」主題

zh-tw.content:
我不需要鬧鐘。

我的後悔每天凌晨三點會準時把我叫醒，
而且從來不會睡過頭。

它會幫我細數：
五年前那句沒講出口的話、
三年前那個沒按下的「送出」、
上個月那次本來可以拒絕的會議、
還有今天那杯下午五點不該喝的咖啡。

它整理得很有條理，分類得很清楚，
甚至比我手機的相簿還勤勞。

我躺在床上聽它報告完畢，
說了一句謝謝，
然後天就亮了。
zh-tw.editorNote: 凌晨三點盯著天花板時用，這段念完天就快亮了，至少有事做
variations: 失眠不是病，失眠是大腦在加班整理你十年來的爛攤子。
我的睡眠品質不好，是因為我的潛意識比我會做專案管理。

en.content:
I don't need an alarm clock.

My regrets wake me up at 3 AM every day, on the dot,
and they have never once overslept.

They help me itemize:
the sentence I didn't say five years ago,
the "send" I didn't click three years ago,
the meeting I could've declined last month,
and that 5 PM coffee I should not have ordered today.

They're well-organized, neatly categorized,
actually more diligent than the photo app on my phone.

I lie there listening to the full briefing,
say thanks,
and then the sun comes up.
en.editorNote: Use this while staring at the ceiling at 3 AM — by the time you finish reciting it, the sun's almost up, at least it's productive
variations: Insomnia isn't a disorder. It's your brain working overtime to file ten years of unfinished business.
My sleep quality is poor because my subconscious is better at project management than I am.

items.sourceNote: 諷刺一句金句參考，重新創作「樂觀只是打了好看燈光的逃避」主題

zh-tw.content:
樂觀是什麼？

樂觀就是——

把問題搬到燈光好的地方，
拍一張很美的照片，
然後告訴自己：「看，它沒那麼可怕。」

至於問題本身？
它還在原地，沒有動，
只是現在拍起來比較有質感。

所謂保持正向，
很多時候不是解決什麼，
只是把同一個爛攤子，
換上不同濾鏡再看一次。

我不是不喜歡樂觀，
我只是希望樂觀偶爾也認真做點事，
不要每次都只負責拍照。
zh-tw.editorNote: 看到朋友圈那種「換個角度看世界就會不一樣」貼文時，按讚之前先念一遍冷靜一下
variations: 正向思考很好——但建議搭配一份解決方案，純配文字會消化不良。
樂觀不是改變現實，是把現實調成柔光模式。問題沒解決，只是看起來比較肉。

en.content:
What is optimism?

Optimism is —

moving the problem to a spot with better lighting,
taking a really nice photo of it,
and telling yourself: "See? It's not that scary."

The problem itself?
Still there. Hasn't moved.
It just photographs better now.

"Staying positive" often doesn't solve anything.
It just lets you look at the same mess
through a different filter, one more time.

It's not that I dislike optimism.
I just wish optimism would occasionally do some real work,
instead of always being the one in charge of photography.
en.editorNote: When you see one of those 'change your perspective, change your life' posts on social media, recite this once before tapping like — keeps you grounded
variations: Positive thinking is great — but it pairs better with an actual plan. On its own it gives me indigestion.
Optimism doesn't change reality. It just switches reality to soft-light mode. The problem isn't fixed, it's just better lit.

items.sourceNote: 成年人幽默金句參考，重新創作「成年就是把『晚點再說』說成一輩子」主題

zh-tw.content:
成年的本質，

是把「我等等再處理」說了一千次，
然後發現「等等」連在一起，就是你的人生。

那封信，等等回。
那筆帳，等等繳。
那場體檢，等等再約。
那個夢想，等等再追。
那段關係，等等再談。

等等等等，等到有一天，
你回頭一看，
才發現「等等」已經把你等成中年人了，
而且還是個額頭有點高的那種。

成年不是學會處理事情，
成年是學會用「等等」當動詞、形容詞、和遺囑。
zh-tw.editorNote: 拖了三天還沒回的訊息再次跳出來時，傳給自己看，順便提醒你按一下「已讀」
variations: 我不是拖延，我只是給未來的自己準備了一個很豐富的待辦清單。
成年人的時間管理，是把所有事情都排在「等比較有空的時候」——那個時候從來沒有到過。

en.content:
Adulthood, fundamentally,

is saying "I'll deal with it later" a thousand times
and then realizing all those "laters" stitched together is your entire life.

That email, later.
That bill, later.
That physical exam, later.
That dream, later.
That conversation, later.

Later, later, later — until one day
you look back
and find that "later" has quietly aged you into a middle-aged person,
the kind with a slightly higher forehead than they remember.

Adulthood isn't learning to handle things.
Adulthood is learning to use "later" as a verb, an adjective, and a last will.
en.editorNote: When that message you've been ignoring for three days pops up again, send this to yourself — and maybe tap 'mark as read' while you're there
variations: I'm not procrastinating. I'm just preparing a very generous to-do list for my future self.
Adult time management means scheduling everything for 'when I have more bandwidth' — a moment that has yet to arrive.

items.sourceNote: 諷刺幽默一句參考，重新創作「自律不是習慣，是另一種自我懲罰」主題

zh-tw.content:
他們說：自律的人最自由。

我試了。

我六點起床，七點運動，八點吃健康早餐，
九點開始工作，十二點吃一份不加美乃滋的雞胸肉沙拉。

我確實感覺到自由——

自由地懷疑人生、
自由地想念奶茶、
自由地計算今天還能不能吃一塊餅乾、
自由地在睡前因為多吃了一口堅果而對自己生氣。

所謂自律，
不是擺脫慾望，
是讓慾望變成你 24 小時的同事，
而且不能 fire 他。
zh-tw.editorNote: 健身第三週、體重沒變、奶茶卻變得更香時，傳給跟你一起報名健身房的朋友
variations: 我自律之後唯一變強的，是拒絕自己慾望時的內心戲。
自律不會讓你變得快樂，但會讓你變成一個非常會罵自己的人。

en.content:
They said: the disciplined are the most free.

I tried it.

Up at 6, workout at 7, healthy breakfast at 8,
start work at 9, grilled chicken salad at noon, no mayo.

I did feel free —

free to question my life choices,
free to miss bubble tea,
free to calculate whether I could afford one cookie today,
free to be furious at myself before bed for eating one extra almond.

Discipline isn't escaping your desires.
Discipline is making your desires a 24-hour coworker
you are not allowed to fire.
en.editorNote: Week three at the gym, the scale hasn't moved, but bubble tea has somehow gotten more appealing — send this to whoever signed up with you
variations: The only thing that got stronger after I became disciplined was the inner monologue I use to refuse myself things.
Discipline doesn't make you happy. It makes you extremely skilled at yelling at yourself.

items.sourceNote: 諷刺 Instagram caption 參考，重新創作「假裝沒事是當代成年人最高技能」主題

zh-tw.content:
有人問我：你最近好嗎？

我說：我很好。

翻譯一下：
我有點累、有點煩、有點想離職、
有點懷疑人生、有點想哭、有點不想說話，
但這些講出來會嚇到你，也會嚇到我自己，
所以我選擇講「我很好」這三個字，
然後笑得跟保險業務員一樣燦爛。

當代成年人不是不會表達情緒，
是發現表達了也沒人有空接，
乾脆改成自助情緒回收。
zh-tw.editorNote: 下次再被同事問「最近還好嗎」、你張口就想說「我很好」的時候，傳給自己看一眼
variations: 「我很好」是成年人的密碼，意思是「請不要再問了，我會自己處理」。
情緒沒有不見，只是被我打包進那句「沒事啦」的語助詞裡了。

en.content:
Someone asks: how have you been?

I say: I'm good.

Translation:
I'm tired, a little annoyed, kind of considering quitting,
slightly questioning my entire existence, occasionally crying in the bathroom,
and mostly not in the mood to talk —
but saying that out loud would scare you and frankly scare me too,
so I'll just say "I'm good,"
and smile like I'm selling you life insurance.

Modern adults aren't bad at expressing emotions.
We just figured out nobody has the bandwidth to receive them,
so we switched to self-service emotional recycling.
en.editorNote: Next time a coworker asks how you're doing and you instinctively reply "I'm good" — read this to yourself first
variations: "I'm good" is the adult password for "please stop asking, I'll handle it myself."
My feelings didn't disappear. I just packed them into the filler word inside "it's fine."

items.sourceNote: 千禧世代成年笑話參考，重新創作「我不是 work-life balance，我是 work-life 通通沒了」主題

zh-tw.content:
公司說：我們很重視員工的 work-life balance。

好喔。

那為什麼我下班後還在回訊息、
週末還在看 Slack、
睡前還在想週一要交的簡報、
做夢還夢到主管說「方便講一下嗎？」

所謂 work-life balance，
不是工作跟生活各佔一半，
是工作把生活當成緩衝區，
隨時可以借用、隨時不還。

我已經不追求平衡了，
我現在追求的是——
下班那兩個小時，請不要再有人 tag 我。
zh-tw.editorNote: 週日晚上看到主管丟一句「明天開會記得帶資料」時，傳給同事一起翻白眼
variations: Work-life balance 是工作借走生活，然後說「我下次還你」。
我不需要平衡，我只需要一個沒有人問我「在嗎」的週末。

en.content:
Company says: we really value work-life balance.

Cool.

Then why am I still replying to messages after hours,
checking Slack on weekends,
thinking about Monday's deck while brushing my teeth,
and dreaming of my manager saying "got a quick sec?"

Work-life balance isn't a 50/50 split.
It's work using your life as a buffer zone —
freely borrowed, never returned.

I've stopped chasing balance.
Now I'm just asking for one thing:
please, in those two hours after I log off,
do not tag me.
en.editorNote: When your manager drops a Sunday-night "bring the file tomorrow" message, send this to your coworker and roll your eyes together
variations: Work-life balance is work borrowing your life and saying "I'll pay it back next time."
I don't need balance. I need a weekend where nobody asks "you around?"

items.sourceNote: 成年人 Google 搜尋語錄參考，重新創作「成年的真相就是邊 Google 邊裝懂」主題

zh-tw.content:
小時候以為，長大之後就會知道很多事。

結果長大之後發現——

所得稅怎麼報？Google。
洗衣機 E3 是什麼意思？Google。
感冒跟流感差在哪？Google。
房貸試算？Google。
人生意義？也是 Google，雖然搜出來都是廣告。

成年的本質，
不是你真的會了，
是你學會在別人問你之前，
先快速 Google 然後一臉「這個我以前就知道」的表情。

大人不是知道答案的人，
大人是 Google 速度比較快的人。
zh-tw.editorNote: 下次有人問你一個「成年人應該要知道」的問題、你開始偷偷打字搜尋時，傳給自己壓壓驚
variations: 我不是會處理事情，我是會在五秒內找到「步驟教學」那種人。
成年人最大的技能不是獨立，是裝得一副「這我懂啦」然後回去偷偷查。

en.content:
As a kid, I assumed adults just knew things.

Then I grew up and discovered —

How do taxes work? Google.
What does E3 mean on the washing machine? Google.
Flu or just a bad cold? Google.
Mortgage math? Google.
Meaning of life? Also Google, though the results are mostly ads.

Adulthood isn't actually knowing things.
Adulthood is learning to Google fast enough,
before the person asking you notices,
then pulling a confident "oh yeah, I already knew that" face.

Adults aren't people with answers.
Adults are people with faster search reflexes.
en.editorNote: When someone asks you an "adults should know this" question and you start secretly typing into the search bar — send this to yourself for moral support
variations: I'm not good at handling things. I'm good at finding a step-by-step tutorial in under five seconds.
The real adult skill isn't independence. It's pretending you know, then looking it up the moment they leave.

items.sourceNote: 諷刺金句參考，重新創作「我不是內向，我是社交電量低」主題

zh-tw.content:
他們說：你太內向了，要多出去走走。

我不是內向。

我是社交電池只有 23%，
而且充電速度是 iPhone 4 那種，
出門兩小時，回家要躺三天才能恢復。

你叫我多出去走走，
等於叫一支低電量的手機去打 LINE 視訊、
開兩個 App、再順便導航——
它不是壞掉，是真的會關機。

我不討厭人，
我只是需要在見過人之後，
靜靜地、什麼都不做地、
當一塊沙發坐墊三小時。
zh-tw.editorNote: 週末聚會結束後，回到家連話都不想講的時候，傳給也是同款人格的朋友（不用回沒關係）
variations: 我不是冷淡，我是社交模式關閉中，請晚一點再試。
外向的人下班是回家充電，內向的人是回家躺成標本。

en.content:
They say: you're too introverted, you should get out more.

I'm not introverted.

I'm running on 23% social battery,
charging at iPhone-4 speeds,
so two hours out means three days flat on the couch.

Telling me to "get out more"
is like asking a low-battery phone to start a video call,
open two apps, and run GPS at the same time —
it's not broken. It will simply shut down.

I don't dislike people.
I just need, after seeing them,
to sit perfectly still doing absolutely nothing
for approximately three hours,
like a decorative couch cushion.
en.editorNote: After a weekend gathering when you get home and don't want to speak to anyone — send this to a same-wavelength friend (no reply needed, that's the point)
variations: I'm not cold. I'm in social mode: off. Please try again later.
Extroverts go home to recharge. Introverts go home to lie down like a museum exhibit.

items.sourceNote: 職場厭世語錄參考，重新創作「通勤就是還沒上班就先下班一次」主題

zh-tw.content:
有人說：通勤時間可以拿來聽 Podcast、學語言、規劃人生。

好喔。

但我的通勤是——
捷運被擠成真空包裝、
公車卡在五個紅綠燈、
下雨天傘開了又收、收了又開，
到公司之前，我已經先把今天的力氣花掉七成。

通勤不是上班前的暖身，
通勤是上班前的第一場硬仗。

打完之後再打第二場，
叫做「上班」。

所以你問我為什麼一坐到位子就放空——
我不是懶，
我是已經陣亡一次了。
zh-tw.editorNote: 週一早上九點剛走到公司、連電腦都還沒開就想回家的時候，傳給同樣剛從捷運生還的同事
variations: 通勤兩小時，上班八小時，加起來十小時，工作只給薪八小時——那兩小時是慈善。
我不是早上沒精神，我是早上已經先過完一輪人生了。

en.content:
Someone said: use your commute to listen to podcasts, learn a language, plan your life.

Sure.

But my commute is —
being vacuum-sealed into a subway car,
stuck behind five red lights on the bus,
opening and closing an umbrella six times in the rain.
By the time I reach the office,
I've already burned through 70% of my daily energy.

Commuting isn't a warm-up for work.
Commuting is the first boss fight before work.

The second boss fight
is called "my actual job."

So when you ask why I just stare into space the second I sit down —
I'm not lazy.
I already died once today.
en.editorNote: When you've just walked into the office on a Monday at 9am and you already want to go home — send to a coworker who also survived the morning rush
variations: Two hours commuting, eight hours working — that's ten hours total. They only pay for eight. The other two are charity.
I'm not low-energy in the morning. I've already lived a whole life before getting here.

items.sourceNote: 群組未讀訊息諷刺語錄參考，重新創作「群組 999+ 不是已讀是裝死」主題

zh-tw.content:
我的群組未讀數字停在 999+ 已經三個月。

不是因為我忙，
是因為我打開的瞬間，
會看到——

「請問有人在嗎？」
「跟上面那個一樣 +1」
「我也是+1」
「+1」
「+1」
「不好意思我剛剛沒看到」
（沒人說剛剛在說什麼）

我滑了三分鐘
還是不知道事情是什麼。

所以後來我選擇——
讓未讀繼續累積，
讓世界自己解決，
反正重要的事，他們會直接 tag 我。

不被 tag，就代表我安全。
zh-tw.editorNote: 週末早上打開 LINE 看到 12 個群組各有 200 則未讀，想直接登出地球時，傳給同款躺平派朋友
variations: 群組未讀不是已讀不回，是已讀不想處理。
我不是冷漠，我是先讓事情自己沉澱一下，看會不會跟我沒關係。

en.content:
My group chat unread count has been stuck at 999+ for three months.

Not because I'm busy.
Because the moment I open it,
I see —

"Hey, is anyone around?"
"+1 to whoever just said that"
"same +1"
"+1"
"+1"
"sorry just catching up"
(no one says what they were actually catching up on)

I scroll for three minutes
and still have no idea what's happening.

So now my strategy is —
let the unread count grow,
let the world sort itself out.
If it's important, someone will tag me directly.

Not getting tagged means I'm safe.
en.editorNote: When you open your messaging app on a Saturday morning, see twelve group chats each with 200+ unread, and want to leave the planet — send to your fellow opt-out friend
variations: Unread group messages aren't 'read and ignored.' They're 'seen and refuse to engage.'
I'm not cold. I'm letting the situation marinate until it stops involving me.

items.sourceNote: 成年人物價諷刺語錄參考，重新創作「去超市一趟，回來覺得自己變窮」主題

zh-tw.content:
以前去超市，是補貨。

現在去超市，是去進行一場心理建設訓練。

蛋——猶豫。
肉——猶豫。
水果——拿起來、看一眼價錢、放回去、再看一眼、最後只拿香蕉。
衛生紙——本來打算抽一包，結果只有十二包裝，咬牙買下去。

結帳時店員說「總共 1,247 元」，
我笑了一下，
那個笑容裡有三分驚訝、五分認命、兩分對人生的釋懷。

回家路上看著塑膠袋，
感覺自己不是買了菜，
是花了一千多塊買到一個「我有在好好生活」的錯覺。
zh-tw.editorNote: 週末從全聯走出來、看著手裡兩包東西卻刷掉一千多塊時，傳給同樣剛被物價教做人的朋友
variations: 我不是不會理財，是物價先一步把我的理財計畫吃掉。
現在的超市，逛一圈就能完成「對人生有體悟」這個成就。

en.content:
Grocery shopping used to be: restock the fridge.

Now grocery shopping is: a guided meditation on humility.

Eggs — hesitate.
Meat — hesitate.
Fruit — pick it up, look at the price, put it back, look again, leave with bananas only.
Toilet paper — wanted a small pack, but they only sell 12-rolls now. Buy anyway, weep internally.

At checkout the cashier says "that'll be $42."
I smile.
That smile is 30% surprise, 50% resignation, 20% spiritual acceptance.

Walking home with the bags,
it doesn't feel like I bought groceries.
It feels like I paid $42 for the illusion that I have my life together.
en.editorNote: When you leave the grocery store with two small bags and a $42 receipt — send to a friend who also just got humbled by the produce aisle
variations: I'm not bad with money. Prices were just bad to me first.
Modern grocery runs are a one-stop shop for both food and existential clarity.

items.sourceNote: 拖延症金句參考，重新創作「明天的我會處理／但明天的我也是我」主題

zh-tw.content:
我有一個非常厲害的助理，叫做「明天的我」。

所有今天不想做的事，
我都會說——

「沒關係，明天的我會處理。」

「這個交給明天的我。」

「明天的我比較會搞這個。」

結果到了明天，
我看著昨天交辦的清單，
才發現——

那個「明天的我」，
根本是「今天的我」本人，
只是換了個日期。

而且他比昨天的我更累、更不想動、更想再拖一天。

所以「明天的我」沒有要救我，
他只是排隊等著被下一個「明天的我」放鴿子。
zh-tw.editorNote: 晚上躺在床上、想到明天還有十件事沒做的時候，傳給同樣愛把事情往後丟的朋友
variations: 「我等等再做」是這個世界上最大的謊言，僅次於「我只看一集就睡」。
明天的我不是來救你的，他是來繼承你爛攤子，然後也罷工的。

en.content:
I have an incredibly capable assistant named "Future Me."

Everything I don't want to do today,
I say —

"It's fine, Future Me will handle it."

"Future Me is better at this kind of thing."

"Future Me has more energy."

Then tomorrow arrives,
I look at yesterday's to-do list,
and realize —

"Future Me"
is just Present Me
with a different date stamp.

And he's more tired than yesterday, even less motivated, and wants to push everything one more day.

So Future Me isn't here to save me.
He's just standing in line
to get stood up by the next Future Me.
en.editorNote: When you're lying in bed at night thinking about the ten things you didn't do today — send to a friend who also runs an entire life on 'I'll do it tomorrow'
variations: "I'll do it later" is the biggest lie in the world. Right after "just one more episode."
Future Me isn't your rescuer. He's the next guy who inherits your mess and goes on strike too.

items.sourceNote: 辦公室諷刺語錄參考，重新創作「這場會議本來只要一封信」主題

zh-tw.content:
今天的會議流程是這樣的——

9:00 開會。
9:05 大家還在連線。
9:10 主管說「我們來同步一下」。
9:40 我們還在同步同一件事。
10:15 結論是「下次再討論」。
10:16 行事曆跳出通知：下次會議已排好。

我看著螢幕，心裡只有一個念頭——

這場會議，
本來只需要一行字。

甚至不用一行字，
貼一個👍就解決了。
zh-tw.editorNote: 從一場毫無結論的會議走出來、看著行事曆發現下一場馬上又要開時，傳給同樣被會議耗乾的同事
variations: 「這個我們會議上再說」是這個世界上第二大的謊言，僅次於「五分鐘就好」。
我不是在開會，我是在用人生陪那個本來可以用一封 email 解決的議題。

en.content:
Today's meeting agenda —

9:00 Meeting starts.
9:05 Everyone still connecting.
9:10 Manager says "let's sync up."
9:40 We're still syncing on the same thing.
10:15 Conclusion: "let's discuss next time."
10:16 Calendar pings: next meeting already booked.

I stare at the screen with one thought —

This meeting
could have been a single line of text.

Actually, not even that.
A thumbs-up emoji would've handled it.
en.editorNote: When you walk out of a meeting that solved nothing and immediately see the follow-up meeting on your calendar — send to a coworker drained by the same loop
variations: "Let's circle back in the next meeting" is the second biggest lie in the world. Right after "this'll only take five minutes."
I'm not in a meeting. I'm donating my life force to a topic that one email would've closed.

items.sourceNote: 成年人睡眠語錄參考，重新創作「現在的我，睡姿錯了會痛三天」主題

zh-tw.content:
小時候——

席地而睡、樓梯邊小睡、甚至趴在桌上一覺到天亮，
醒來都是一條好漢。

現在——

只是睡覺翻身翻錯方向，
隔天脖子就直接罷工三天，
連回頭看人都要整個身體一起轉。

以前的我，是「年輕氣盛」。
現在的我，是「氣不太上來，盛也盛不起來」。

以前晚睡叫做「夜生活」，
現在晚睡叫做「明天我會付出代價」。

身體沒有壞掉，
它只是開始忠實地記帳。
zh-tw.editorNote: 早上起床發現脖子轉不動、但前一晚什麼都沒做的時候，傳給同樣被身體背叛的朋友
variations: 我的身體已經從「免費試用」進入到「按次收費」階段。
我不老，我只是身體開始要求加班費。

en.content:
As a kid —

I could sleep on the floor, nap on a staircase, or pass out face-down on a desk all night,
and wake up like nothing happened.

Now —

I roll the wrong way in my sleep,
and my neck files a three-day strike.
I have to turn my entire body just to look behind me.

The old me was "young and full of energy."
The current me is "running low and unable to refill."

Staying up late used to be called "nightlife."
Now it's called "I'll be paying for this tomorrow."

My body isn't breaking down.
It's just keeping much more accurate receipts.
en.editorNote: When you wake up unable to turn your head after doing absolutely nothing the night before — send to a friend whose body has also started billing them
variations: My body has officially moved from the free trial to pay-per-use.
I'm not old. My body just started charging overtime.

items.sourceNote: 同事互動諷刺語錄參考，重新創作「這個很簡單啊你怎麼不會」主題

zh-tw.content:
同事說：「這個很簡單啊，你怎麼不會？」

我說：「對，超簡單。」
「就像你解釋的時候一樣簡單。」
「簡單到我聽完更不會了。」

然後我笑了一下。

那個笑容裡，
有三分禮貌，
五分忍耐，
還有兩分——
「不然你來做？」
zh-tw.editorNote: 被同事用一種高高在上的口氣解釋完一件事、結果什麼都沒聽懂的時候，傳給同樣受夠這種人的朋友
variations: 「這個很簡單」這句話，從來不是用來幫助別人，是用來顯示自己。
如果這個真的很簡單，你就不會用「我跟你說過了」開頭七次。

en.content:
Coworker: "This is super easy. How do you not know how to do this?"

Me: "Right. So easy."
"As easy as your explanation just made it."
"Which is to say, I understand even less now."

Then I smile.

That smile is
30% politeness,
50% restraint,
and 20% —
"Would you like to do it yourself?"
en.editorNote: When a coworker condescendingly 'explains' something and you somehow understand less afterward — send to a friend who's also done with that energy
variations: "It's easy" is never said to help you. It's said to remind you who's smarter.
If it were really that easy, you wouldn't have started the sentence with "like I already told you" seven times.

items.sourceNote: 社畜厭世語錄參考，重新創作「今天也充滿希望，希望快點下班」主題

zh-tw.content:
早上九點。

我充滿希望地走進辦公室——

希望今天會議少一點。
希望主管今天心情好一點。
希望客戶今天理性一點。
希望系統今天不要再壞了。
希望午餐不要踩雷。
希望咖啡撐得到下午三點。

最大的希望是——

希望時鐘走快一點。

我不是不積極，
我是把所有積極都拿來盼下班。

這就是現代上班族的「正能量」：

每天都對未來充滿希望，
而未來，就是 18:00。
zh-tw.editorNote: 早上九點剛坐下、心已經在等下班的時候，傳給同樣靠「下班」兩個字撐著的同事
variations: 我每天都很有目標，我的目標叫做「準時下班」。
上班的意義，是為了證明下班有多珍貴。

en.content:
9 AM.

I walk into the office full of hope —

Hope today has fewer meetings.
Hope my manager is in a good mood.
Hope the client is being reasonable.
Hope the system doesn't crash again.
Hope lunch doesn't disappoint.
Hope my coffee lasts until 3 PM.

My biggest hope —

that the clock moves faster.

It's not that I'm not motivated.
I just channel all my motivation into longing for clock-out.

This is the modern worker's "positive energy":

Full of hope for the future every day.
And the future, in this case, is 6 PM.
en.editorNote: Send when it's 9:05 AM and you're already counting down to clock-out — to a coworker also surviving on the words 'end of day'
variations: I have a clear goal every day. It's called "leaving on time."
The whole point of working is to prove how precious not working is.

items.sourceNote: 生活諷刺語錄參考，重新創作「我跟宇宙要一個指示，它給我一個Check Engine燈」主題

zh-tw.content:
我問宇宙：

「請給我一個方向，一個指示，一個答案。」

宇宙回答了——

冷氣壞掉。
機車發不動。
手機跳出儲存空間不足。
信用卡帳單寄來。
體重計顯示一個我不想看的數字。

我懂了。

宇宙不是沒給我指示，
它是用「全部一起來」的方式回我，
意思是——

「你問太多了，先把眼前這些處理一下再說。」

從此我不再問宇宙問題。

我怕它連續回我七天。
zh-tw.editorNote: 一整天倒楣事接連發生、想對天空大喊「為什麼是我」的時候，傳給同樣覺得命運在惡作劇的朋友
variations: 我向人生許願「給我一個機會」，人生給了我一張催繳單。
命運從不沉默，它只是用帳單、故障燈和小擦撞跟你溝通。

en.content:
I asked the universe:

"Please give me a direction, a sign, an answer."

The universe replied —

AC broke.
Car won't start.
Phone says storage full.
Credit card bill arrived.
Scale showed a number I refuse to acknowledge.

I get it now.

The universe wasn't ignoring me.
It was replying with an "everything all at once" combo,
which roughly means —

"You're asking too much. Handle these first, then we'll talk."

I no longer ask the universe questions.

I'm afraid it'll keep replying for seven days straight.
en.editorNote: When everything that could go wrong goes wrong in one day and you want to yell at the sky — send to a friend who also feels personally targeted by fate
variations: I asked life for a chance. Life gave me a past-due notice.
Fate isn't silent. It just communicates through bills, warning lights, and minor fender benders.

items.sourceNote: 經典機智回應參考，重新創作「我不是在爭，我只是在解釋為什麼我對」主題

zh-tw.content:
朋友說：「你不要每次都這麼愛爭好不好？」

我說：「我沒有在爭。」

「我只是在很冷靜地、有條理地、用三個論點，
解釋為什麼是你錯。」

「這叫做說明。」
「不叫做爭。」

朋友沉默了三秒。

然後說——

「你看，你又來了。」

我笑了。

因為連這句話，
我都想反駁。
zh-tw.editorNote: 和朋友辯論到一半被指控「太愛爭」的時候，傳給那個總是站在你那邊的人
variations: 我不是固執，我是用事實在抵抗你的情緒。
我可以承認我錯，但你得先給我一個我真的錯的理由。

en.content:
Friend: "Why do you always have to argue?"

Me: "I'm not arguing."

"I'm calmly and methodically presenting three reasons
why you're wrong."

"This is called explaining."
"Not arguing."

Three seconds of silence.

Then they say —

"See? You're doing it again."

I smiled.

Because even that sentence,
I wanted to push back on.
en.editorNote: When you're mid-debate and someone accuses you of 'always arguing' — send to the friend who always backs you up
variations: I'm not stubborn. I'm just using facts to push back against your feelings.
I'll admit I'm wrong. As soon as you give me an actual reason that I am.

items.sourceNote: 負能量語錄參考，重新創作「努力不一定成功」延伸主題

zh-tw.content:
他們說：努力就會成功。

沒成功的人就是不夠努力。

我認真想了一下——

樓下早餐店阿姨凌晨四點起床煎蛋三十年。
計程車運將一天開十六小時。
清潔阿姨同時兼三份工。

他們不夠努力？

還是這句話原本應該是：

「努力是必要條件，
但運氣、家世、時機、
還有那個剛好認識誰的誰，
才是充分條件。」

只是這樣寫，
勵志書就賣不出去了。
zh-tw.editorNote: 看完別人發限動寫「努力就會成功」的雞湯文之後，傳給那個跟你一樣覺得社會比想像複雜的朋友
variations: 努力不一定成功，但不努力會非常輕鬆，這也算一種報酬。
勵志書的祕密是：作者本來就會成功，跟那本書沒什麼關係。

en.content:
They say: work hard and you'll succeed.

People who didn't make it just didn't try hard enough.

I thought about it for a minute —

The breakfast shop auntie has been up at 4 AM for thirty years.
The cab driver works sixteen hour shifts.
The cleaning lady juggles three jobs.

They weren't working hard enough?

Or maybe the sentence should actually be:

"Hard work is necessary,
but luck, family, timing,
and who happens to know someone who knows someone,
is what makes it sufficient."

It's just that nobody buys a self-help book
that opens with that.
en.editorNote: After scrolling past yet another 'just work hard' inspirational story — send to the friend who also thinks the world is more complicated than that
variations: Hard work doesn't guarantee success. Not working hard does guarantee a more relaxing failure though.
The secret of every self-help book is that the author was going to succeed anyway. The book had very little to do with it.

items.sourceNote: 經典回應參考，重新創作「請對製造你氧氣的樹道歉」延伸主題

zh-tw.content:
他在群組裡發了一段很長的訊息。

關於他多努力、多累、多有遠見，
以及世界多不懂他。

我滑過去，
按了一個讚。

不是因為我認同。

是因為我覺得——

如果我什麼都不回，
他可能會再發一段。

那個讚，
是給空氣的。

是給時間的。

是給今天還願意呼吸這個世界、
沒把手機丟出窗外的我自己。
zh-tw.editorNote: 看到群組裡有人發了一長串自我感動文、又不想回又不想得罪人時，傳給同樣只想已讀的朋友
variations: 我按讚，不代表我同意，只代表我選擇結束這個對話。
已讀是一種禮貌，已讀不回是另一種禮貌，已讀按讚是最高境界的禮貌。

en.content:
He posted a long message in the group chat.

About how hard he works, how tired he is, how visionary he is,
and how the world just doesn't get him.

I scrolled past
and tapped a like.

Not because I agreed.

Because I figured —

if I left it alone,
he'd post a second one.

That like
was for the air.

It was for time.

It was for the version of me who, today,
still chose to breathe the same atmosphere as him
and not throw my phone out the window.
en.editorNote: When someone drops a self-pitying essay in the group chat and you don't want to engage but don't want to be rude either — send to the friend who'd also just hit 'like' and move on
variations: A like doesn't mean I agree. It means I've decided this conversation is over.
'Seen' is polite. 'Seen with no reply' is another kind of polite. 'Seen with a like' is the highest form of polite.

items.sourceNote: 負能量金句參考，重新創作「我不是適合上班，我只是適合領薪水」延伸主題

zh-tw.content:
有人問我：「你喜歡你的工作嗎？」

我認真想了一下，回答——

「我不喜歡『工作』這個動作。」
「但我非常喜歡『領薪水』這個結果。」

所以我們之間是有交換的——

我假裝喜歡這份工作八小時，
公司假裝我每個小時都值那個價錢，
大家一起假裝這是一場合作。

等月底入帳的那一瞬間，
才是我們唯一誠實的時刻。

那一刻，
誰也不騙誰，
錢進來，
靈魂出去。

剛剛好。
zh-tw.editorNote: 月初剛上班、滿心期待月底發薪日的時候，傳給同樣靠薪水的紅光撐過每一天的同事
variations: 我不是討厭工作，我只是希望薪水可以每天領一次。
公司給我薪水，我給公司表情，這就是現代職場的等價交換。

en.content:
Someone asked me: "Do you like your job?"

I thought about it carefully, and answered —

"I don't like the verb 'working.'"
"But I'm extremely fond of the noun 'paycheck.'"

So really, we have an arrangement —

I pretend to enjoy this job for eight hours,
the company pretends I'm worth what they're paying me by the hour,
and together we pretend this is a partnership.

The one moment of honesty between us
is when the deposit hits at the end of the month.

In that instant,
nobody is lying.
The money comes in,
the soul goes out.

Fair trade.
en.editorNote: Send at the start of the month when payday already feels like a distant lighthouse — to the coworker also surviving on the glow of the next deposit
variations: I don't hate working. I just wish payday came every day instead of once a month.
The company gives me a salary. I give the company a facial expression. That's the modern workplace exchange rate.

items.sourceNote: 負能量金句參考，重新創作「努力 vs 聰明」主題

zh-tw.content:
他們說：努力就會成功。

但比你聰明的人，早就比你努力了。

比你努力的人，又比你有人脈。

比你有人脈的人，剛好還是老闆的兒子。

你那點努力，
在這條食物鏈上，
只能算晚餐前的開胃菜。
zh-tw.editorNote: 看完勵志雞湯文又被現實打臉時，傳給也在努力打怪但隊友是老闆兒子的同事
variations: 努力是必要條件，運氣是充分條件，背景是隱藏條件。
別人是含著金湯匙出生，我是含著體溫計出生——一不舒服就請假。

en.content:
They say: hard work pays off.

But people smarter than you have been working harder than you for years.

People harder-working than you also have better connections.

People with better connections happen to be the boss's son.

Your effort,
on this food chain,
is basically an appetizer before the real meal.
en.editorNote: Send after closing yet another 'rise and grind' motivational post — to the coworker also fighting the same boss-fight on hard mode
variations: Hard work is necessary. Luck is sufficient. Family background is the cheat code.
Some people were born with a silver spoon. I was born with a thermometer — one cough and I'm calling in sick.

items.sourceNote: 厭世金句參考，重新創作「錢能解決的問題」延伸主題

zh-tw.content:
有人說：「用錢能解決的事，都不是事。」

聽起來很有道理。

直到我發現——

我的問題，
剛好全部都是用錢能解決的事。

房租、帳單、健保、車貸、晚餐。

所以對我來說，
它們不是不是事，
它們是「真的很大條的事」。

會說這句話的人，
通常是已經有錢的人。
zh-tw.editorNote: 月底錢包見底、又看到有錢人在說「錢不是萬能」的時候，傳給也覺得這句話很欠揍的朋友
variations: 錢不是萬能，但沒錢真的萬萬不能，這兩句沒有衝突，是同一件事的不同角度。
說「錢買不到快樂」的人，通常已經買完了。

en.content:
Someone said: "Problems money can solve aren't really problems."

Sounds wise.

Until I realized —

every single one of my problems
happens to be the kind money can solve.

Rent. Bills. Insurance. Car loan. Dinner.

So to me,
they're not 'not really problems.'
They're 'extremely real problems.'

People who say that sentence
usually already have the money.
en.editorNote: Send at month-end when your wallet is empty and some millionaire is on TV explaining that money isn't everything — to the friend who agrees that line deserves a slap
variations: Money isn't everything, but having none of it is everything. Same sentence, two angles.
'Money can't buy happiness' is something said exclusively by people who have already bought it.

items.sourceNote: 經典回應參考，重新創作「拖延症」延伸主題

zh-tw.content:
有人問我：「你會拖延嗎？」

我說：「我不會。」

「我只是把事情排在『等我心情比較好』的那一格，
而那一格——
從來沒有來過。」

所以嚴格來說，
我不是在拖延。

我是在「永久等待最佳狀態」。

這聽起來，
比較像策略，
比較不像逃避。

至少我這樣告訴自己。
zh-tw.editorNote: 明明該寫報告卻一直滑手機、又不想承認自己在拖延的時候，傳給同樣很會自圓其說的朋友
variations: 我不是拖延，我只是在等靈感降臨，順便等地球停止轉動。
deadline 來之前我是文豪，deadline 來了之後我才開始打字。

en.content:
Someone asked me: "Do you procrastinate?"

I said: "No."

"I just file tasks under 'when I'm in a better mood' —
and that mood
has never actually arrived."

So technically,
I'm not procrastinating.

I'm 'permanently awaiting optimal conditions.'

Which sounds
more like a strategy
and less like avoidance.

At least that's what I tell myself.
en.editorNote: Send when you should be writing the report but you're scrolling instead and refusing to call it procrastination — to the friend who's equally gifted at self-justification
variations: I'm not procrastinating. I'm waiting for inspiration, and also for the Earth to stop spinning.
Before the deadline I'm a literary genius. After the deadline arrives, I finally start typing.

items.sourceNote: 經典回應參考，重新創作「我不是不努力，是不想」主題

zh-tw.content:
別人說：「你只是還沒找到熱情。」

我說：「不，我找到了。」

「我的熱情就是——
躺著、滑手機、然後等下班。」

你看，
我熱情十足，
只是社會沒打算付薪水給這種熱情。

問題不是我沒熱情，
問題是我的熱情，
剛好不在市場上有報價。
zh-tw.editorNote: 被親戚問「最近有什麼夢想」又懶得解釋的時候，傳給同樣熱情都用在週末補眠的朋友
variations: 我有熱情，只是我的熱情比較內向，它叫做「想休息」。
找到熱情很重要，但找到願意付錢給你那個熱情的人更重要。

en.content:
Someone said: "You just haven't found your passion yet."

I said: "No, I found it."

"My passion is —
lying down, scrolling my phone, and waiting for clock-out."

See, I'm passionate.

It's just that society has decided
not to pay for this particular kind of passion.

The problem isn't that I lack passion.

The problem is that my passion
doesn't have a market price.
en.editorNote: Send when relatives at dinner ask what your dreams are and you've run out of polite answers — to the friend whose passion also peaks during Sunday naps
variations: I have passion. It's just introverted. It goes by the name 'I'd like to rest now.'
Finding your passion matters. Finding someone willing to pay for that passion matters more.

items.sourceNote: 辦公室幹話與職場諷刺金句參考，重新創作會議主題

zh-tw.content:
這場會議本來可以是一封信。

這封信本來可以是一句話。

那一句話本來可以是「不用開會」。
zh-tw.editorNote: 下次又被拉進無意義會議時，傳給隔壁那個跟你一樣在偷滑手機的同事，會心一笑就夠了
variations: 會議的定義：把一個十分鐘能講完的事，分成五個人各講四十分鐘。
我參加會議的方式，是讓我的臉假裝有在聽，腦袋去別的地方度假。

en.content:
This meeting could have been an email.

That email could have been a sentence.

That sentence could have been: "no meeting needed."
en.editorNote: Send to the coworker sitting next to you in another pointless meeting — a quiet smile across the table is all you need
variations: A meeting: where ten minutes of content gets stretched across five people talking for forty minutes each.
My meeting strategy is letting my face pretend to listen while my brain takes a small vacation elsewhere.

items.sourceNote: 幽默金句與機智回應參考，重新創作成熟與大人主題

zh-tw.content:
別人說：你已經是大人了，要成熟一點。

我說：好，那請告訴我，成熟的大人晚上十一點還想吃鹹酥雞，是要怎麼成熟地處理？
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個一邊罵自己幼稚、一邊在揪你吃宵夜的朋友，互相確認彼此都是合格的不成熟大人
variations: 成熟的大人，就是學會在崩潰前先繳完信用卡費。
我是大人。我只是選擇用大人的薪水，買小孩才會買的東西。

en.content:
Someone said: you're an adult now, act mature.

I said: okay, then tell me how a mature adult is supposed to maturely handle wanting fried chicken at eleven at night.
en.editorNote: Send to the friend who calls themselves childish while also texting you about late-night snacks — mutual confirmation that you're both perfectly qualified immature adults
variations: A mature adult is someone who learned to pay the credit card bill before having the breakdown.
I am an adult. I just choose to spend my adult salary on things only a child would buy.

items.sourceNote: 上班族諷刺金句參考，重新創作星期一主題

zh-tw.content:
有人問：你最討厭一週中的哪一天？

我說：所有跟星期一有血緣關係的日子。

包括星期二、星期三、星期四，

還有「明天是星期一」的星期日晚上。
zh-tw.editorNote: 週日晚上突然心情變差時拿出來用，傳給那個跟你一起等待週末復活的同事
variations: 星期一不可怕，可怕的是它每七天就會回來找我一次。
我跟星期一的關係，就像兩個被迫一起住的室友——我們互相討厭，但不得不見面。

en.content:
Someone asked: which day of the week do you hate most?

I said: every day related to Monday by blood.

That includes Tuesday, Wednesday, Thursday,

and the Sunday evening that whispers "tomorrow is Monday."
en.editorNote: Pull this out when your Sunday-evening mood collapses on schedule — send to the coworker who's also waiting for the weekend to come back to life
variations: Monday isn't scary. What's scary is that it comes back to find me every seven days, reliably.
My relationship with Monday is like two roommates forced to share an apartment — we hate each other but we have to meet.

items.sourceNote: 機智回應與幽默金句參考，重新創作建議與雞湯主題

zh-tw.content:
有人說：你只是還沒走出舒適圈，所以才不會成長。

我說：可以。但請你先告訴我，我的舒適圈在哪裡，

因為我目前住的這個區域，房租很貴、工時很長、睡眠很短，

一點都不舒適。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合傳給那種愛轉發成功學文章來「鼓勵」你的長輩或前同事，溫和但堅定地讓他閉嘴
variations: 走出舒適圈是好事，但前提是——你要先有舒適圈。
我的舒適圈早就被房租、工作、生活費聯手拆掉了，現在我住在「將就圈」。

en.content:
Someone said: you just haven't stepped out of your comfort zone, that's why you're not growing.

I said: sure. But first, please tell me where my comfort zone is,

because the zone I currently live in has expensive rent, long hours, and very little sleep,

and it isn't comfortable at all.
en.editorNote: Send to the relative or former coworker who keeps forwarding hustle-culture articles to 'motivate' you — polite but firm enough to make them stop
variations: Stepping out of your comfort zone is great. The prerequisite is that you must first have a comfort zone.
My comfort zone was dismantled long ago by rent, work, and bills working together. I now live in the 'making-do zone.'

items.sourceNote: 未經請求建議的回應金句參考，重新創作建議轟炸主題

zh-tw.content:
有人說：我這是為你好，給你一點建議。

我說：謝謝。但我的人生不是你的建議收集箱，

而且老實說，你的建議是免費的，

但對我來說還是太貴。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合傳給總是「我跟你說啦」開頭、但其實沒人問他的長輩或同事，溫柔但有效地畫出界線
variations: 你的建議是免費的，但聽完我覺得我虧到了。
我沒問你，是因為我不需要。如果哪天我需要，我會記得不要問你。

en.content:
Someone said: I'm telling you this for your own good — just a little advice.

I said: thanks. But my life isn't your suggestion box,

and honestly, your advice is free,

yet somehow still overpriced.
en.editorNote: Send to the relative or coworker who opens every conversation with 'let me tell you something' even though nobody asked — a gentle but effective boundary
variations: Your advice is free, and yet somehow I feel like I lost money listening to it.
I didn't ask, because I didn't need to. If I ever do, I'll remember not to ask you.

items.sourceNote: 經典機智回應參考，重新創作那是你的問題主題

zh-tw.content:
有人說：你這樣讓我很難過耶。

我說：嗯，那聽起來，

是一個非常屬於你的問題。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合那種動不動就把責任丟給你的朋友或前任，溫和地把鍋還回去
variations: 你的情緒是你的，不是我的KPI。
「你害我難過」這句話，邏輯上跟「你害我下雨」差不多。

en.content:
Someone said: this is really making me upset, you know.

I said: hmm. That sounds like

a very you-shaped problem.
en.editorNote: Use on the friend or ex who keeps dumping responsibility on your lap — politely hand the feelings back where they belong
variations: Your emotions are yours. They are not on my performance review.
'You're making me sad' has roughly the same logic as 'you're making it rain.'

items.sourceNote: 負能量厭世金句參考，重新創作努力與運氣主題

zh-tw.content:
有人說：只要努力，就一定會成功。

我說：對。

但努力的人那麼多，成功的位子那麼少，

剩下的人會被歸類成：「努力過了，但運氣不好。」

而通常，那就是我們。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合那種被主管要你「再加把勁」的時刻，自己默默打開記事本看一眼就好
variations: 努力一定會有回報——只是回報的對象不一定是你。
成功的人說努力很重要，是因為他們已經成功了。失敗的人說努力很重要，是因為他們不知道還能說什麼。

en.content:
Someone said: if you just work hard enough, you'll succeed.

I said: sure.

But there are a lot of people working hard and very few seats at the top,

and the rest of us get filed under 'tried hard, bad luck.'

And usually, that's us.
en.editorNote: For the moment your manager tells you to 'push a little harder' — open your notes app, read this, close it, and carry on
variations: Hard work always pays off. The payoff just doesn't always go to you.
Successful people say hard work matters because they already made it. Unsuccessful people say it because they don't know what else to say.

items.sourceNote: 幽默回應參考，重新創作社交電量主題

zh-tw.content:
有人問：你為什麼都不太參加聚會？是不是不喜歡我們？

我說：不是不喜歡你們，

是我每次跟人類相處超過兩小時，

隔天就會需要一個完整的週末來重新開機。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個老是揪你去人多場合的朋友，讓他知道你不是討厭他，只是電池容量小
variations: 我不是不合群，我是低耗電模式。
我喜歡你們，只是分批次喜歡，一次一個人那種。

en.content:
Someone asked: why do you never come to our gatherings? Do you not like us?

I said: it's not that I don't like you.

It's that every time I spend more than two hours around humans,

I need an entire weekend to reboot.
en.editorNote: Send to the friend who keeps inviting you to crowded events — they should know it isn't personal, your battery is just small
variations: I'm not antisocial. I'm just running on low-power mode.
I do like you all. I just like you in batches — one person at a time.

items.sourceNote: 成人生活諷刺一句話參考，重新創作待辦清單主題

zh-tw.content:
有人問：你的待辦清單為什麼從去年寫到現在？

我說：因為「等一下再做」是一種生活哲學，

而我，是這個流派的代表人物。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個老是看你待辦清單還沒劃掉、就忍不住關心你的朋友，順便宣示主權
variations: 我不是拖延，我是讓事情先在腦中熟成。
待辦清單不是要做完的，是要陪我一起變老的。

en.content:
Someone asked: why is your to-do list from last year still going?

I said: because 'I'll do it later' is a lifestyle philosophy,

and I happen to be its leading scholar.
en.editorNote: Send to the friend who keeps side-eyeing your unfinished to-do list — politely reclaim your right to procrastinate
variations: I'm not procrastinating. I'm letting the task marinate in my brain.
A to-do list isn't meant to be finished. It's meant to grow old with me.

items.sourceNote: 成人生活幽默語錄參考，重新創作假期幻想主題

zh-tw.content:
公司說：我們重視員工的工作與生活平衡。

翻譯：我們希望你下班後也能想到工作，

上班時也能稍微想一下你還有生活。
zh-tw.editorNote: 週日晚上躺床上滑手機的時候傳給同事，比任何安慰都療癒
variations: 工作與生活平衡是一個美麗的詞，意思是兩邊都顧不好，但兩邊都覺得你不夠努力。
我需要一個放六個月的長假，一年放兩次，這樣才公平。

en.content:
The company says: we value work-life balance.

Translation: we'd like you to think about work after hours,

and briefly remember you have a life during work hours.
en.editorNote: Send to a coworker on Sunday night while you're both doomscrolling in bed — more healing than any pep talk
variations: Work-life balance is a beautiful phrase meaning you fail at both, and both sides think you're not trying hard enough.
I need a six-month vacation, twice a year. For fairness.

items.sourceNote: 經典幽默回應參考，重新創作建議與意見主題

zh-tw.content:
有人說：我給你一個建議，是為你好。

我說：謝謝，請放在那邊，

旁邊那一疊「為我好」已經堆到天花板了。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個動不動就「我這是為你好」的長輩或朋友，禮貌但堅定地畫一條線
variations: 為我好的建議我收下，要不要照做是另一回事。
別人的建議是參考資料，不是工作指派。這一點要分清楚。

en.content:
Someone said: I'm giving you this advice for your own good.

I replied: thanks, just leave it over there.

The pile labeled 'for my own good' already reaches the ceiling.
en.editorNote: Send to the relative or friend who opens every sentence with 'I'm just saying this because I care' — polite but firm boundary
variations: I'll accept advice given for my own good. Whether I act on it is a separate question.
Other people's advice is reference material, not a work assignment. Important distinction.

items.sourceNote: 靠北語錄參考，重新創作早起與咖啡主題

zh-tw.content:
有人說：早起的鳥兒有蟲吃。

我說：那是因為晚起的人類有外送，

而且我比較想當人類，謝謝。
