zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個一直叫你早睡早起的養生派朋友，讓他知道你有自己的生存策略
variations: 早起的鳥有蟲吃，早起的我有黑眼圈。物種不同，待遇不同。
我不是不能早起，我是覺得早起這件事，應該由咖啡先示範一次給我看。

en.content:
Someone said: the early bird gets the worm.

I said: yes, but the late human gets delivery,

and I'd rather be the human, thanks.
en.editorNote: Send to the wellness-evangelist friend who keeps preaching early-to-bed-early-to-rise — let them know you have your own survival plan
variations: The early bird gets the worm. The early me gets dark circles. Different species, different rewards.
I'm not incapable of waking up early. I just think coffee should demonstrate it for me first.

items.sourceNote: 上班族厭世語錄參考，重新創作努力與加薪主題

zh-tw.content:
主管說：只要你夠努力，公司一定看得見。

我說：好，那我努力把履歷投完，

讓別家公司也看得見。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個被畫大餅畫到血糖過高的同事，提醒他努力的方向可以自己選
variations: 公司說會看見我的努力，結果只看見我準時打卡，沒看見我加班到幾點。
努力會被看見是真的，只是先被看見的通常是隔壁那個會說話的。

en.content:
My boss said: work hard enough and the company will notice.

I said: great, then I'll work hard on finishing my resume,

so other companies notice too.
en.editorNote: Send to the coworker who's been promised a raise 'soon' for the third year running — a reminder that hard work has more than one destination
variations: They said they'd notice my effort. Turns out they only noticed my clock-in time, not how late I stayed.
Hard work does get noticed. It's just that the smooth talker next to you usually gets noticed first.

items.sourceNote: 經典幽默回應參考，重新創作減肥與自律主題

zh-tw.content:
有人說：減肥很簡單，少吃多動就好。

我說：對，賺錢也很簡單，多收少付就好，

怎麼大家都沒做到。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個一邊吃鹹酥雞一邊說明天開始減肥的朋友，互相取暖最實在
variations: 我的減肥計畫很完整，唯一的問題是它從明天開始，而明天永遠在明天。
少吃多動我都懂，我只是把「多動」理解成多動腦想要吃什麼。

en.content:
Someone said: losing weight is easy, just eat less and move more.

I said: sure, and getting rich is easy too, just earn more and spend less.

Funny how nobody's pulling that off either.
en.editorNote: Send to the friend who announces a diet between bites of fried chicken — solidarity is the real comfort food
variations: My weight-loss plan is flawless. The only flaw is that it starts tomorrow, and tomorrow keeps being tomorrow.
Eat less, move more — got it. I just interpreted 'move more' as moving my brain harder to decide what to eat.

items.sourceNote: 靠北語錄參考，重新創作存錢與小確幸主題

zh-tw.content:
理財專家說：每天省一杯咖啡，三十年後就能買房。

我說：那我喝完這杯，

順便算一下三十年後那間房會在哪一個縣市的山上。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個一直叫你別亂花錢的長輩或同事，溫柔提醒物價漲得比存款快
variations: 省一杯咖啡存下來的錢，遇到房價就像把一桶水倒進大海，撲通一聲就不見了。
我有努力存錢，只是我的存款和房價在賽跑，而它沒在等我。

en.content:
A finance guru said: skip one coffee a day and in thirty years you can buy a house.

I said: cool, let me finish this cup

and calculate which mountain that house will be on in thirty years.
en.editorNote: Send to the relative or coworker who keeps lecturing you about spending — a gentle nudge that prices climb faster than savings
variations: The money you save by skipping coffee, next to housing prices, is like pouring a bucket into the ocean — plop, gone.
I am saving money. It's just that my savings and housing prices are in a race, and the prices aren't waiting for me.

items.sourceNote: 幹話語錄參考，重新創作休息與罪惡感主題

zh-tw.content:
有人說：休息是為了走更長遠的路。

我說：我同意，所以我打算先休息到，

搞清楚這條路到底要走去哪裡為止。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個放假還在回工作訊息、休息一下就有罪惡感的朋友，准許他真的躺平一次
variations: 休息是為了走更長遠的路，但沒人規定那條路一定要今天就出發。
我不是在偷懶，我是在替我的人生做策略性暫停，聽起來就專業多了。

en.content:
Someone said: rest so you can go the distance.

I said: agreed, so I plan to rest until

I figure out where this distance is even supposed to lead.
en.editorNote: Send to the friend who answers work messages on vacation and feels guilty after ten minutes of doing nothing — official permission to actually lie down
variations: Rest so you can go the distance — but nobody said the distance has to start today.
I'm not being lazy. I'm taking a strategic pause for my life. Sounds way more professional.

items.sourceNote: 社畜厭世語錄參考，重新創作上班與希望主題

zh-tw.content:
他們說：每天都要充滿希望。

好，我充滿希望。

我希望快點下班。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個週一早上就在群組哀號的同事，告訴他你懂，而且你的希望很具體
variations: 我對人生還是有期待的，我期待的是五點半那一刻。
別說我沒有夢想，我的夢想就是這個會議趕快結束。

en.content:
They say: greet each day full of hope.

Fine, I'm full of hope.

I hope I can clock out early.
en.editorNote: Send to the coworker already groaning in the group chat on Monday morning — let them know your hope is very specific
variations: I still have expectations for life. My expectation is the exact moment it hits 5:30.
Don't say I have no dreams. My dream is for this meeting to end.

items.sourceNote: 英文諷刺語錄參考，重新創作努力與天賦主題

zh-tw.content:
有人說：比你聰明的人都比你努力，那你還有什麼資格偷懶。

我說：對啊，所以我決定保留實力，

免得贏太多沒朋友。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個老是拿別人比較來激勵你的長輩，用幽默的方式擋掉沒必要的壓力
variations: 我不是不努力，我只是在替那些需要成就感的人保留位置。
天才靠的是百分之一的靈感，剩下百分之九十九我打算先睡飽再說。

en.content:
Someone said: people smarter than you work harder, so what gives you the right to slack off?

I said: exactly, that's why I'm holding back

so I don't win so much I lose all my friends.
en.editorNote: Send to the relative who motivates you by comparing you to others — deflect unnecessary pressure with a grin
variations: I'm not unmotivated. I'm just saving the spotlight for people who need the validation more than I do.
Genius is one percent inspiration. The other ninety-nine percent, I'll handle after a nap.

items.sourceNote: 幽默勵志語錄參考，重新創作運動與藉口主題

zh-tw.content:
朋友問我有沒有在運動。

有啊，我每天都做一種結合伸展跟收縮的動作。

我叫它「吃午餐」。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個一直揪你去健身房的朋友，用一句話交代你的「運動計畫」
variations: 我不是懶，我只是開了節能模式，省下來的力氣要留著吃飯。
我有規律運動，每天都把手機從這隻手換到另一隻手。

en.content:
A friend asked if I work out.

Sure do. Every day I do a move that blends a stretch and a contraction.

I call it lunch.
en.editorNote: Send to the friend who keeps dragging you to the gym — sum up your entire 'fitness routine' in one line
variations: I'm not lazy, I'm in energy-saving mode. The power I save goes toward eating.
I exercise regularly. Every day I move my phone from one hand to the other.

items.sourceNote: 職場厭世金句參考，重新創作辦公室與工作主題

zh-tw.content:
主管說：把公司當成自己的家。

好的，那我家我想睡到幾點就幾點，

而且不會有人扣我薪水。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合傳給剛被主管畫大餅的朋友，幫他把那句話的邏輯漏洞戳破
variations: 把公司當家可以，但我家不會在月底才匯薪水給我。
人在辦公室，心已經在辦離職。

en.content:
My boss said: treat the company like it's your own home.

Great, then at home I sleep in as late as I want,

and nobody docks my pay for it.
en.editorNote: Send to the friend whose boss just fed them a 'we're a family here' speech — poke a hole in the logic for them
variations: Happy to treat the company like home — but my home doesn't wait until the last day of the month to pay me.
Body's at the office. The mind already filed the resignation.

items.sourceNote: 幽默語錄參考，重新創作動力與洗澡主題

zh-tw.content:
有人安慰我：動力這種東西本來就維持不久。

我說：洗澡的乾淨也維持不久，

但我們還是建議你每天都洗。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個總說「我就是提不起勁」的朋友，提醒他有些事不靠心情靠習慣
variations: 動力會消失沒關係，反正紀律本來就是用來補位的。
我不是沒動力，我只是把動力外包給了截止日期。

en.content:
Someone consoled me: motivation never really lasts anyway.

I said: neither does being clean after a shower,

yet we still recommend doing it daily.
en.editorNote: Send to the friend who keeps saying 'I just can't get motivated' — a reminder that some things run on habit, not mood
variations: It's fine that motivation fades. That's literally what discipline is there to cover.
I'm not unmotivated. I just outsourced my motivation to the deadline.

items.sourceNote: 厭世幹話語錄參考（睡得跟豬一樣梗），重新創作睡眠與起床主題

zh-tw.content:
有人說我每天睡得跟豬一樣。

這話我不能接受，

因為豬天還沒亮就起來工作，

而我連鬧鐘響五次都還在跟它談條件。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個被家人嫌睡太晚的朋友，幫他把罪惡感轉成笑話
variations: 我不是睡太多，我只是在用睡眠對抗這個世界的物價。
鬧鐘響的時候，我跟它的關係正式從鬧鐘變成室友——一個吵，一個不想理。

en.content:
Someone said I sleep like a pig every day.

I can't accept that,

because a pig is up working before sunrise,

while I'm still negotiating with my fifth alarm.
en.editorNote: Send to the friend who gets nagged for sleeping in — turns the guilt into a punchline
variations: I'm not oversleeping. I'm using sleep to fight back against inflation.
When the alarm goes off, our relationship officially shifts from alarm-and-me to roommates — one of us is loud, the other isn't interested.

items.sourceNote: 諷刺生活語錄參考（energy-saving mode梗），重新創作懶惰與省電主題

zh-tw.content:
我不是懶，

我只是開了節能模式。

而你不知道的是，

這是成年人的原廠預設值。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個老是說自己「應該要更勤勞」的朋友，幫他放過自己一馬
variations: 我沒有不動，我只是把電量留給真正重要的事——比如晚上要追的劇。
別人是充飽電出門，我是充飽電躺好。

en.content:
I'm not lazy.

I'm just in energy-saving mode.

What you don't realize is,

that's the factory default for being an adult.
en.editorNote: Send to the friend who keeps insisting they 'should be more productive' — permission to cut themselves some slack
variations: I'm not doing nothing. I'm reserving battery for the things that matter — like the show I'm bingeing tonight.
Some people charge up to go out. I charge up to lie down properly.

items.sourceNote: 成年人幹話語錄參考（deal with it later梗），重新創作拖延主題

zh-tw.content:
當大人就是一直說「之後再處理」，

直到「之後」變成了你的一整個人生。

我現在的待辦清單，

已經有資格申請古蹟保存了。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給跟你一樣把代辦清單當傳家寶在養的朋友，互相取暖用
variations: 我不是拖延，我只是在給每件事一個慢慢成熟的機會。
待辦清單上最久的那一項，已經陪我換過三支手機了。

en.content:
Adulting is just saying 'I'll deal with it later'

until 'later' becomes your entire life.

My to-do list at this point

qualifies for heritage preservation.
en.editorNote: Send to the friend who's been growing their to-do list like a family heirloom — mutual moral support
variations: I'm not procrastinating. I'm giving each task a chance to mature slowly.
The oldest item on my to-do list has outlived three of my phones.

items.sourceNote: 諷刺語錄參考（3AM regrets梗），重新創作焦慮與睡眠主題

zh-tw.content:
我不需要鬧鐘，

因為我的後悔每天凌晨三點都會準時把我叫醒。

而且它比鬧鐘敬業，

從來不會貪睡。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個半夜睡不著、腦袋一直自動播放黑歷史的朋友，懂的人秒懂
variations: 白天負責假裝沒事，凌晨三點負責結算今天的帳。
我的失眠不是因為咖啡，是因為我十年前說過的某一句話又上線了。

en.content:
I don't need an alarm clock,

because my regrets wake me up at 3 AM, right on schedule.

And they're more dedicated than any alarm —

they never hit snooze.
en.editorNote: Send to the friend who lies awake at night while their brain auto-plays the highlight reel of embarrassing memories
variations: Daytime me handles pretending I'm fine. 3 AM me handles settling the day's accounts.
My insomnia isn't from caffeine. It's from something I said ten years ago coming back online.

items.sourceNote: 成年人幹話語錄參考（winging it / fake competence梗），重新創作職場假裝主題

zh-tw.content:
成熟的職場大人，

就是一邊上網查怎麼做，

一邊發出非常有自信的信，

同時祈禱沒有人發現我其實也是邊做邊查。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個剛接了新任務、嘴上說沒問題其實在狂查資料的同事，互相心照不宣
variations: 所謂專業，就是把「我不會」翻譯成「我來研究一下」的能力。
我不是在裝懂，我是在表演到自己真的懂為止。

en.content:
A seasoned working adult

is just someone googling how to do the thing

while sending extremely confident emails,

praying nobody notices they're winging it too.
en.editorNote: Send to the coworker who just took on a new task and said 'no problem' while frantically googling — a knowing nod
variations: Professionalism is the ability to translate 'I have no idea' into 'let me look into that.'
I'm not faking competence. I'm performing it until it accidentally becomes real.

items.sourceNote: 成年人幹話語錄參考（meeting could've been an email梗），重新創作開會主題

zh-tw.content:
如果每一場「其實寄信就好」的會議都能換五十塊，

我現在已經坐在海邊，

看著我那些還在開會的同事，

為我祈福。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個剛從一個小時的會議走出來、發現結論一句話就能講完的同事
variations: 這場會議唯一的產出，就是決定下次再開一場會。
開會的意義，是讓大家一起看著時間，被慢慢地花掉。

en.content:
If I got fifty cents for every meeting that could've been an email,

I'd already be sitting on a beach

watching my still-in-meetings coworkers

send good thoughts my way.
en.editorNote: Send to the coworker who just walked out of an hour-long meeting whose only conclusion fits in one sentence
variations: The only deliverable from that meeting was the decision to schedule another meeting.
The point of meetings is to let everyone watch the time get spent together.

items.sourceNote: 諷刺語錄參考（keeping it together while falling apart梗），重新創作假裝穩定主題

zh-tw.content:
大人的世界，

就是一邊在裡面崩潰，

一邊在外面維持很有條理的樣子，

而且兩件事都做得很熟練。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個表面雲淡風輕、內心其實在跑馬燈的朋友，讓他知道你看穿了但不拆穿
variations: 我的穩定，是用很多看不見的螺絲勉強鎖在一起的。
別人問我還好嗎，我都回「還行」，但「還行」其實有十七種意思。

en.content:
Being an adult

is just falling apart on the inside

while looking very organized on the outside,

and somehow getting really good at both.
en.editorNote: Send to the friend who looks calm on the surface while their brain runs a marathon — let them know you see it but won't call it out
variations: My stability is held together by a lot of invisible screws.
When people ask if I'm okay, I say 'I'm fine' — but 'I'm fine' has seventeen different meanings.

items.sourceNote: 負能量幹話語錄參考（口袋鈔票顏色決定心情顏色梗），重新創作金錢與心情主題

zh-tw.content:
他們說錢買不到快樂。

對，因為我根本還沒走到買得起的那一格，

就在「房租」那關被卡住了。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個月底在算錢、被人勸「錢不是萬能」的朋友，幫他回嘴
variations: 錢買不到快樂，但至少能買到讓我假裝沒事的那杯飲料。
我不是物質，我只是發現帳戶餘額真的會影響我看世界的角度。

en.content:
They say money can't buy happiness.

Sure — but I never even reached the part where I could afford to test that,

because I got stuck back at the 'rent' level.
en.editorNote: Send to the friend who's counting coins at month's end while someone lectures them that 'money isn't everything'
variations: Money can't buy happiness, but it can buy the drink that helps me pretend I'm fine.
I'm not materialistic. I've just noticed my bank balance genuinely affects how I see the world.

items.sourceNote: 社畜厭世金句參考（今天也充滿希望，希望快點下班梗），重新創作上班期待主題

zh-tw.content:
今天的我也充滿了希望，

希望快點下班，

希望明天放假，

希望老闆突然發現公司其實不需要開會。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給每天進公司就開始倒數下班的同事，週一早上發特別有共鳴
variations: 我對工作是有熱情的，熱情地等它結束。
上班最大的動力，就是知道它總有一天會下班。

en.content:
Today I'm full of hope too —

hope that it's clock-out time soon,

hope that tomorrow's a day off,

hope my boss suddenly realizes we don't actually need meetings.
en.editorNote: Send to the coworker who starts counting down to clock-out the moment they arrive — extra relatable on a Monday morning
variations: I'm passionate about my job — passionately waiting for it to end.
My biggest motivation at work is knowing it eventually clocks out too.

items.sourceNote: 「老闆用提醒房租到期來激勵我」梗參考，重新創作上班動力主題

zh-tw.content:
同事問我，每天上班的動力到底是什麼？

我說，是房東。

他每個月都很準時地提醒我，這個月還沒結束。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個老是說「找份有熱情的工作」的長輩，順便讓他幫你出房租
variations: 我不是為夢想而工作，我是為了月底那筆轉帳不要失敗而工作。
激勵我每天起床的不是太陽，是還沒繳的卡費。

en.content:
A coworker asked what keeps me motivated to show up every day.

I said: my landlord.

He reminds me very punctually each month that the month isn't over yet.
en.editorNote: Send to the relative who keeps telling you to 'find work you're passionate about' — then ask them to cover the rent
variations: I don't work for the dream. I work so the auto-payment on the 28th doesn't bounce.
What gets me out of bed isn't the sunrise. It's the credit card bill I haven't paid.

items.sourceNote: 「成年就是一堆結局都通向繳稅的選擇」梗參考，重新創作成年無奈主題

zh-tw.content:
小時候以為長大就自由了。

長大後才發現，所謂自由，就是可以自由選擇要先繳哪一筆帳單。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給剛出社會、還在幻想「賺錢就能爽花」的學弟妹，給他們一點溫柔的現實
variations: 成年人的快樂很簡單，就是這個月帳單繳完還剩一點點。
長大不是解鎖更多選項，是解鎖更多帳單。

en.content:
As a kid I thought growing up meant freedom.

Turns out freedom just means you get to freely choose which bill to pay first.
en.editorNote: Send to the new grad still dreaming that 'a paycheck means I can finally spend freely' — a gentle reality check
variations: Adult happiness is simple: there's a little left after this month's bills.
Growing up didn't unlock more options. It unlocked more invoices.

items.sourceNote: 「會議是生產力打盹的地方」梗參考，重新創作開會無意義主題

zh-tw.content:
今天開了一個三小時的會，

結論是：下週再開一個會討論今天為什麼要開這個會。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給每天被會議塞滿行事曆、卻一件事都推不動的同事，會議室裡偷傳特別解氣
variations: 開會就是一群人坐在一起，集體確認大家都不知道接下來要幹嘛。
這個會其實一封信就能解決，但我們選擇了犧牲三個小時的人生。

en.content:
Sat through a three-hour meeting today.

The conclusion: we'll schedule another meeting next week to discuss why we had this one.
en.editorNote: Send to the coworker whose calendar is all meetings and zero progress — extra satisfying texted from inside the conference room
variations: A meeting is just a group of people gathering to collectively confirm nobody knows what's next.
This could've been an email, but we chose to sacrifice three hours of our lives instead.

items.sourceNote: 「靠咖啡、諷刺和沒解決的創傷運轉」梗參考，重新創作社畜續命主題

zh-tw.content:
有人問我都靠什麼撐過每一天。

我想了想說：咖啡、嘴硬，還有一點沒處理好的童年。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個早上沒咖啡就無法做人的同事，午休沒精神時互相取暖用
variations: 我不是早起的人，我是被咖啡硬撈起來的人。
支撐我活下去的三大支柱：咖啡因、截止日，還有不甘心。

en.content:
Someone asked what gets me through each day.

I thought about it and said: coffee, stubbornness, and a little childhood I never quite dealt with.
en.editorNote: Send to the coworker who can't function before their morning coffee — perfect for the mutual 3 p.m. slump
variations: I'm not a morning person. I'm a person caffeine drags upright by force.
My three load-bearing pillars: caffeine, deadlines, and pure spite.

items.sourceNote: 「成年就是一直說等等再處理直到死掉」梗參考，重新創作拖延與長大主題

zh-tw.content:
長大到底是什麼？

就是把每一件煩人的事都標上「之後再說」，然後一路標到躺進棺材那天。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個待辦清單越拉越長、卻一件都不想動的朋友，互相承認大家都一樣廢
variations: 我的人生規劃很完整：今天的事明天做，明天的事看明天的心情。
成年人的超能力，是把焦慮無限期延後，還能假裝自己很冷靜。

en.content:
What is being a grown-up, really?

It's tagging every annoying task with 'I'll deal with it later' — and keeping that streak going right into the coffin.
en.editorNote: Send to the friend whose to-do list keeps growing while nothing gets crossed off — mutual permission to feel like a mess
variations: My life plan is airtight: today's tasks get done tomorrow, tomorrow's depend on my mood.
An adult's superpower is postponing anxiety indefinitely while pretending to be calm.

items.sourceNote: 「我沒破產，我只是重金投資外送和爛決定」梗參考，重新創作財務與消費主題

zh-tw.content:
我不是沒錢，

我只是把錢大量投資在外送、手搖，還有一連串想起來會後悔的決定上。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給每個月薪水入帳當天就見底的同類，月底互相取暖時最有共鳴
variations: 我的存款和我的自尊共用一個餘額：都低到讓人擔心。
理財專家叫我開源節流，我做到了一半——很節制地一直流出去。

en.content:
I'm not broke.

I've simply made heavy investments in delivery apps, bubble tea, and a long string of decisions I'll regret later.
en.editorNote: Send to the friend whose paycheck evaporates the day it lands — peak relatability at the end of the month
variations: My savings and my self-esteem share one balance: both alarmingly low.
A finance guru told me to cut spending and grow income. I nailed half of it — money flows out very consistently.

items.sourceNote: 「我不需要鬧鐘，凌晨三點的後悔會叫醒我」梗參考，重新創作睡眠與焦慮主題

zh-tw.content:
別人問我都怎麼準時起床。

我說不用鬧鐘，凌晨三點的那些後悔，會很盡責地把我搖醒。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個半夜睡不著、腦袋一直重播糗事的朋友，深夜時段送上特別貼切
variations: 我沒有失眠，我只是在認真完成今晚的回憶反芻行程。
睡覺前我會躺著想十年前說錯的那句話，免費又準時的失眠服務。

en.content:
People ask how I always wake up on time.

I tell them I skip the alarm — my 3 a.m. regrets shake me awake right on schedule.
en.editorNote: Send to the friend who lies awake replaying every embarrassing moment — lands extra hard in the late-night hours
variations: I don't have insomnia. I'm just diligently completing tonight's scheduled memory rewind.
Before bed I lie there replaying a line I botched ten years ago — free, punctual insomnia service.

items.sourceNote: 「我運動就是運氣用盡和跳到結論」梗參考，重新創作健身與運動藉口主題

zh-tw.content:
有人問我平常有沒有在運動。

有啊，我每天勤練「跳到結論」和「拖延式深蹲」——蹲在沙發上，深深地拖延。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個辦了健身房會員、去三次就改線上續費的朋友，運動季節限定嘴他
variations: 我的有氧運動是趕公車，重訓是把外送拎上五樓。
健身房會員費是我每個月固定的贖罪券，繳了就當有去過。

en.content:
Someone asked if I work out.

Oh, definitely — I train daily in 'jumping to conclusions' and 'procrasti-squats': squatting on the couch, deeply putting things off.
en.editorNote: Send to the friend who bought a gym membership, went three times, then auto-renews forever — seasonal roast material
variations: My cardio is sprinting for the bus; my strength training is hauling delivery bags up five flights.
My gym membership is a monthly indulgence I pay to absolve the guilt of never going.

items.sourceNote: 「發薪日前看餘額最謙卑」梗參考，重新創作薪水與帳單主題

zh-tw.content:
薪水入帳那天，我是全公司最有錢的人。

隔天帳單一扣，我又恢復成需要被社會關懷的弱勢族群。
zh-tw.editorNote: 每個月薪水入帳當天傳到群組，跟同事一起體驗從天堂到地獄只要二十四小時
variations: 我的薪水很守時，它準時來，也準時離開，從不留戀。
發薪日前一天看銀行餘額，是我每個月最謙卑的時刻。

en.content:
On payday I'm the richest person in the office.

The next morning the bills clear and I'm back to being a charity case in need of social support.
en.editorNote: Drop in the group chat the day your salary lands — share the 24-hour journey from heaven to broke with your coworkers
variations: My paycheck is very punctual: it arrives on time and leaves on time, no sentiment involved.
Checking my bank balance the day before payday is my most humbling moment each month.

items.sourceNote: 「成年就是知道所有東西多貴卻還是照買」梗參考，重新創作成年與消費主題

zh-tw.content:
長大最殘忍的地方，就是你清楚知道每樣東西多貴。

然後還是照買，因為不買日子也不會比較好過。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合傳給那個一邊喊著好貴、一邊把東西放進購物車結帳的朋友，懂的都懂
variations: 小時候以為長大就能想買什麼買什麼，長大後發現是想買什麼都先算一下房租。
成年人的犒賞自己，就是買那條貴五塊的吐司。

en.content:
The cruelest part of growing up is knowing exactly how much everything costs.

And buying it anyway, because not buying it won't make your day any better.
en.editorNote: Send to the friend who yells 'so expensive' while adding it to cart and checking out — we've all been there
variations: As a kid I thought adulthood meant buying whatever I wanted. Turns out it means doing rent math first.
An adult's idea of 'treating yourself' is buying the bread that costs five bucks more.

items.sourceNote: 「努力不一定成功，不努力一定很輕鬆」梗參考，重新創作努力與躺平主題

zh-tw.content:
他們說：努力不一定會成功。

我說：對，但不努力一定很輕鬆，這個我已經親自驗證過很多年了。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個老是被主管喊話要拚一點的同事，幫他把心聲翻譯成幹話
variations: 我不是不努力，我只是把努力留到下輩子比較有把握的時候用。
比我聰明的人都比我努力了，那我努力到底是要爭什麼？讓一下他們。

en.content:
They say hard work doesn't guarantee success.

True. But doing nothing guarantees a very relaxing time — I've personally verified this for years.
en.editorNote: Send to the coworker who keeps getting the 'hustle harder' speech from the boss — translate their inner voice into a comeback
variations: It's not that I don't try. I'm just saving my effort for a future life where the odds look better.
People smarter than me already work harder than me — so what exactly am I competing for? Let them have it.

items.sourceNote: 「為什麼還在繳帳單，只是鼓勵他們寄更多」梗參考，重新創作帳單主題

zh-tw.content:
我認真想過要不要繼續繳帳單。

後來決定不繳了——因為我一繳，他們就以為我喜歡，下個月又寄一張過來。
zh-tw.editorNote: 帳單堆在桌上不想面對的時候傳給自己，至少能笑著逃避一下
variations: 帳單是我這輩子最忠實的訂閱制，我從來沒按過續訂，它卻月月準時送達。
信箱裡唯一還在乎我的，只有帳單和催繳簡訊。

en.content:
I've seriously considered whether to keep paying my bills.

Decided to stop — because every time I pay, they assume I enjoy it and send another one next month.
en.editorNote: Text this to yourself when the bills are piling up on the table and you can't face them — at least you can avoid them with a laugh
variations: Bills are the most loyal subscription of my life: I never hit renew, yet they arrive punctually every month.
The only things in my inbox that still care about me are bills and overdue payment reminders.

items.sourceNote: 「能者多勞／能者過勞」職場厭世梗參考，重新創作能力與責任主題

zh-tw.content:
老闆說：能者多勞。

我懂了——意思是只要我表現得夠廢，事情自然會分給別人。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合傳給那個事情永遠做不完、卻一直被誇「你最可靠」的同事，提醒他可靠是會被剝削的
variations: 能者多勞的下一句不是升遷，是過勞，這點公司從來沒寫在合約裡。
被誇能力強的當下要小心，那通常是更多工作正在路上的前兆。

en.content:
My boss says: capable people get more work.

Now I get it — if I act useless enough, the work naturally goes to someone else.
en.editorNote: Send to the coworker who's drowning in tasks but keeps getting told 'you're so reliable' — remind them reliability gets exploited
variations: The sequel to 'capable people get more work' isn't a promotion — it's burnout. The company just forgot to put that in the contract.
When they praise how capable you are, brace yourself: more work is usually already on its way.

items.sourceNote: 「I love deadlines, the whooshing noise they make」梗參考，重新創作截止日主題

zh-tw.content:
我其實很愛截止日。

尤其是它咻一聲從我身邊飛過去、消失在遠方的那一刻，特別療癒。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合傳給一起趕死線又一起擺爛的戰友，苦中作樂用
variations: 死線不可怕，可怕的是它過了之後，老闆居然還記得。
我跟截止日的關係很單純：它追我，我躲它，誰都別想真的抓到誰。

en.content:
Honestly, I love deadlines.

Especially that soothing little whoosh they make as they fly right past me and vanish into the distance.
en.editorNote: Send to the work buddy you procrastinate with — gallows humor for the days the deadline already lost
variations: Deadlines aren't scary. What's scary is the boss still remembering them after they've passed.
My relationship with deadlines is simple: it chases me, I dodge it, and neither of us ever actually wins.

items.sourceNote: 「我最大的不足，就是餘額不足」梗參考，重新創作金錢與自我主題

zh-tw.content:
面試官問：你覺得自己最大的不足是什麼？

我說：餘額。其他方面我都還算滿意。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合傳給剛領完薪水隔天又見底的朋友，順便提醒他你也一樣
variations: 我這個人最大的缺點，就是太誠實——誠實到帳戶數字騙不了任何人。
別人在存退休金，我在存「撐到月底」的勇氣。

en.content:
Interviewer: what would you say is your biggest shortfall?

Me: my account balance. Everything else I'm pretty happy with.
en.editorNote: Send to the friend whose paycheck evaporates a day after it lands — and quietly admit yours does too
variations: My biggest flaw is being too honest — so honest that my bank balance can't lie to anyone either.
Other people are saving for retirement. I'm saving up the courage to make it to the end of the month.

items.sourceNote: 「I'd agree with you but then we'd both be wrong」梗參考，重新創作辯論諷刺主題

zh-tw.content:
他說：你就不能認同我一次嗎？

我說：可以啊，但那樣我們倆就一起錯了，我不忍心拉你下水。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合在群組裡回那個永遠覺得自己最有道理的朋友，殺傷力強但還算客氣
variations: 我很想站你這邊，可惜這邊已經被事實佔走了。
你說得很有自信，可惜自信跟正確是兩回事。

en.content:
Him: can't you just agree with me for once?

Me: sure, but then we'd both be wrong, and I'd hate to drag you down with me.
en.editorNote: Drop this in the group chat at the friend who's always convinced they're right — sharp but still technically polite
variations: I'd love to take your side, but it's already been claimed by the facts.
You said that with so much confidence. Shame confidence and correctness aren't the same thing.

items.sourceNote: 社畜厭世語錄參考，重新創作上班錢包主題

zh-tw.content:
今天也是充滿希望的一天。

希望快點下班。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合早上九點打卡後傳到同事群組，大家會默默按讚但沒人敢回覆主管看得到的話
variations: 我對人生還是有目標的，目標就是六點準時消失。
每天醒來都覺得未來一片光明，那是因為我還沒打開行事曆。

en.content:
Today is full of hope.

I hope it ends soon.
en.editorNote: Perfect for the work group chat right after clocking in — everyone quietly likes it but nobody replies where the boss can see
variations: I still have goals in life. The main one is disappearing at six on the dot.
Every morning I wake up feeling the future is bright. That's just because I haven't opened my calendar yet.

items.sourceNote: 省電模式哏參考，重新創作能量低落主題

zh-tw.content:
我不是懶，我是開了省電模式。

跟手機一樣，只是我多附贈一份對人生的絕望。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個一直唸你「怎麼又躺著」的家人，幽默自嘲化解尷尬
variations: 我沒有不努力，我只是把效能調到最低來延長壽命。
別催我，背景程式已經很多了，再開一個我會當機。

en.content:
I'm not lazy. I'm in energy-saving mode.

Same as my phone, except mine comes with a complimentary sense of dread.
en.editorNote: Send to the relative who keeps asking why you're lying down again — self-deprecating enough to defuse the lecture
variations: I'm not slacking, I just dimmed my performance to extend my battery life.
Stop nudging me. I've got too many background processes running and one more will crash the whole system.

items.sourceNote: 拖延長大成人哏參考，重新創作成年人逃避主題

zh-tw.content: 所謂長大成人，就是一直跟自己說「等等再處理」，直到那個等等變成永遠。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合貼在自己堆滿待辦事項的桌面截圖旁，誠實到讓人安心
variations: 成年人的時間管理，就是把麻煩的事推到一個叫「以後」的垃圾桶。
我不是不負責任，我只是把責任預約到一個還沒到的日期。

en.content: Being an adult is just telling yourself "I'll deal with it later" until later quietly becomes never.
en.editorNote: Pair it with a screenshot of your overflowing to-do list — honest enough to be weirdly comforting
variations: Adult time management is dragging every annoying task into a folder labeled 'someday.'
I'm not irresponsible. I just scheduled the responsibility for a date that hasn't arrived yet.

items.sourceNote: 錢包像洋蔥哏參考，重新創作財務焦慮主題

zh-tw.content:
我的錢包就像洋蔥。

每次打開都想哭，而且裡面什麼料都沒有。
zh-tw.editorNote: 發薪日前一週的最佳寫照，配上空蕩蕩的存款截圖效果加倍
variations: 我不是不會理財，是根本沒有財可以理。
別人理財是讓錢生錢，我理財是看著錢默默離開。

en.content:
My wallet is like an onion.

Every time I open it I want to cry — and there's nothing useful inside either.
en.editorNote: The perfect mood for the week before payday — hits even harder next to a screenshot of your empty balance
variations: It's not that I'm bad with money. There's just no money to be bad with.
Other people manage money by making it grow. I manage mine by watching it quietly leave.

items.sourceNote: 可以用email解決的會議哏參考，重新創作職場無效會議主題

zh-tw.content:
這場會議開了兩個小時。

結論是：下次用email講就好。而這個結論，本來也可以用email講。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合在被叫去開第三場「對齊一下」的會議後，傳到部門群組裡引發共鳴
variations: 我不討厭開會，我討厭的是開完會才發現什麼都沒決定。
會議室裡最忙的永遠是時鐘。

en.content:
This meeting lasted two hours.

The conclusion: next time just send an email. That conclusion could also have been an email.
en.editorNote: Drop this in the team chat right after getting pulled into your third 'quick sync' of the day
variations: I don't hate meetings. I hate realizing at the end that nothing got decided.
The busiest thing in any meeting room is the clock.

items.sourceNote: 在家工作零通勤哏參考，重新創作遠端工作主題

zh-tw.content:
在家工作最棒的地方，就是通勤距離是零步。

但我還是每天從床走到書桌的途中，emo了一下。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合遠端工作者在週一早上發限動，配上凌亂的居家辦公桌照
variations: 在家上班的意思是：你的床、你的廚房、你的崩潰，全部都在同一個房間。
通勤零分鐘，加班無上限，這就是在家工作的隱藏條款。

en.content:
The best thing about working from home is the commute — zero steps.

And yet I still manage to have a small breakdown somewhere between the bed and the desk.
en.editorNote: Perfect for a Monday-morning story post, paired with a photo of your chaotic home setup
variations: Working from home means your bed, your kitchen, and your meltdown all happen in the same room.
Zero-minute commute, unlimited overtime — that's the fine print of working from home.

items.sourceNote: 繳帳單像送錢單程旅行哏參考，重新創作財務焦慮主題

zh-tw.content:
繳帳單就是幫你的錢買一張單程票。

它出發了，不會回來，連明信片都不寄。
zh-tw.editorNote: 發薪日當天帳單一次扣完時的最佳寫照，配上轉帳成功的截圖更有畫面
variations: 我的錢進來的時候很安靜，走的時候很戲劇化。
薪水只是路過我的戶頭，順便跟我打個招呼而已。

en.content:
Paying bills is just buying your money a one-way ticket.

It leaves, it never comes back, and it doesn't even send a postcard.
en.editorNote: The perfect caption for payday when every bill clears at once — even better next to a transfer-confirmed screenshot
variations: My money arrives quietly and leaves dramatically.
My paycheck just passes through my account to say hi on its way out.

items.sourceNote: 摺床單沒人會哏參考，重新創作成年人都在假裝主題

zh-tw.content:
當大人就像摺那種有鬆緊帶的床單。

沒有人真的會摺，我們只是把它揉成一團，然後假裝那是個成果。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合傳給同樣在硬撐的同齡朋友，互相確認大家都只是裝得很有條理
variations: 所謂的長大，就是把一團混亂折好，看起來像有在控制。
我不是有條理，我只是把亂的地方藏得比較好。

en.content:
Being an adult is like folding a fitted sheet.

Nobody actually knows how — we just ball it up and pretend that counts as done.
en.editorNote: Send to a friend who's also barely holding it together, as a mutual reminder that everyone's just faking the 'organized' part
variations: Growing up is just folding the chaos until it looks like you're in control.
I'm not organized. I've just gotten better at hiding the mess.

items.sourceNote: 月薪是精神賠償金哏參考，重新創作社畜薪水主題

zh-tw.content:
有人問我月薪多少。

我說那不是薪水，那是我每天把脾氣吞下去的精神賠償金。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合在被同事問薪水、或自己看薪資單嘆氣時自嘲使用
variations: 公司付我的不是薪水，是封口費，封住我想離職的那張嘴。
我不是在領薪水，我是在收每天忍住不翻桌的代價。

en.content:
Someone asked how much I make a month.

I said that's not a salary — it's the settlement I get for swallowing my temper every single day.
en.editorNote: Great for some self-deprecating humor when a coworker asks about pay, or when you're sighing at your own payslip
variations: They don't pay me a salary. They pay me hush money to keep my 'I quit' to myself.
It's not a paycheck. It's compensation for not flipping a table every day.

items.sourceNote: 成年人 Google 一切的哏參考，重新創作裝懂主題

zh-tw.content: 所謂長大成人，就是一邊 Google 怎麼做，一邊祈禱沒人發現你其實完全不會。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合報稅季、第一次自己處理瑣事時自嘲，傳給同樣在裝鎮定的朋友
variations: 我不是會處理事情，我只是 Google 速度比較快而已。
成年人的自信，七成靠搜尋引擎撐著。

en.content: Adulting is just Googling how to do things while quietly praying nobody notices you have no idea.
en.editorNote: Perfect for tax season or any 'first time doing this myself' moment — send to a friend who's also faking competence
variations: I'm not capable. I just Google faster than I panic.
Seventy percent of adult confidence is held together by a search engine.

items.sourceNote: 本來可以用 email 解決的會議哏參考，重新創作開會主題

zh-tw.content:
明明一封 email 就能解決的事，硬要開一個小時的會。

這不是溝通，這是集體浪費生命的儀式。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合在收到第三個「快速對齊一下」的會議邀請時，傳到同事群組裡發洩
variations: 會議的存在，是為了證明這封 email 不夠有誠意。
我們不是在開會，我們只是輪流朗讀本來可以已讀的內容。

en.content:
A meeting for something one email could've solved isn't communication.

It's just a group ritual for wasting everyone's afternoon.
en.editorNote: Drop this in the team chat when the third 'quick sync' invite of the day lands
variations: Meetings exist to prove the email wasn't sincere enough.
We're not meeting. We're taking turns reading aloud what could've stayed unread.

items.sourceNote: 薪水安靜進來戲劇化離開的哏參考，重新創作月底金錢主題

zh-tw.content:
薪水進帳的時候靜悄悄，出帳的時候卻像在開演唱會。

我跟我的存款，這個月已經形同陌路。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合月底看帳戶餘額嘆氣，或被朋友揪聚餐想婉拒時使用
variations: 錢進來的時候像路人，走的時候像主角。
我的錢包跟洋蔥一樣，每次打開都讓我想哭。

en.content:
Money walks in quietly and leaves like it's headlining a concert.

My savings and I are basically strangers this month.
en.editorNote: For sighing at your bank balance at month's end, or politely dodging a dinner invite
variations: Money arrives like an extra and exits like the lead role.
My wallet is like an onion — opening it just makes me cry.

items.sourceNote: 我不是懶我只是維持永續能量哏參考，重新創作耍廢主題

zh-tw.content:
我不是懶，我只是在維持一個非常永續的能量水平。

環保的事情，本來就急不得。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合被催「動作快一點」或被說懶散時，優雅地幫自己辯護
variations: 這不叫躺平，這叫節能減碳。
我有在動，只是頻率設定在很環保的那一檔。

en.content:
I'm not lazy. I'm just maintaining a very sustainable level of energy.

You can't rush something this eco-friendly.
en.editorNote: An elegant self-defense for when someone tells you to 'hurry up' or calls you a slacker
variations: It's not lying flat. It's reducing my carbon footprint.
I am moving — just on the most energy-efficient setting available.

items.sourceNote: 在家工作零通勤卻仍心累哏參考，重新創作遠距工作主題

zh-tw.content:
在家工作很棒，通勤只要從床走到桌前。

結果我還是累，因為心累根本不看距離。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合遠距上班族在「明明沒出門卻一樣崩潰」的日子裡自嘲
variations: 通勤零步數，崩潰滿格數。
我的辦公室離床只有三步，但心離快樂有三千公里。

en.content:
Working from home is great — the commute is just bed to desk.

And yet I'm still exhausted, because burnout doesn't care about distance.
en.editorNote: Self-deprecating fuel for remote workers on a 'never left the house but still falling apart' kind of day
variations: Zero steps to commute, full bars of burnout.
My office is three steps from my bed, but happiness is three thousand miles away.

items.sourceNote: 「我太忙著不在乎」哏參考，重新創作對嗆對方廢話的主題

zh-tw.content:
你剛剛是不是說了什麼？

抱歉，我那一秒剛好忙著完全不在乎。
zh-tw.editorNote: 對方在耳邊碎念、想找你吵架時，用最省力的方式表達已讀不回
variations: 你嘴巴有在動，可是我這邊收訊不太到。
再講一次好嗎？我剛剛把注意力調到別人身上了。

en.content:
Wait, did you just say something?

Sorry, I was busy not caring for that one second.
en.editorNote: For when someone's nagging in your ear looking for a fight — the most low-effort way to leave them on read
variations: Your mouth is moving, but my signal over here is pretty weak.
Run that by me again? I had my attention pointed at someone who matters.

items.sourceNote: 「我會同意你，但那樣我們就都錯了」哏參考，重新創作反駁的主題

zh-tw.content:
我是可以勉強同意你啦。

但這樣一來，就變成兩個人一起錯了，太浪費。
zh-tw.editorNote: 對方堅持一個明顯有問題的觀點時，用來優雅地不買單
variations: 要我認同你也行，只是我不太想跟你一起跌進同一個坑。
你說得很有自信，可惜自信跟正確不是同一件事。

en.content:
I could agree with you, I suppose.

But then we'd both be wrong, and that just feels wasteful.
en.editorNote: For when someone clings to an obviously shaky take and you want to decline gracefully
variations: Sure, I could agree — I'd just rather not fall into the same hole as you.
You say it with a lot of confidence. Shame confidence and correctness aren't the same thing.

items.sourceNote: 「那聽起來是你的問題」哏參考，重新創作甩鍋反擊的主題

zh-tw.content:
你說的這些聽起來很嚴重。

好消息是，那聽起來完全是你自己的問題。
zh-tw.editorNote: 對方把情緒或責任硬塞給你時，用來把球溫柔地丟回去
variations: 我懂你很困擾，只是那份困擾它有名字，叫做你的。
這個鍋我看了一下，背帶尺寸只適合你。

en.content:
Wow, that all sounds really serious.

Good news is, it sounds entirely like a you problem.
en.editorNote: For when someone tries to dump their feelings or blame on you — lob the ball gently back
variations: I get that you're upset. That upset just happens to have a name, and it's yours.
I looked at this whole situation, and the strap only fits your shoulders.

items.sourceNote: 「我會羞辱你，但還得解釋給你聽」哏參考，重新創作懶得吵的主題

zh-tw.content:
我本來想酸你一句。

但想到還得解釋你才聽得懂，我就累了，算了。
zh-tw.editorNote: 懶得跟人認真吵架時，用一句話同時表達看不起跟省力
variations: 罵你太花時間了，那個時間我拿去睡覺比較划算。
我準備了一句很狠的，可惜你的理解速度配不上它。

en.content:
I was going to roast you.

Then I remembered I'd have to explain it afterward, and honestly I got tired.
en.editorNote: For when you can't be bothered to actually fight — one line that lands the diss and saves your energy
variations: Insulting you takes too long. I'd rather spend that time napping.
I had a brutal one ready, but your processing speed couldn't keep up with it.

items.sourceNote: 「叫你做自己的人給了你很糟的建議」哏參考，重新創作反諷對方的主題

zh-tw.content:
當初叫你「做自己」的那個人。

說真的，給你的建議有夠糟。
zh-tw.editorNote: 對方理直氣壯地展現討人厭時，用反諷溫柔地戳破
variations: 你很忠於自我，這點我承認，只是這個自我有待商榷。
勇敢做自己是好事，但你這個版本可能需要召回。

en.content:
Whoever told you to 'just be yourself'?

Honestly, that was terrible advice.
en.editorNote: For when someone proudly leans into being insufferable — pop the balloon with a smile
variations: You're very true to yourself, I'll give you that. The self just needs work.
Being authentically you is great, but this particular model might need a recall.

items.sourceNote: 諷刺正向思考的英文語錄參考，重新創作為樂觀有 bug 的主題

zh-tw.content:
我也是樂觀派的。

只是我這個版本目前還在測試階段，已知有不少 bug。
zh-tw.editorNote: 當朋友嫌你太悲觀時丟給他，承認自己樂觀但保留全部吐槽空間
variations: 我不是悲觀，我只是把樂觀調成省電模式。
我對未來很有信心，信心爆棚到我都懷疑系統是不是壞了。

en.content:
I'm an optimist too.

Mine's just stuck in beta, and the known-bug list is pretty long.
en.editorNote: For when someone calls you too gloomy — claim the optimism, keep all the side-eye
variations: I'm not pessimistic. I just keep my optimism on low-power mode.
I have full faith in the future. So much faith I assume something's broken.

items.sourceNote: 成年人崩潰主題英文語錄參考，重新創作為「晚點再說直到死掉」

zh-tw.content:
長大成人的訣竅就是：

所有麻煩都跟它說「晚點再處理」，然後一路晚到你過世為止。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給一起拖延人生大小事的同溫層朋友，用厭世笑點互相取暖
variations: 我的待辦清單就是一張寫滿「等等再說」的卷軸。
成熟不是學會處理問題，是學會跟問題和平共處到天荒地老。

en.content:
The secret to being an adult:

tell every problem 'I'll deal with it later,' then keep saying it until you die.
en.editorNote: Send to your fellow procrastinator — mutual comfort through shared dread
variations: My to-do list is just one long scroll of 'eh, later.'
Maturity isn't solving your problems. It's learning to coexist with them forever.

items.sourceNote: 週一假裝認真工作的英文語錄參考，重新創作為假裝生產力主題

zh-tw.content:
週一是全新的開始。

全新地假裝自己很認真在工作。
zh-tw.editorNote: 週一早上群組打卡用，承認大家都在演但演得很努力
variations: 我週一給工作百分之百的投入，其中百分之九十是投入在裝忙。
週一不是不能有好心情，只是那個好心情要等到星期五才會到貨。

en.content:
Monday is a fresh start.

A fresh start to pretending I'm actually working.
en.editorNote: Perfect for the Monday morning team chat — admit we're all performing, and commit to the bit
variations: I give Monday one hundred percent. Ninety of it goes into looking busy.
Monday can absolutely be a good day. That good mood just ships on Friday.

items.sourceNote: 正向態度惹人厭主題英文語錄參考，重新創作為態度好但沒用

zh-tw.content:
正向態度也許解決不了你的問題。

但絕對能成功惹毛足夠多的人，讓這件事變得很值得。
zh-tw.editorNote: 對方一直跟你說教正能量時，用同樣的邏輯溫柔回敬
variations: 保持微笑不會改變現實，但會讓討厭你的人更困惑，這就夠了。
我的正能量不是用來療癒自己的，是用來氣死對手的。

en.content:
A positive attitude may not fix your problems.

But it'll annoy enough people to make the whole thing worth it.
en.editorNote: Hand it back to the person lecturing you about good vibes — same logic, gentler delivery
variations: Smiling won't change reality, but it confuses the people who hate you. Good enough.
My positivity isn't there to heal me. It's there to irritate the competition.

items.sourceNote: 機智回嗆主題參考，重新創作成自嘲型幹話

zh-tw.content:
我不是在發呆。

我是在等一句完美的回嗆從天上掉下來，結果到現在還沒掉。
zh-tw.editorNote: 被人說「你怎麼都不講話」的時候用，把句點變成笑點
variations: 我不是反應慢，我是在後台幫你想台詞，但我選擇不出聲。
完美的回嘴永遠是在散會三小時後才想到的，這就是人生。

en.content:
I'm not spacing out.

I'm waiting for the perfect comeback to fall from the sky. Still waiting.
en.editorNote: For when someone asks why you've gone quiet — turn the awkward pause into the joke
variations: I'm not slow, I'm just rendering your comeback in the background and choosing not to ship it.
The perfect reply always arrives three hours after the conversation ends. That's just life.

items.sourceNote: 負能量努力主題參考，重新創作成黑色幽默金句

zh-tw.content:
他們說，比你厲害的人都還比你努力。

聽完我就放心了，反正再怎麼努力也追不上，那就好好休息吧。
zh-tw.editorNote: 被人用「別人都很努力」激勵的時候，用更厭世的邏輯化解壓力
variations: 努力會有回報，這句話騙了我好幾年，我決定原諒它，但不再相信它。
我不是不努力，我是把努力留給下輩子，這輩子先躺平觀察一下。

en.content:
They say the people who are better than you are also working harder than you.

Great, now I can relax — I'll never catch up anyway, so I might as well rest.
en.editorNote: Defuse the 'everyone else is hustling harder' guilt trip with cheerful surrender
variations: Hard work pays off — a line that scammed me for years. I forgive it, but I no longer believe it.
I'm not lazy. I'm saving my effort for my next life and observing from the couch in this one.

items.sourceNote: 機智回嗆主題參考，重新創作成關於建議的諷刺句

zh-tw.content:
謝謝你的建議。

我會把它放在我所有不打算採用的好建議旁邊，那邊位子很多。
zh-tw.editorNote: 對方明明沒問就硬要給意見時，用禮貌的方式優雅拒收
variations: 你的意見很寶貴，所以我會好好收藏，永遠不拿出來用。
謝謝指教，我有在聽，只是聽完就還給你了。

en.content:
Thanks for the advice.

I'll file it right next to all the other great advice I have no intention of using. Plenty of room over there.
en.editorNote: For the person who hands out unsolicited opinions — a polite way to decline delivery
variations: Your input is precious, so I'll treasure it and never take it out of the box.
Thanks for the feedback. I heard it, and then I gently handed it back.

items.sourceNote: 職場厭世主題參考，重新創作成上班族幹話

zh-tw.content:
我熱愛我的工作。

尤其是它結束、我可以離開的那一刻，那是我一天最有熱情的時候。
zh-tw.editorNote: 週一早上傳到同事群組，大家會默默按讚但不敢回覆
variations: 我對工作充滿熱情，這份熱情主要展現在我準時打卡下班。
上班最療癒的時刻，就是看到下班時間離我越來越近。

en.content:
I love my job.

Especially the part where it ends and I get to leave. That's the most passionate I get all day.
en.editorNote: Drop it in the work chat on a Monday — everyone quietly likes it, nobody dares reply
variations: I'm full of passion for my work, mostly expressed by clocking out exactly on time.
The most healing moment of the workday is watching the clock crawl toward five.

items.sourceNote: 諷刺幽默主題參考，重新創作成自我認知型幹話

zh-tw.content:
有人問我有沒有什麼缺點。

有，我太誠實了，誠實到剛剛差點把另外幾十個缺點也一起講出來。
zh-tw.editorNote: 面試被問缺點、或朋友互虧時用，先發制人地自爆超有效
variations: 我最大的優點就是謙虛，我的缺點清單長到連我自己都沒看完過。
問我缺點？這個問題太大了，建議分成上下集慢慢來。

en.content:
Someone asked if I had any flaws.

Yeah — I'm too honest. So honest I almost listed the other forty just now.
en.editorNote: For the 'what's your biggest weakness' interview question, or roasting friends — beat them to the punch
variations: My greatest strength is humility. My list of flaws is so long even I haven't finished reading it.
Ask about my flaws? That's a big topic. I'd suggest splitting it into a two-part series.

items.sourceNote: 諷刺金句參考，重新創作成成年人理財型幹話

zh-tw.content:
我不是沒錢，我只是把資產全部重押在外送跟一連串的糟糕決定上。

目前看來，回報很穩定地是零。
zh-tw.editorNote: 月底看戶頭餘額崩潰時自我安慰用，或傳給一起點宵夜的損友
variations: 我的存款跟自尊共用同一個餘額，現在兩個都低到危險邊緣。
理財第一步是記帳，記完帳之後的第二步就是後悔。

en.content:
I'm not broke. I just went all-in on takeout and a long series of bad decisions.

So far the returns have been consistently zero.
en.editorNote: Self-soothing for the end-of-month bank-balance meltdown, or send it to the friend you order late-night snacks with
variations: My savings and my self-esteem share one balance, and right now both are dangerously low.
Step one of budgeting is tracking your spending. Step two is regretting everything you just tracked.

items.sourceNote: 諷刺幽默主題參考，重新創作成成年生活無力感幹話

zh-tw.content:
長大就是一直跟自己說「這個之後再處理」，然後處理到天荒地老都沒處理。

等到真的處理了，通常是因為已經來不及了。
zh-tw.editorNote: 拖延症發作、帳單疊成山的時候傳給同樣在逃避人生的朋友
variations: 成年人的待辦清單只有兩種狀態：還沒開始，跟已經太遲。
我沒有在擺爛，我只是開啟了人生的節能模式。

en.content:
Being an adult is just telling yourself 'I'll deal with that later' until the heat death of the universe.

And when you finally deal with it, it's usually because it's already too late.
en.editorNote: For when procrastination hits and the bills are stacking up — send it to a friend who's also dodging adulthood
variations: An adult's to-do list has exactly two states: haven't started, and already too late.
I'm not slacking off. I just switched my life into energy-saving mode.

items.sourceNote: 社畜厭世語錄參考，重新創作成上班族通勤型幹話

zh-tw.content:
我的青春沒有揮霍在戀愛上，全都耗在通勤的路上了。

捷運看著我長大，公車看著我變老。
zh-tw.editorNote: 塞在尖峰時段的車陣裡，懷疑人生的時候傳到群組剛剛好
variations: 我不是討厭工作，我只是討厭起床、出門、上班、還有醒著的每一分鐘。
上班最療癒的時刻，就是看著時鐘一秒一秒爬向下班。

en.content:
I didn't waste my youth on romance. I spent every last drop of it on the commute.

The subway watched me grow up. The bus watched me grow old.
en.editorNote: Perfect to drop in the group chat while stuck in rush-hour traffic, questioning all your life choices
variations: I don't hate my job. I just hate waking up, leaving the house, working, and every waking minute in between.
The most healing part of the workday is watching the clock crawl, second by second, toward quitting time.

items.sourceNote: 諷刺迷因金句參考，重新創作成失眠後悔型幹話

zh-tw.content:
我不用設鬧鐘，凌晨三點的後悔會準時把我叫醒。

而且它從來沒有遲到過，比我老闆還敬業。
zh-tw.editorNote: 半夜睡不著翻來覆去、滑手機到天亮時傳給同樣失眠的夜貓子
variations: 別人數羊助眠，我數的是這輩子說過的每一句蠢話。
我的睡眠品質跟我的人生規劃一樣，一片混亂又毫無進展。

en.content:
I don't need an alarm clock. My 3 AM regrets wake me up right on schedule.

They've never once been late — more dedicated than my boss, honestly.
en.editorNote: For the wide-awake-at-night doomscroll — send it to a fellow night owl who can't sleep either
variations: Other people count sheep to fall asleep. I count every dumb thing I've ever said.
My sleep quality is a lot like my life plan: total chaos and going absolutely nowhere.

items.sourceNote: 搞笑金錢金句參考，重新創作成發薪日秒空型幹話

zh-tw.content:
發薪日是我一個月裡最快樂的三秒鐘。

第四秒，房租、卡費和保險就排隊來把它領走了。
zh-tw.editorNote: 每個月薪水入帳隔天就見底時，傳到群組裡跟同事一起哭笑
variations: 我的薪水有兩種狀態：還沒入帳，跟已經是別人的。
別人說錢要花在刀口上，我的錢還沒看到刀口就先飛了。

en.content:
Payday is the happiest three seconds of my entire month.

By second four, rent, credit cards, and insurance have already lined up to take it away.
en.editorNote: Send to the work group chat the day after payday, when your balance is already gone
variations: My paycheck has two states: not deposited yet, and already someone else's.
People say spend money where it counts. Mine flies off before I even find where that is.

items.sourceNote: 成年人幽默金句參考，重新創作成假裝會當大人主題

zh-tw.content: 所謂當大人，就是一邊上網查「這個到底怎麼用」，一邊祈禱沒人發現你其實什麼都不會。
zh-tw.editorNote: 報稅季或第一次自己處理水電瓦斯帳單時，傳給同樣在裝鎮定的朋友
variations: 長大後我才知道，大人也是一群不知道在幹嘛、只是比較會演的小孩。
我現在唯一精通的成人技能，是面無表情地說「沒事我可以處理」。

en.content: Being an adult is just Googling 'how does this work' while praying nobody notices you have no clue what you're doing.
en.editorNote: For tax season or the first time you handle your own utility bills — send to a friend who's also faking it
variations: Growing up taught me that adults are just kids who got better at pretending they know what's going on.
The only adult skill I've truly mastered is saying 'it's fine, I've got this' with a completely straight face.

items.sourceNote: 社畜厭世語錄參考，重新創作成下班倒數型幹話

zh-tw.content:
我不是討厭工作，我只是討厭在不想去的地方，做不想做的事，還要假裝很開心。

除此之外，這份工作其實很完美。
zh-tw.editorNote: 週一早上鬧鐘響起、靈魂還沒到公司時，傳給同樣在床上掙扎的同事
variations: 上班最大的成就感，來自於成功撐到了下班。
我對這份工作沒有意見，我只是對「要去上班」這件事有很多意見。

en.content:
I don't hate my job. I just hate going somewhere I don't want to go, doing things I don't want to do, and pretending I'm thrilled about it.

Other than that, honestly, perfect gig.
en.editorNote: For Monday morning when the alarm goes off and your soul hasn't clocked in yet — send to a coworker still in bed
variations: My biggest sense of accomplishment at work is successfully surviving until quitting time.
I have no problem with the job itself. I just have a lot of problems with the part where I have to show up.

items.sourceNote: 諷刺幽默金句參考，重新創作成壓力疊加型幹話

zh-tw.content:
如果煩惱可以換錢，我大概早就財務自由了。

可惜這世界只收錢，不收煩惱，不然我願意整批出清。
zh-tw.editorNote: 壓力大到想笑出來的那種日子，傳給懂你苦中作樂的朋友
variations: 我這輩子最不缺的就是煩惱，偏偏它一毛錢都不值。
別人收集點數換好禮，我收集壓力換失眠。

en.content:
If worrying paid money, I'd have hit financial freedom years ago.

Sadly the world only takes cash, not anxiety — otherwise I'd happily sell the whole stockpile.
en.editorNote: For the kind of stressful day where you can only laugh — send to a friend who gets gallows humor
variations: The one thing I've never run short on is worries, and they're not worth a single cent.
Some people collect loyalty points for rewards. I collect stress and redeem it for insomnia.

items.sourceNote: 搞笑職場語錄參考，重新創作「截止日」主題

zh-tw.content:
別人是被夢想追著跑。

我是被截止日追著跑，而且它討債的姿態比誰都認真。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給每天被deadline壓到喘不過氣的同事，苦中作樂一下
variations: 截止日從來不會遲到，遲到的永遠是我的進度。
我跟截止日的關係很穩定——它一直追，我一直逃。

en.content:
Some people are chased by their dreams.

I'm chased by deadlines — and they collect debts more aggressively than anyone I've ever met.
en.editorNote: Send to the coworker who's perpetually one deadline away from a breakdown — shared misery, lightly seasoned
variations: Deadlines are never late. The only thing running behind is my progress.
My deadlines and I have a very stable relationship: they keep chasing, I keep running.

items.sourceNote: 搞笑金錢語錄參考，重新創作「錢的單戀」主題

zh-tw.content:
我跟錢的關係，是標準的單戀。

我很愛它，它卻只想離開我，連個回頭都沒有。
zh-tw.editorNote: 發薪日隔天錢就不見的時候，傳給一樣月光的朋友
variations: 錢進到我戶頭只是路過，從來沒打算定居。
別人說要學會放手，我的錢倒是教得很好。

en.content:
My relationship with money is textbook unrequited love.

I adore it. It just wants to leave me — without so much as a goodbye glance.
en.editorNote: For the day after payday when the balance has already vanished — send to a fellow paycheck-to-paycheck survivor
variations: Money only passes through my account. It never had any intention of settling down.
People say I should learn to let go. My money taught me that lesson beautifully.

items.sourceNote: 諷刺職場語錄參考，重新創作「節能模式」主題

zh-tw.content:
我不是懶，我只是長期開著節能模式。

為公司省電，這份心意主管應該要懂。
zh-tw.editorNote: 被說「最近怎麼這麼沒幹勁」的時候，拿來幫自己找個體面的台階
variations: 我這不叫擺爛，這叫永續經營，把力氣留給更需要的明天。
全速運轉很傷機器，所以我選擇用最低耗能完成今天。

en.content:
I'm not lazy. I've simply been running on energy-saving mode for an extended period.

It cuts the company's power bill — management should really appreciate the gesture.
en.editorNote: A dignified excuse for when someone asks why you've seemed so low on drive lately
variations: This isn't slacking, it's sustainability — saving my strength for a tomorrow that needs it more.
Running at full throttle wears out the machine, so I'm completing today at minimum power draw.

items.sourceNote: 負能量厭世語錄參考，重新創作「餘額決定心情」主題

zh-tw.content:
今天心情好不好，不用問我。

打開戶頭看一眼餘額，答案它自己會告訴你。
zh-tw.editorNote: 月底餘額見底時，傳給那個問你「最近還好嗎」的朋友最精準
variations: 我的情緒很單純，跟著餘額一起上上下下，主要是下。
別人靠星座算運勢，我靠存款數字算心情。

en.content:
Want to know if I'm in a good mood today? Don't bother asking me.

Just peek at my bank balance — it'll give you the answer all by itself.
en.editorNote: The most accurate reply to a friend asking 'how have you been?' at the end of the month
variations: My emotions are simple: they rise and fall with my balance. Mostly fall.
Some people read horoscopes for their fortune. I read my account balance for my mood.

items.sourceNote: 不想上班厭世語錄參考，重新創作「理想就是不上班」主題

zh-tw.content:
別跟我談理想，談理想太傷感情。

我的理想很單純，就是不用上班，這個理想還滿遠大的。
zh-tw.editorNote: 面試被問「你對未來有什麼規劃」時不能講，但可以傳給同事一起笑
variations: 我上班的目的只有一個，就是早日達成不用上班的目標。
問我熱情在哪？在我下班之後，公司管不到的那段時間。

en.content:
Please don't ask me about my dreams — it gets emotional.

My dream is very simple: not having to work. Admittedly, it's quite an ambitious one.
en.editorNote: Can't say this in a job interview when they ask about your five-year plan, but perfect for the group chat with coworkers
variations: I have exactly one career goal: to reach the point where I no longer need a career.
You want to know where my passion lives? After clock-out, in the hours the company can't touch.

items.sourceNote: 社畜厭世語錄參考，重新創作「能者過勞」主題

zh-tw.content:
主管說「能者多勞」的時候，聽起來很像在誇你。

等你累到不成人形、薪水卻原地踏步，才發現那叫「能者過勞」。
zh-tw.editorNote: 被塞一堆額外工作又沒加薪時，拿來精準翻譯主管的場面話
variations: 他們欣賞你的能力，方式是讓你一個人做三個人的事。
「這個交給你我最放心」的真正意思是「這個我不想做」。

en.content:
When your manager says 'the capable carry the load,' it sounds like a compliment.

Then you're worked into the ground with a salary that hasn't moved an inch, and you realize it actually meant 'the capable get burned out.'
en.editorNote: The precise translation of corporate flattery for when you're handed extra work and zero extra pay
variations: They admire your skills — by having one person do the work of three.
'I trust you with this one' is just a polished way of saying 'I don't want to do this one.'

items.sourceNote: 諷刺職場語錄參考，重新創作「會議與郵件」主題

zh-tw.content:
開了一個小時的會，結論是「我們再約時間討論」。

會議室，一個讓生產力安心睡午覺的地方。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個又被叫去開無意義會議的同事，附上一杯咖啡更佳
variations: 這場會議只要一封信就能解決，但他們選擇困住我一小時。
我不是不想開會，我只是覺得那一小時可以拿來真的把事做完。

en.content:
Sat through a one-hour meeting. The conclusion was: 'let's schedule another time to discuss this.'

The conference room — where productivity goes for a peaceful little nap.
en.editorNote: Send to the coworker who just got dragged into yet another pointless meeting; bonus points if you attach a coffee
variations: This meeting could have been one email, but they chose to trap me for an hour instead.
It's not that I hate meetings. I just think that hour could've gone toward actually finishing the work.

items.sourceNote: 成年人厭世語錄參考，重新創作「咖啡因續命」主題

zh-tw.content:
我不是早上脾氣差，我只是還沒進入「靠咖啡因勉強算個人」的階段。

中午之前，請把我當成一台還在開機的機器。
zh-tw.editorNote: 貼在辦公桌前或傳到群組，提醒大家上午十點前別來找你
variations: 我靠咖啡因、諷刺和沒處理完的情緒在運作，缺一不可。
別期待我中午前是個正常人，系統還在跑開機動畫。

en.content:
I'm not bad-tempered in the mornings. I just haven't reached the 'barely a person thanks to caffeine' stage yet.

Before noon, please treat me as a machine that's still booting up.
en.editorNote: Stick it on your desk or drop it in the group chat to warn everyone not to approach before 10 a.m.
variations: I run on caffeine, sarcasm, and a few unresolved feelings — remove any one and the whole thing crashes.
Don't expect a functional human before noon; the system is still loading the startup animation.

items.sourceNote: 職場諷刺語錄參考，重新創作「deadline 呼嘯而過」主題

zh-tw.content:
我最喜歡 deadline 了。

尤其是它從我旁邊呼嘯而過的那一聲，特別療癒。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個又在跟你哭訴專案爆掉的同事，附一句「沒事，下個 deadline 也會過去的」
variations: 我跟 deadline 的關係很穩定，它從來不會等我，我也從來不會準時。
別問我截止日是什麼時候，問就是「已經過了」。

en.content:
I love deadlines.

Especially the soothing little whoosh they make as they fly right past me.
en.editorNote: Send to the coworker who's panicking over a blown project, with a 'don't worry, the next deadline will pass too'
variations: My relationship with deadlines is very stable: they never wait for me, and I'm never on time.
Don't ask me when it's due. The answer is always 'already past it.'

items.sourceNote: 成年人厭世語錄參考，重新創作「假裝會折床單」主題

zh-tw.content:
當大人就像折一條鋪過床的床單。

沒有人真的會折，我們只是把它揉成一團，然後假裝那叫整齊。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個剛搬出來自己住、正在懷疑人生的朋友，告訴他大家都是這樣矇混過去的
variations: 成年的真相就是：每個人都在偷偷 Google 怎麼當大人，然後祈禱別被發現。
我把床單揉成一坨塞進櫃子，這就是我對「整理」最大的誠意了。

en.content:
Being an adult is like folding a fitted sheet.

Nobody actually knows how. We just wad it into a ball and pretend that counts as neat.
en.editorNote: Send to the friend who just moved out on their own and is quietly questioning everything — reassure them we're all winging it
variations: The truth about adulthood: everyone is secretly Googling how to be an adult and praying nobody notices.
I scrunched the sheet into a lump and shoved it in the closet. That's the most effort 'organizing' is getting today.

items.sourceNote: 上班族厭世語錄參考，重新創作「薪水是精神賠償金」主題

zh-tw.content:
我每個月領的不是薪水。

那是公司為了我這個月受的精神損害，付給我的賠償金。
zh-tw.editorNote: 發薪日傳到部門群組，保證一群人默默按讚不敢明講
variations: 薪水進帳那天最開心，因為那是它離我最近的一刻。
與其說是月薪，不如說是「忍住沒離職」的安慰獎。

en.content:
What I get every month isn't a salary.

It's the compensation the company pays me for the emotional damages I sustained that month.
en.editorNote: Drop it in the team chat on payday — watch everyone quietly like it without daring to comment
variations: Payday is the happiest moment of the month, because that's the closest my paycheck ever gets to me.
It's less of a monthly salary and more of a consolation prize for not quitting.

items.sourceNote: 諷刺人生語錄參考，重新創作「晚上11點算狂歡」主題

zh-tw.content: 我現在到了一個年紀，所謂「玩很瘋的一晚」，指的是撐到晚上十一點還沒睡。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那群以前夜衝、現在十點就喊累的老朋友，配一張被自己嚇到的貼圖
variations: 年輕時熬夜是玩樂，現在熬夜是身體出了什麼問題。
我的叛逆期就是週五晚上決定「今天不洗碗」，然後一路罪惡到天亮。

en.content: I've reached the age where a 'wild night out' means staying up past 11 p.m.
en.editorNote: Send to the old crew who used to pull all-nighters and now yawn by ten — pair it with a self-shocked sticker
variations: Staying up late used to mean I was having fun. Now it means something is medically wrong.
My rebellious phase is deciding on a Friday night that the dishes can wait — then feeling guilty about it till dawn.
